The method determines traffic phases with reward signals derived from traffic pressure and queue length, and states consisting of queue lengths and vehicle flow rates. Simulations were conducted using a replica of Surabaya's road network in PTV VISSIM and Simulation of Urban Mobility. To enhance robustness, the Robust Adversarial Loss DQN (RADIAL-DQN) is introduced, which was tested under both normal and adversarial conditions, including Projected Gradient Descent attacks. The results depicted using Macroscopic Fundamental Diagram show that cycle-based control is 10% more effective in managing signals, while non-cycle-based control offers up to 40% greater robustness against disruptions in terms of throughput.

title: Model-Based Multi-Modal Volumetric Sensing via Fusion of Stereo HFR, ToF, and Thermal Cameras
authors: Tegar Palyus Fiqar, Feiyue Wang, Kohei Shimasaki, Idaku Ishii
abstract: Multi-modal, non-contact sensing systems have become increasingly critical in diverse fields such as biomedical diagnostics, structural health monitoring, and manufacturing. These applications often require synchronized measurement of temperature, vibration, displacement, and surface appearance. Conventional methods that rely on separate sensors for each modality face challenges in spatial alignment, temporal synchronization, and physical integration, particularly when applied to non-rigid or moving targets. In this paper, we present a modelbased volumetric capture system capable of simultaneous threedimensional (3D) visualization of thermal and vibrational characteristics. The system integrates stereo high-frame-rate cameras at hundreds of frames per second (fps), a time-of-flight depth camera, and a thermal infrared camera operating at several dozens of fps. The system employs a unified 3D model as a spatial reference to which multi-modal data are registered. A model-based 3D digital image correlation (3D-DIC) algorithm enables the estimation of small displacements and vibrations in the range of several tens to hundreds of hertz. To demonstrate the effectiveness of our system, thermal mapping is performed on the same 3D model, enabling visualization of the spatial distribution and temporal evolution of both vibration and temperature during the heating and boiling process of an electric kettle.

title: Fractional-N PLL–Based Micro-Oven Control for Ultra-Stable MEMS Frequency References
authors: Karim Elmeligy, Rakibul Islam, Jintark Kim, Mohamed Badr Younis, Jie Yan, Jiawei Yang, Gabrielle Vukasin, Hyun-Keun Kwon, Thomas W. Kenny, Gaurav Bahl, Pavan Kumar Hanumolu
abstract: This paper presents a fully integrated micro-oven temperature compensation method for dual-mode MEMS oscillators using a fractional-N PLL. By locking two temperature-dependent modes and driving the micro-heater with an amplitude-modulated signal, the system achieves a 2 ppb/°C TCF and sub-ppb Allan deviation while remaining fully CMOS compatible. A 65-nm prototype shows a three-order-of-magnitude improvement over previous work and offers a general framework for oscillator stabilization using temperature information encoded in frequency.

title: Design and Operation Analysis of Phase Change Energy Storage and Multi-Energy Coupled Constant-Temperature Biogas System
authors: Junyi Gong, Zhimei Wen, Xishan Sun, Cheng Tian
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: A design of phase change energy storage and multi-energy coupling constant-temperature biogas system was carried out, and an experimental platform was established. The results showed that under the environmental temperature fluctuation range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-5^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> to 25°C, this system could stably control the fermentation temperature at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$35 \pm 2^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, with a temperature fluctuation coefficient of only 2.5%; compared with the traditional system, the daily gas production increased by 75%, and the methane volume fraction was stably maintained at 60% to 65%; the comprehensive energy utilization efficiency of the system reached 89.4%, and the energy recovery rate of the phase change energy storage unit was 78.5%.

title: Classifying Snoring Sounds for Acoustic Detection of Sleep Apnea
authors: Mohammad Al-Hattab, Hussein Ayman Odeh
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Obstructive Sleep Apnea (OSA) is a very common sleep disorder that poses serious issues for human health and the quality of life. Despite its wide dispersal, it is still largely underdiagnosed due to several reasons, varying from social annoyance to the inconvenience associated with traditional polysomnography (PSG). This paper proposes and examines the development of an AI acoustic-based system that detects at real-time the presence of OSA by accurately distinguishing snoring sounds and other nocturnal sounds and any surrounding noise. The paper investigates the mechanisms behind snoring and classifies different types of snoring to identify normal snoring and distinguish it from snoring associated with OSA. The proposed model performs feature extraction and the application of machine learning models.

title: A Multi-Agent System for Automated Lifetime Prediction of Power Electronics
authors: Jialong Liang, Jiajie Fan, Guoqi Zhang, Willem D. Van Driel
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: Accurate lifetime prediction of power semiconductor devices is critical for the reliability of power electronic systems. Power cycling tests generate only a small number of failure samples due to their high cost and long duration. This data scarcity makes it difficult to train reliable prediction models. At the same time, modern deep learning models require significant AI expertise to configure and tune, which creates a barrier for reliability engineers. To address these two problems, this paper proposes a domain-constrained multi-agent framework for automated remaining useful life (RUL) prediction of power semiconductor devices. The framework integrates three collaborative agents: a Stats Agent for statistical reliability assessment, a Physics Agent for physical plausibility validation, and a Model Agent for algorithm selection and hyperparameter tuning. A Central Controller manages the workflow and coordinates agent communication. The agents interact through a Propose-Critique-Refine (PCR) mechanism, in which the Model Agent proposes configurations and the other two agents critique them from statistical and physical perspectives. This iterative negotiation eliminates the need for manual parameter tuning. The framework is validated on the NASA IGBT accelerated aging dataset under Leave-One-Device-Out Cross-Validation. Results show that the framework achieves an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.914 at a 50% observation ratio and remains above 0.79 even at 30%, demonstrating strong generalization under limited data conditions.

title: Research on the Structured Reconstruction and Precise Extraction of Key Information from Power Demand Texts Based on Large Language Models
authors: Huijun Hong, Yiru Yang, Zhengcheng Min, Qiyang Ruan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: The power customer service system generates large amounts of unstructured text in fault reporting and service feedback, containing key information such as user addresses, feeder names, restoration times, and fault causes. Efficiently extracting and structurally reconstructing this information helps improve fault localization efficiency and enhance service intelligence. This paper proposes a structured approach for power demand text based on Large Language Models (LLMs), constructing a processing workflow that includes data preprocessing, prompt engineering, information extraction, and structural reconstruction. To improve extraction accuracy, a dual-stage prompt template is designed, and a function call mechanism is introduced to ensure that the output is structurally standardized and the content is complete. The experiment is conducted based on real grid work order data and compared with rule-based methods and the BERT-CRF model. The results show that LLMs outperform traditional models in multi-field extraction tasks, demonstrating stronger semantic understanding and contextual modeling capabilities, and verifying their effectiveness and generalizability in power business scenarios.

title: Research on the Prediction Method of Recent Photovoltaic Power Generation Based on CNN BiGRU
authors: Yang Guo, Guili Ding, Xin Han, Weili Zhong
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: In response to the fluctuation and intermittency of output caused by large-scale grid connection of photovoltaic power plants, high-precision daily power prediction is crucial for ensuring system power balance and frequency stability. Therefore, this article proposes a CNN-BiGRU hybrid prediction model based on fuzzy C-means (FCM) clustering. This method first integrates historical photovoltaic power, numerical weather forecast (NWP) meteorological data, and four types of weather scene labels automatically divided by FCM to construct a multisource input feature set; Secondly, local spatiotemporal features are extracted through CNN and then input into BiGRU to mine bidirectional temporal dependencies, achieving end-to-end accurate modeling. Finally, experiments based on one-year measured data from a certain photovoltaic power station showed that the introduction of FCM clustering significantly outperformed benchmark models such as CNN and BiGRU in different seasonal scenarios in August and November. Among them, MSE decreased by 23.63%, MAPE decreased by 37.27%, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> increased by 0.012. The results verified the high accuracy and strong robustness of the proposed framework under complex meteorological conditions, which can provide effective decision support for power grid scheduling under high proportion photovoltaic grid connection.

title: Distributed Smart Agriculture Monitoring over the IoT Computing Continuum
authors: Carlos Kamienski, Ivan Zyrianoff, Lei Xue, Marco Di Felice
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Smart agriculture applications utilizing IoT sensors and actuators operate across a geographical and computing continuum, encompassing diverse and resource-constrained devices at the edge and more powerful infrastructure closer to the cloud. However, these applications are typically not designed to be aware of the continuum’s heterogeneity, leading to challenges in efficiency and reliability. We introduce DATUM (Distributed Applications for the IoTinuum), a framework for developing and deploying continuum-aware smart agriculture applications. We qualitatively evaluate DATUM with a smart irrigation use case, demonstrating its potential to address common challenges and leverage the strengths of the IoTinuum. Also, we introduce a system model and a problem formulation to minimize the latency, which can be used to conduct a performance analysis study.

title: Traceability and Research in the Agricultural Chain of Medicinal and Aromatic Plants (MAPs): An IoT-based Approach
authors: Ivan Zyrianoff, Andrea Iannoli, Federico Montori, Luca Sciullo, Luciano Bononi, Alice Baldissara, Beatrice Brintazzoli, Enrico Dall’Olio, Mattia Alpi, Rocco Enrico Sferrazza, Giovanni Dinelli, Ilaria Marotti, Marco Di Felice
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: In precision agriculture, the Internet of Things (IoT) provides essential technologies for collecting and processing real-time data from the field. While numerous deployments have been reported, the unique conditions and requirements of agricultural environments often demand customized IoT solutions. In this paper, we present an innovative use case from the TRACE project, focused on the value chain of Medicinal and Aromatic Plants (MAPS) in Italy. We describe a comprehensive framework designed to enable precision agriculture in this domain, encompassing IoT data collection, farm data integration, and supply chain certification. The TRACE deployment includes a medium-scale LoRaWAN network with nearly 30 IoT devices, comprising Weather Stations (WS) and Soil Moisture Sensors (SMS). Our measurements demonstrate that a single LoRa link can achieve a range exceeding 6.5 km. However, our findings also indicate that RSSI values do not consistently correlate with Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR) performance. Additionally, weather conditions like low temperature appear to slightly influence RSSI values, particularly depending on the exposure of the IoT devices.

title: OmniPlantSeg: Species Agnostic 3D Point Cloud Organ Segmentation for High-Resolution Plant Phenotyping Across Modalities
authors: Andreas Gilson, Lukas Meyer, Oliver Scholz, Ute Schmid
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Accurate point cloud segmentation for plant organs is crucial for 3D plant phenotyping. Existing solutions are designed problem-specific with a focus on certain plant species or specified sensor-modalities for data acquisition. Furthermore, it is common to use extensive pre-processing and down-sample the plant point clouds to meet hardware or neural network input size requirements. We propose a simple, yet effective algorithm KD-SS for sub-sampling of biological point clouds that is agnostic to sensor data and plant species. The main benefit of this approach is that we do not need to down-sample our input data and thus, enable segmentation of the full-resolution point cloud. Combining KD-SS with current state-of-the-art segmentation models shows satisfying results evaluated on different modalities such as photogrammetry, laser triangulation and LiDAR for various plant species. We propose KD-SS as lightweight resolution-retaining alternative to intensive pre-processing and down-sampling methods for plant organ segmentation regardless of used species and sensor modality.

title: 2025 IEEE Latin American Conference on Nanotechnology (LANANO)
authors: N/A

title: Grapevine Leaf Phenotyping Using Deep Learning Based on Three-Dimensional Point Cloud
authors: Dimitrios-Tilemachos Davarakis, Yuqiao Ren, Nikolaos Tsoulias, Marco Bignardi, Dimitrios S. Paraforos, Dimitrios Argyropoulos
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: High-quality phenotyping of grapevine leaves is critical for monitoring plant health, growth, and yield. Traditional manual measurement methods are time-consuming, destructive, and labor-intensive. This study investigates the application of PointNet, a 3D point-based deep learning model, for predicting grapevine leaf traits—area, width, and length—directly from raw point cloud data. A dataset of 930 colorized 3D point clouds was generated from RGB-D images under controlled laboratory conditions. The data were preprocessed with filtering, normalization, imputation, down sampling, and augmentation before training. PointNet was adapted for regression by replacing its classification layers with a multilayer regression architecture. Comparative experiments with traditional machine learning models—KNN, DTR, RFR, and SVR—trained on geometric and statistical features showed that the PointNet regressor achieved superior overall performance, with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values up to 0.99 and consistently low RMSE and MAPE, particularly for leaf width (0.25 and 0.03 respectively) and length (0.5 and 0.05 respectively). While RFR outperformed PointNet in leaf area RMSE, the deep learning approach demonstrated stronger generalization and scalability. Results highlight the potential of 3D point-based deep learning for automated, non-destructive grapevine phenotyping. Future work will expand field datasets and explore alternative 3D models for improved trait prediction and automated organ segmentation.

title: A Novel Current Protection Setting Method with Moderate Sacrifice of Selectivity
authors: Shiqiang Dai, Dongran Liu, Yudong Fang, Ningkai Chen, Yuantao Wang, Wei Ma, Haiyan Chen
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: The structure and operation mode of active distribution networks are complex, and the overall performance of current protection is low. When there is a contradiction between selectivity and sensitivity in the cooperation of line protection section II and adjacent line protection section I, the existing setting method is to sacrifice the rapidity to harmonize the contradiction, which will lead to a long fault removal time on multilevel lines, and will also cause distributed new energy sources to go off the grid. The new setting method is proposed to sacrifice selectivity appropriately, and the risk of load loss is taken as the evaluation index of the methods. The risk of load loss calculation and the comparison of the existing method and the new method are given. Finally, the loss-of-load risk of the existing method and the new method is compared with an example, which shows that the new method can improve the comprehensive performance of current protection.

title: Beyond Productivity: Exploring the Cognitive Cost of Using ChatGPT
authors: Shorouq F. Eletter, Ghaleb A. Elrefae, Abdullah Elrefae, Hashem Aliter
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Generative AI is a category of artificial intelligence that focuses on generating new content and data. The rapid integration of AI tools, including ChatGPT and other large language models LLMs offers many promises in terms of productivity and efficiency. Currently, these tools are employed as personal intellectual and managerial assistants at both the individual and professional levels. This is due to their ability to provide unprecedented access to information at speed and creativity, which enhances our short-term productivity and performance in various tasks. On the other hand, concerns emerged about potential cognitive consequences. This study aims to investigate the impact of excessive AI use on attention, critical thinking, reasoning, and memory retention. The findings revealed that excessive reliance on AI increases the extrinsic load. This strains attention and decreases deep involvement. There is also an accompanying risk of reduced internal memory retention, often referred to as digital amnesia. Additionally, it might hinder synthesis, analysis, and critical thinking skills. This will affect the well-being of the individual and society. Also, relying on it too heavily may discourage individuals from engaging in deep thinking, independent reasoning, and verifying the quality of sources and information. This study suggests a novel model, “the Cognitive Engagement Spectrum” framework. The framework presents three types of users based on their level of interaction and engagement with AI tools. The passive users, active editing users, and generative prompting and socratic dialogue users. Additionally, experts should keep in mind that the performance of AI models improves when they interact with users. Therefore, there should be a warrant for their future capabilities. Ultimately, we need to reach a point where we control the tool, not the other way around.

title: Application Research of an Efficient Blockchain Consensus Mechanism Integrating Clustering Algorithms and Zero-Knowledge Proofs in Distributed Power Trading
authors: Mingyue Xie
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: Facing two challenges in distributed power trade, that is, blockchain throughput bottleneck problem and transaction privacy leakage problem, this paper designs an efficient consensus mechanism based on dynamic clustering and zero knowledge proof. Specifically, the network is firstly divided into multiple consensus groups in parallel by multi-dimensional feature dynamic clustering algorithm, which improves the communication topology; then, zero knowledge proofs are applied into consensus process, and groups can generate globally verified proofs for all transactions in certain period without leaking any information; finally, a hierarchical hybrid consensus architecture is designed to achieve fast local sorting and efficient global confirmation. The experimental results show that the mechanism can achieve 2150 TPS with 300 nodes, and the success rate of privacy attack is lower than 10 %. Meanwhile, the mechanism still maintains high stability under dynamic perturbation. The research proves that the mechanism can effectively solve the core requirements of efficiency and privacy in distributed energy trade.

title: AI-Powered Optimization and Reusability Prediction of Visible-Light Photocatalyst for Methylene Blue Degradation
authors: Youvaraja Sundararaman, Gurulakshmi Mariappan, Gopirajan Punnniyakotti Varadharajan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The use of mathematical prediction models in waste management has increased recently in order to understand the behavior of organic pollutants during degradation. As there is a tremendous increase in waste production due to human activities, these models have become very important for improving the efficiency of waste management, particularly in wastewater treatment. Recently, the use of machine learning techniques has further improved these predictive models, helping in better management of waste and reducing the harmful effects of toxic industrial waste on the global water supply. Visible-light photocatalysis is regarded as a promising advanced oxidation process that can be used for industrial applications. It utilizes Titania (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{TiO}_{2}$</tex>) based photocatalysts to break down various organic pollutants such as methylene blue (MB) and other dyes. This paper presents a proposal of an artificial intelligence (AI) based framework to maximize the synthesis and performance of titaniabased nanocomposites <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{V}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5} / \text{TiO}_{2}, \mathrm{V}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5} / \mathrm{N}-\text{TiO}_{2}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5} / \mathrm{S} \text{TiO}_{2}$</tex>) and predict their reusability towards MB. The Random Forest Regression (RFR) was used to determine the optimal dopant concentrations to maximize MB degradation and a nonlinear regression using pseudo-first-order kinetics to interpret the reaction pathways and the stability are performed. A Support Vector Machine (SVM) discriminator was used to forecast catalyst lifecycle with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5+$</tex> cycles being predicted. The best <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5} / \mathrm{S}-\text{TiO}_{2}$</tex> catalyst had optimized degradation efficiency of 96 and the rate constant of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.034 \min^{-1}$</tex>. The directed AI methodology leads to lower experimentation, higher predictability and offers a way of efficiently designing reusable photocatalysts in a rational manner. This approach proves scalable when applying real-life exercise on wastewater treatment.

title: Region-Adaptive Learned Hierarchical Encoding for 3D Gaussian Splatting Data
authors: Shashank N. Sridhara, Birendra Kathariya, Fangjun Pu, Peng Yin, Eduardo Pavez, Antonio Ortega
abstract: We introduce Region-Adaptive Learned Hierarchical Encoding (RALHE) for 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) data. While 3DGS has recently become popular for novel view synthesis, the size of trained models limits its deployment in bandwidth-constrained applications such as volumetric media streaming. To address this, we propose a learned hierarchical latent representation that builds upon the principles of “overfitted” learned image compression (e.g., Cool-Chic and C3) to efficiently encode 3DGS attributes. Unlike images, 3DGS data have irregular spatial distributions of Gaussians (geometry) and consist of multiple attributes (signals) defined on the irregular geometry. Our codec is designed to account for these differences between images and 3DGS. Specifically, we leverage the octree structure of the voxelized 3DGS geometry to obtain a hierarchical multi-resolution representation. Our approach overfits latents to each Gaussian attribute under a global rate constraint. These latents are decoded independently through a lightweight decoder network. To estimate the bitrate during training, we employ an autoregressive probability model that leverages octree-derived contexts from the 3D point structure. The multi-resolution latents, decoder, and autoregressive entropy coding networks are jointly optimized for each Gaussian attribute. Experiments on 3DGS models from the Synthetic-NeRF dataset demonstrate that the proposed RALHE compression framework achieves a rendering PSNR gain of up to 2 dB at low bitrates (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 1 \text{MB}$</tex>) compared to the baseline 3DGS compression methods.

title: Film Grain Synthesis with Debanding Feature
authors: Andrey Norkin
abstract: Film grain synthesis is a powerful tool that can significantly reduce the bitrate of a grainy video. It is typically used with noise removal before the compression, which can make banding more pronounced in the compressed video. When the synthesized grain is added, the banding can still be visible, even at mid QPs. This article describes three algorithms that can be used with the AV1/AV2 film grain synthesis to reduce visibility of underlying bands in the re-noised video. These changes to the film grain synthesis algorithm are computationally inexpensive and improve the perceptual video quality when banding is present.

title: Intelligent Smart Energy Monitoring Architecture for Real-Time Consumption Analysis and Automated Efficiency Optimization in Systems
authors: Dineshraj V, Arshith K B, D Beaulah Princiba
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The Smart Energy Monitoring System responds to the increased demand of the effectiveness of electricity usage in the home setting. Conventional monitoring methods have no idea of real-time monitoring and control which is actionable and a resultant wastage of energy and increasing bills. The business idea is to design a system that will utilize a microcontroller that has both a voltage and current sensor to gauge usage by the household appliances. With wireless communication on IoT, data is processed and sent to a mobile or web dashboard on which users may monitor real-time usage and receive notifications as well as generate consumption reports. The setting of appliances remotely improves safety and demand-side efficiency. It is experimentally verified that the system helps it to minimize unnecessary power consumption and provides a userfriendly interface with the lower cost. The work leads to a sustainable way of living because it allows making better decisions and encourages the use of energy resources responsibly in the houses of the age of smart products.

title: Computer Vision-Based Emotion Recognition Methods
authors: Haoqi Qin
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Emotion recognition from facial expressions plays an important social and economic role in scenarios such as intelligent human-computer interaction, online education, public safety, and mental health monitoring. Traditional machine learning methods can perform emotion recognition based on facial images, but they often require manually designing feature representations for different emotion categories, heavily relying on expert knowledge and having limited generalization ability. In recent years, deep learning methods have been able to automatically learn emotional features in an end-to-end manner, significantly improving the level of automation and feature expression. However, models are still susceptible to the impact of imbalanced class labels, with a risk of overfitting, and there is still room for improvement in overall accuracy. To address this, this paper constructs an emotion recognition model based on ResNet34 and proposes a training strategy combining data augmentation and a weighted loss function to mitigate the performance degradation caused by label imbalance. Experimental results on the AffectNet dataset show that, compared to the baseline method <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(70.39 {\%})$</tex>, the classification accuracy of the proposed method improves to 71.79%, demonstrating the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method for facial emotion recognition tasks.

title: IoT-Based Intelligent Child Tracking System with Cloud Integration and Geofencing
authors: Sujay Chandran R, Krishna A S, Sri Selva Muthu Kumaran S, Sivapalanirajan M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent and energyefficient IoT-based Child Tracking System for real-time safety monitoring. The system consists of synchronized communication between the Child device and Parent device. They enable continuous location awareness and rapid emergency response with device location. The child device acquires GPS coordinates and transmits data via a low-power LoRa link, while GSM is activated only for geofence violations, SOS alerts, and communication fallback. Location data are periodically synchronized with a cloud platform to ensure secure storage, real-time visualization, and historical traceability. An integrated geofencing mechanism defines virtual safety zones and triggers instant SMS alerts with map links upon boundary breaches, even in the GPS-denied zone, using LoRa. Analytics-based evaluation shows that the proposed hybrid architecture reduces average power consumption from 1.46 W to 0.46 W, achieving a 69% reduction and improving battery life compared to GSM-only systems. The hybrid communication design ensures redundancy, reliability, and optimal power usage by balancing continuous local updates with periodic cloud synchronization. This architecture provides high accuracy, low latency, and extended device operation.

title: Time-Domain Waveform Mapping and Prediction of Ultrasonic Guided Waves Based on Deep Learning
authors: Kuangdong Zhou, Gang Liu, Lei Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Addressing the signal processing challenges in guided wave nondestructive testing of storage tank bottom plates, this paper proposes a surrogate model aimed at establishing a mapping relationship between the scattering signal database and damage types. By employing the Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) model, the changes in wave packets are analyzed to assess the operational condition of the storage tank. This study provides a novel signal generation method for damage identification and detection in storage tank bottom plates.

title: ASIM 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)

title: Deep Non-Negative Matrix Factorization for Recommender Systems
authors: Chahrazed Mediani, Abdelaziz Lakhfif, Ferial Senator, Hanane Boutouta, Karima Anani
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Cyber Physical Systems (AICPS)
abstract: A recommender system is a specific form of information filtering that helps users find items from enormous catalogs to present them based on their preferences and behavior. Items can be books, films, news, and music. Selecting the right approach has gotten difficult due to the abundance of available methods. When a recommender system performs better than other approaches, it can be compared and found to be a valuable tool. This work uses deep learning approach to implement collaborative recommendation algorithms for online resources (MFDL Model). This model is based on the matrix factorization. The Matrix Factorization with Deep Learning contributes to deep learning collaborative recommender systems by effectively bridging the gap between traditional matrix factorization (MF) and the expressive power of deep neural networks (DNNs). To compare the proposed model, we have implemented, evaluated, and compared two machine learning approaches: Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and Non-Negative Matrix Factorization (NMF). The metrics evaluation allowed us to deduce that the MFDL model present good results for the dataset used.

title: Influence of Free-Stream Turbulence on the Aerodynamic Performance of Paired Vertical-Axis Wind Turbine
authors: Ayoub Talamalek, Ahmed Chafik Guermache, Akram Gouaidia
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: Vertical-axis wind turbines (VAWTs), particularly when arranged in pairs, present promising opportunities for specific applications, yet their theoretical foundation and technological maturity still lag behind those of horizontal-axis wind turbines (HAWTs). Advances in experimental techniques and computational modelling now offer pathways to close this gap. The present work investigates the influence of inflow turbulence on the power output of paired VAWTs using a hybrid numerical framework that couples the actuator line model (ALM) with unsteady Reynolds-averaged Navier–Stokes (uRANS) simulations. Two modelling strategies are examined: a conventional ALM using static polars combined with the Leishman–Beddoes dynamic stall model, and a modified ALM that incorporates experimentally derived dynamic polars without any dynamic stall modelling. In both cases, aerodynamic coefficients are obtained from pitching aerofoil measurements and Xfoil. Model predictions are benchmarked against wind tunnel data to assess accuracy. The findings highlight the capability of the ALM–uRANS method to reliably capture the aerodynamic behaviour of VAWTs operating under turbulent inflow conditions.

title: A Σ-Structure Ripple-Cancellation Switching Regulator with Automatic Ripple Calibration Achieving Sub-mV Output Voltage Ripple
authors: Haoran Xu, Desheng Zhang, Jingbin Wu, Chang Liu, Jianming Lei, Han Peng, Xuecheng Zou, Run Min, Qiaoling Tong
abstract: This work presents a Σ-structure ripple-cancellation switching regulator, achieving sub-mV output ripple with low-profile passive components in a wide operation range. Through feedback-based automatic ripple calibration, a low-voltage auxiliary branch provides a complementary current for dominant ripple cancellation. Furthermore, the residual ripple caused by parasitic effects is eliminated by a feedforward-based active nonlinear ripple decoupler. Fabricated in 180-nm BCD process, the regulator achieves an average output ripple of 700μV in the full operation range.

title: Multi-Spectral Filter-Less Computational Imager in Standard CMOS for Beyond-Visible Ubiquitous Machine Vision and Flexible Adaptation to Application
authors: Udara Samurdhi Harshanga Kalingage, Karim Ali, Ruiyuan Yang, Massimo Alioto
abstract: A 256×256 computational multispectral imager capable of extracting visual features from UV to visible and NIR is presented. A novel 14T pixel enables spectral selectivity without physical filters, while sliding-row biasing achieves 30 fps at sub-mW power. This architecture extends machine vision to beyond-visible conditions (e.g., fog, smoke, inspection) and supports seamless integration into standard CMOS SoCs. The computational approach enables die reuse and unprecedented adaptation to the application (e.g., setting wavelengths of interest).

title: Genetic Algorithm–Based Optimization of RoBERTa for Sexism Detection
authors: Luis Miguel Huerta-Venegas, Rodrigo Israel Hernandez-Marariegos, Jaime Cerda-Jacobo, José Ortiz-Bejar
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: This paper presents the results achieved with a RoBERTa model optimized using genetic algorithms (GAs). The proposal is evaluated over the dataset provided for the task CLEF 2025 EXIST: Sexism detection in social media content. Our strategy combines the power of RoBERTa with evolutionary optimization techniques to fine-tune hyperparameters in a multi-task learning framework. The genetic algorithm is used to adjust the learning rate, dropout, batch size, and loss weight across three interconnected tasks: binary sexism detection, source intention classification, and sexism categorization. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements over a direct fine-tuning approach, achieving accuracy improvements of $3.39 \%, 15.09 \%$, and $6.73 \%$ for the respective tasks.

title: Development of IoT stations for measuring air and water quality by using optical spectroscopy
authors: Marvin Ramos, Samuel De la O, Nelson Cisneros, Jorge Cuadra, Oscar Deodanes, Carlos Rudamas
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: This study presents the development and implementation of a low-cost IoT station for air quality monitoring using Differential Optical Absorption Spectroscopy (DOAS). The proposed technology enables real-time detection of key parameters such as atmospheric pollutants. In addition, this IoT devices can also be used to measure water quality through computer simulations, retrieving parameters of phytoplankton concentrations, particulate matter and total suspended matter.

title: GPU-Accelerated Chirplet Transform: Scalable Runtime Profiling and Analysis
authors: Nishant Kumar, Steve Mann
abstract: The chirplet transform provides a powerful and flexible approach to analyzing non-stationary signals in many types of sensing modalities. Its broad application has been hindered, however, by the computational expense of its parameter search process, especially in cases where high-resolution parameter sweeps are needed. This paper presents a hardware-accelerated framework for chirplet decomposition based on GPU parallelism with unified memory that achieves scalable performance across a variety of signal types. The system was tested on seven simulated sensing signals with different temporal and spectral dynamics, including EEG (awake and sleep), EMG, radar, and vibration signals. Results demonstrate up to 18x acceleration in the chirplet search process, with considerable reduction of CPU usage and system memory load. Remarkably, the GPU version is increasingly faster for longer signals, e.g., sleep EEG, even with overhead from unified memory. The results prove the potential of GPU-accelerated chirplet transforms for large-scale or near real-time sensing applications, while also highlighting dictionary generation as an important area for further optimization.

title: Light Detector System for Analyzing Plant Cultivation Utilization of Reflected Light from Ground Surfaces
authors: Takashige Kaihatsu, Jo Kitada, Toshihiko Kinugasa, Daiju Taketoshi, Yusuke Miyazaki, Sang-Seok Lee, Tadao Matsunaga
abstract: This study aims to elucidate how plants utilize reflected light and to develop a light detection system for the associated measurements and analysis. The light environment was measured using different types of ground surfaces. In addition, the differences between the intensities of upward and downward incident light, as well as changes in reflected light according to the ground surface type, were effectively compared.

title: Data-Driven Prediction of Electric Vehicle Charging Behavior
authors: Liwei Zhu, Yucheng Du, Dong Cheng
publish: 2026 5th Asia Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Machine Learning (CACML)
abstract: The rapid growth rate of electric vehicle (EV) ownership has, to some extent, led to issues such as supplydemand mismatch of charging facilities and charging load, as well as grid impacts. There is an urgent need to investigate EV charging behavior to better predict charging loads and optimize the layout of charging infrastructure. This paper proposes a spatiotemporal prediction model for EV charging behavior based on GAT-BiGRU. The model treats actual distributed charging stations as nodes in a graph structure and employs a multi-head graph attention mechanism (GAT) to dynamically quantify the importance of neighboring nodes, thereby accurately capturing complex spatial dependencies between charging stations. This enables the model to focus on the interactive influences of key nodes. Furthermore, the model utilizes a bidirectional gated recurrent unit (BiGRU) network to mine the temporal evolution patterns of multi-feature charging behavior data and integrate multi-dimensional features, adapting to dependencies at different time scales. Case validation conducted on a specific demographic groups within a city demonstrates that, compared to existing spatiotemporal prediction models, the proposed model exhibits superior performance in both prediction efficiency and accuracy.

title: A Modified Gilbert-Cell Down-Conversion Mixer in 0.13 μm SiGe BiCMOS
authors: Rahul Maithani, Vadim Issakov
abstract: This paper presents a modified down-conversion Gilbert cell mixer designed in a 130nm BiCMOS technology. The mixer is designed with a Gilbert cell architecture with a passive transconductance stage. The mixer achieves a conversion gain of 7.2 dB and a 1 dB compression point of around −13 dBm, consuming a current close to 5.3mA from a 1.8V supply. The presented mixer favors compared to state-of-the-art in terms of power consumption and conversion gain, while preserving a competitive linearity and noise figure. The total area of the chip is 0.51mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> which makes it a viable solution for radar applications.

title: Research on Vulnerable Regions of MEMS Sensors Based on Multi-Physics Coupling Simulation
authors: Gaoyu Sun, Wenchao You, Jie Cheng, Jiaoying Huang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Capacitive MEMS sensors with silicon microstructures exhibit high sensitivity, low noise, and low power consumption, making them widely applicable in various markets. To further enhance their reliability and identify potential failure mechanisms, this study focuses on the MAX53X accelerometer as a research subject. A device physical model was established to perform multi-physics coupling simulations, aiming to analyze vulnerable regions. Simulation results reveal that under electro-thermo-mechanical coupled stress, stress concentrations occur at solder joints, bonding necks, the roots of spring structures in micromechanical mechanisms, structure-chip interfaces, and geometric corners. These stress concentrations may exceed the yield limit, leading to potential failures. This research provides valuable insights for the structural design and optimization of MEMS sensors.

title: Deep Learning for Friction Velocity Estimation in Urban Roughness Sublayer
authors: Tao Chen, Renming Yuan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Urban friction velocity is a key parameter governing turbulent momentum transport. Traditional Monin-Obukhov similarity theory(MOST) fails over heterogeneous urban surfaces due to its assumptions of horizontal homogeneity and steady flux. To overcome this limitation, we propose multiscale TSMixer(MS-TSMixer), a specialized multi-scale extension of the TSMixer architecture that embeds parallel convolution modules across four physical scales to enable end-to-end friction velocity estimation without prior stability functions. Using 362,000 quality-controlled one-minute samples from an 18-meter urban tower in Hefei from January 2022 to June 2023, we conduct a systematic benchmark of MOST, RF, ANN, and MS-TSMixer. Results show MS-TSMixer achieves an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R$</tex> squared value of 0.8168 and a root mean square error of 0.0702 meters per second, reducing error by 42 percent relative to MOST. Although overall <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}$</tex> squared improves only modestly from 0.8018 to 0.8168, wind-speed stratification reveals significant gains: strong-wind performance exceeding three meters per second increases from 0.4745 to 0.5442, confirming that the multi-scale module effectively captures enhanced coupling under high winds. This study demonstrates that MS-TSMixer robustly characterizes urban turbulence without prior assumptions, providing a transferable framework for intelligent urban meteorological parameterization.

title: RGB-T Multi-modal Visual Question Answering in Nighttime and Adverse Environment
authors: Songyuan Yang, Fan Yang, Biwen Yang, Jing Zhao, Yongqiang Sun, Ruiheng Zhang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Visual Question Answering (VQA) models that rely only on RGB inputs often fail in nighttime and adverse environments due to poor illumination and semantic loss. To address this, we propose an RGB-T VQA framework that integrates visible and thermal infrared (TIR) modalities. The framework contains two key modules: a Cross-Modal Guided Attention (CGA) that uses thermal cues to refine RGB features, and a Thermal-Semantic Prior (TSP) that compensates for the limited semantics of TIR data. In addition, we construct a large-scale RGB-T VQA dataset covering diverse nighttime, low-light, and adverse weather scenes. Experiments show that our mode

title: OAFA-Net: A Lightweight Orientation-Aware Network for Remote Sensing Scene Classification
authors: Shuo Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Remote sensing scene classification (RSSC) is a significant research direction within the field of remote sensing. Traditional deep learning networks are hindered in practical deployment for RSSC due to their substantial parameter counts and high computational overhead. Furthermore, standard convolutional operations struggle to adapt to the arbitrary object orientations and multi-scale variations inherent in remote sensing imagery, which constrains further improvements in classification accuracy. To address the above challenges, we propose a lightweight orientation-aware adaptive feature aggregation network, OAFA-Net. The network enhances spatial awareness by incorporating CoordConv to resolve the position insensitivity of traditional Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). We also designed an Orientation-Aware Attention (OAA) module and an Adaptive Feature Aggregation (AFA) module. The OAA module improves feature capturing for arbitrarily oriented objects, while the AFA module intelligently fuses multi-scale features to generate a highly discriminative feature map for classification. Experiments on the publicly available AID and NWPU datasets show that OAFA-Net achieves excellent classification results while reducing computational costs and parameter counts, providing a new paradigm for constructing efficient and accurate RSSC models.

title: BWPF-XGB: A Gradient-Reshaped and Weighted Fusion Framework for Extreme Imbalanced Fault Detection in Semiconductor Manufacturing
authors: Tengkai Huang, Yaozong Wang, Jianming Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Semiconductor fault detection faces challenges like gradient swamping and boundary bias under extreme class imbalance (1.598% rate) and high-dimensional data. This paper proposes BWPF-XGB, a framework integrating gradientreshaping asymmetric loss (M1), weighted probability fusion (M2), and dynamic threshold calibration (M3) to enhance rare fault capture. Validation on the industrial P4-CV07 dataset demonstrates significant performance gains: Average Precision (AP) rose from 0.1042 to 0.5865, and the Matthews Correlation Coefficient (MCC) reached 0.5745. BWPF-XGB provides robust algorithmic support for monitoring high-dimensional sparse targets in advanced manufacturing environments.

title: Modeling and Robust Control of SEIG-Based Wind Energy Systems Using Linear RST and Nonlinear Sliding Mode VOC Techniques
authors: Zabouri Abdelhamid, Abdallah Belabbes, Della Krachai Mohamed, Riyadh Bouddou, Adel Oubelaid, Abdussalam Ali Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: In this work, we address the challenge of ensuring robust control of a self-excited induction generator (SEIG) operating in isolated wind energy systems, where parameter variations and operating conditions significantly affect performance. As a first contribution, the magnetization curve of the generator is experimentally extracted in order to ensure an accurate modeling of the SEIG under different operating points. Based on this model, two voltage-oriented control (VOC) strategies are implemented and compared: a linear cascade controller (RST for current control and PI for DC-bus regulation) and a nonlinear second-order sliding mode controller (SMC). The proposed study evaluates the robustness of both controllers against parameter variations, wind-speed fluctuations, and load-torque disturbances. Simulation results obtained under MATLAB/Simulink demonstrate that the SMC provides superior disturbance rejection and reduced current harmonic distortion, while the RST-PI structure offers fast dynamics with simpler implementation.

title: Multimodal Large Model-Driven AI Parking Recognition Technology
authors: Yu Luo, Yuhao Cao, Kun Cheng, Liang Yi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This paper proposes an AI parking recognition technology based on the multimodal visual model GLM-4.6V for parking detection tasks. First, prompt engineering is employed to optimize model outputs, enhancing their accuracy and practicality. Through formulaic prompt design, the model achieves low-code implementation and visualization, thereby lowering the barrier to entry. By training and fine-tuning the model, deeper integration with multimodal interaction is realized. For precise localization of parking space corners, GLM-4.6V utilizes special markers ˋ<\vert begin_of_box\vert >ˋ and ‘<\vert end_of_box\vert >’ to embed target region coordinates within linguistic descriptions, enabling accurate alignment between verbal instructions and visual elements. Finally, by integrating prompt engineering, the multimodal interaction capabilities of GLM-4.6 V are leveraged to perform parking space detection and parking maneuver detection tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that GLM-4.6V outperforms GLM-4.5V, YOLOv7, and YOLOv3 in overall detection speed, while offering more comprehensive and stable performance.

title: Improved ASD Detection from Brain fMRI Using Fine-Tuned Transfer Learning on Pretrained Deep Convolutional Models
authors: Fedia Bahloul, Raouia Mokni, Monji Kherallah
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Autism spectrum disorder (ASD) is a complex neurodevelopmental condition that primarily affects social interaction, communication, and behavior, often manifesting through repetitive patterns. Early detection of ASD is crucial, as timely intervention significantly improves patients' quality of life. However, conventional diagnostic approaches are frequently subjective, time-consuming, and resource-intensive. To address these challenges, this study proposes an enhanced deep learning-based framework for the early detection of ASD using brain fMRI data. Specifically, we fine-tuned two pretrained architectures, EfficientNetB2 and ResNet50, and evaluated their performance on the preprocessed ABIDE dataset. Data augmentation techniques were applied to overcome dataset limitations and improve generalization. The resulting dataset after the augmentation contents 7,775 images which were 3,773 to ASD class and 4,002 to NON-ASD class. Experimental results demonstrate that the fine-tuned EfficientNetB2 model achieved an accuracy of 96%, significantly outperforming the fine-tuned ResNet50 model, which reached 80%, representing a 16% improvement in performance. These findings highlight the potential of optimized transfer learning models for clinical applications, underscoring their effectiveness in supporting early and accurate ASD diagnosis. The study contributes to the development of robust, efficient, and reliable diagnostic systems, paving the way for improved management of neurodevelopmental disorders.

title: CDDPM Data Augmentation and Fault Diagnosis for Small-Sample Vibration Signals
authors: Mingze Bai, Junxia Guo
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Vibration data from industrial equipment commonly suffers from issues such as sparse samples and class imbalance, which limit the performance of fault diagnosis models based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs). To address this issue, this paper proposes a vibration signal data augmentation method based on the Conditional Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (CDDPM). The method first converts one-dimensional vibration signals into frequency-domain visualizations (FDVIs), then trains a conditional diffusion model to generate high-quality fault samples, and finally uses these generated samples to augment the training set, thereby improving the diagnostic accuracy of the CNN classifier. Experimental results on a dataset of rolling bearing failure data from oil transfer pumps collected in an actual petrochemical production environment demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves classification performance, achieving an accuracy of 85.7% and with a macro F1-score of 0.84. This outperforms traditional data augmentation methods as well as deep generative models based on Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Variational Autoencoders (VAEs), validating its effectiveness and practicality in fault diagnosis tasks under low-sample-size and class-imbalanced scenarios, and providing new technical support for the predictive maintenance of industrial equipment.

title: Comprehensive Analysis and Detection of Network Level Session Hijacking Attacks Across TCP/IP and UDP Protocols
authors: Dena Abu Laila
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Network-level session hijacking attacks pose significant threats to modern communication systems by exploiting vulnerabilities in TCP/IP and UDP protocols. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of six critical attack vectors: TCP/IP hijacking, RST hijacking, blind hijacking, UDP hijacking, IP spoofing, and Man-in-the-Middle (MiTM) attacks using ICMP and ARP spoofing. We develop a controlled testbed environment to systematically evaluate these attacks and analyze their impact on network layers 3 and 4. Our experimental methodology combines packet-level analysis, performance impact assessment, and detection mechanism evaluation. Results demonstrate that traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) exhibit limited effectiveness against sophisticated session hijacking techniques, with detection rates varying from 34% to 78% depending on attack complexity. We propose an enhanced defense framework that integrates real-time behavioral analysis with machine learning-based anomaly detection, achieving 94.2% accuracy in identifying session hijacking attempts while reducing false positive rates by 67%. The proposed framework provides network administrators with actionable insights for strengthening security postures against evolving session hijacking threats.

title: A Planar and Low-Profile CPW-Fed UWB MIMO Antenna with High Isolation
authors: Rahul Kumar Garg, Mayank Jalan, Nihal Mittal, Rahul Porwal
abstract: The paper presents a novel coplanar waveguide (CPW)-fed two-port MIMO antenna designed for ultrawideband (UWB) applications (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.1-10.6 \text{GHz}$</tex> frequency range). The antenna achieves a wide impedance bandwidth of 7.2 GHz, spanning from 3.2 GHz to 10.4 GHz, and is fabricated on a lowcost FR-4 substrate with a compact footprint of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16 \times 23 \text{mm}^{2}$</tex>. A fractional bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 5. 8 8 {\%}}$</tex> is achieved, ensuring robust performance across the entire UWB spectrum. The MIMO configuration employs two identical monopole elements, strategically spaced to maximize isolation and minimize mutual coupling, and achieves isolation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>16 \text{dB}$</tex> across the band. The antenna exhibits a maximum of 4 dB of gain with constant radiation patterns. The performance metrics of MIMO, such as envelope correlation coefficient (ECC) and diversity gain (DG), achieve values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.01$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>9.98$</tex>, respectively. The proposed design is fabricated and experimentally tested, and measurements have close agreement with simulated values. The antenna is compact in size, has a wide bandwidth, good isolation, and can be used in modern UWB MIMO communication.

title: Peruvian Starch-Based Magnetic Hydrogel for Crystal Violet Dye Removal in Aqueous Media
authors: Anibal Alviz-Meza, María V. Carranza-Oropeza, Ysabel Huaccallo-Aguilar, Silvia Álvarez-Torrellas, Juan Garcia-Rodriguez
publish: 2025 IEEE Latin American Conference on Nanotechnology (LANANO)
abstract: This study deals with the synthesis and performance of a magnetic hydrogel prepared from the starch of the native Amarilla Reyna Peruvian potato. The hydrogel was successfully synthesized by grafting polyacrylic acid onto starch, followed by crosslinking with N, N'-methylene-bisacrylamide and a further in situ magnetization with iron oxides. The performed characterization of the material involved FTIR, SEM-EDS, and Hg intrusion porosimetry techniques. Also, the hydrogel was tested in the Crystal Violet (CV) dye removal from aqueous solution by adsorption, assessing kinetic, equilibrium, and thermodynamic studies. From the equilibrium adsorption studies of CV, an outstanding equilibrium adsorption capacity (q<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">e</inf>) value of 1000 mg g<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> was obtained. The experimental data of CV adsorption best fit the pseudo-first-order kinetic model and the Langmuir adsorption model, for kinetic and equilibrium studies, respectively. Thermodynamics revealed an exothermic and spontaneous process driven by an electrostatic physisorption mechanism. The hydrogel achieved over 88% CV removal efficiency across five consecutive adsorption cycles, indicating stability and straightforward magnetic recovery of the synthesized material. These results suggested that this biodegradable, affordable hydrogel can be an effective alternative for removing cationic dyes from water.

title: Review of Advances in the Modeling and Design of Composite Pressure Vessels for Hydrogen Storage
authors: Nour El Houda Boughedaoui, Omar Labbadlia, Zakarya Madaoui, Camelia Ait Hammouda, Younes Chiba
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: Hydrogen is gaining recognition as a key clean energy carrier for future transportation systems, particularly through its application in fuel cell electric vehicles. However, one of the main challenges lies in developing storage solutions that ensure safety, performance, and reliability, especially under high-pressure conditions. In this context, ongoing research and industrial efforts are focused on designing a new generation of high-pressure composite tanks with enhanced structural performance and resistance to harsh environments. This paper reviews the latest trends and technological advances in the design of these tanks, with an emphasis on their integration into future mobility systems. It further explores the link between microscopic failure mechanisms and early indicators of structural degradation in fiber-reinforced composites. Special attention is given to filament-wound tanks, analyzing their mechanical behavior and damage progression. In addition, it discusses the contribution of recent numerical and experimental research to better prediction of burst pressure and improved real-time monitoring of structural integrity.

title: Machine Learning Framework for Early Lung Cancer Risk Prediction Using Patient-Reported Clinical Data
authors: Shrimant Rana, Aditya Dubey, Dhananjay Bisen
abstract: The research proposes a machine learning (ML) pipeline which predicts lung cancer risk through analysis of survey data from patients [1]. The framework includes all essential components which make up the ML workflow beginning with data preprocessing and ending with neural network testing. The technique is implemented within a reproducible Jupyter notebook environment. The dataset contains both categorical and numerical attributes which researchers collected through questionnaires and they conducted thorough data cleaning processes that included duplicate removal and missing value imputation and outlier detection through interquartile range method to minimize extreme value effects. The features undergo Min-Max scaling which enables all algorithms to handle them correctly. Pearson correlation analysis along with domain knowledge drives the feature selection process which keeps clinically significant symptoms such as coughing and wheezing and alcohol consumption and swallowing difficulty and allergies. The research utilizes Decision Tree, KNN, Random Forest, Gaussian Naïve Bayes, AdaBoost, Logistic Regression, and SVM as its core classical classification methods. The researchers assessed the models through stratified cross-validation while testing TensorFlow/Keras-based feedforward neural network systems. The evaluation process uses AUC, accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC analysis as performance metrics. The research results show that structured survey data enables effective early lung cancer risk prediction through a framework that medical AI systems can use at scale.

title: Structured Irradiance Benchmark for Performance Assesment of MPPT Algorithms in PV System
authors: Hania Ladaycia, Bouloukza Ibtissam, Senani Fawzi, Rahab Abderrezak
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: This paper proposes a realistic and structured irradiance benchmark for evaluating Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) algorithms in photovoltaic (PV) systems. Unlike conventional step or ramp profiles, the proposed irradiance signal emulates stochastic cloud dynamics and atmospheric variability over time. Three MPPT techniques Perturb and Observe (P&O), Adaptive Particle Swarm Optimization (APSO), and Pelican Optimization Algorithm (POA) are implemented using a detailed single-diode PV model in MATLAB/Simulink. Simulation results reveal that APSO achieves the fastest convergence and highest tracking efficiency, while POA offers superior voltage and current stability. P&O, though simple, exhibits slower response and increased ripple under dynamic conditions. The benchmark enables fair and reproducible comparisons, providing a valuable tool for researchers seeking robust MPPT solutions in real-world solar environments.

title: NutriGuardian: An AI System for Personalized Health Guidance and Ingredient Evaluation in Packaged Foods
authors: Deepa R, Chaithanya L, Ankitha M D, Priyanka Gone, Sowmya Bai R, Vindhya C
abstract: NutriGuardian is a cutting-edge AI-powered web tool that helps people, especially the elderly and those with long-term medical conditions, make educated dietary decisions by making nutritional information in packaged food products easier to understand. The proposed system integrates LSTM based optical character recognition (LSTM-OCR), Rule-Based Classification (RBC) and Natural Language Processing (NLP), to gather, assess, and personalize nutritional information from product labels. This study addresses the crucial problem of comprehending complicated ingredient lists on food packaging, particularly for people with PCOS, obesity, diabetes, hypertension, and thyroid issues. NutriGuardian enhances how consumers engage with food labels by offering condition-specific meal recommendations, color-coded ingredient classifications (Safe/Caution/Avoid), and individualized health scores (0-100%).

title: Intrusion Detection and Prevention System Using Hybrid Classification Model
authors: Surya R, Robin Jose R, Ahila Devi E
abstract: This work focuses on creating a classification model that can detect and identify the type of attacks. We used an IDS dataset to train and evaluate four supervised learning models: Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest, Logistic Regression and XGBoost. To improve predictive performance, we implemented a Stacking Ensemble model that uses three models as base learners and Logistic Regression as the meta classifier. We compared the models based on their accuracy. The experimental results show that while individual models performed well, the stacking ensemble outperformed the standalone classifiers by leveraging their complementary strengths. This approach demonstrates how effective ensemble learning can be used in improving classification accuracy and robustness for intrusion detection tasks. It provides a solid foundation for securing network systems against evolving cyber threats.

title: Green Synthesis of Gold Nanoparticles Using Camellia sinensis and Baccharis trimera for a Sustainable Colorimetric Biosensor for Rapid and Selective Drug Detection
authors: Renan Avelar Da Silva, Nathan de Mello Munhoz, Willian Eduardo Rodrigues Da Silva, Kátia Palma Matias, Fernando Reinoldo Scremin, Giselle Giovanna Do Couto de Oliveira, Otávio Akira Sakai
publish: 2025 IEEE Latin American Conference on Nanotechnology (LANANO)
abstract: The synthesis of gold nanoparticles (AuNPs) was carried out using a rapid method at room temperature. The influence of different concentrations of the metal precursor (HAuCl<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>) on the formation, stability, and size distribution of the nanoparticles was evaluated, characterized by UV-Vis spectroscopy and dynamic light scattering (DLS). Higher concentrations (≈1.5 mM) resulted in an intense and well-defined surface plasmon resonance (SPR) band (520–540 nm) and narrow hydrodynamic distributions, with low polydispersity indices. Lower concentrations resulted in weak and poorly defined SPR, associated with heterogeneous particles. The optimized AuNPs were applied in the colorimetric detection of Paracetamol (acetaminophen) and dipyrone (metamizole sodium). Both drugs were observed to interact with AuNPs, promoting spectral changes and noticeable color variations, attributed to aggregation processes induced by chemical interactions between the analyte functional groups and the nanoparticle surface. The results confirm that precursor concentration is crucial not only for controlled AuNP formation but also for maximizing sensitivity and reproducibility in colorimetric drug detection applications.

title: Martingale and Graph Neural Network Edge Reweighting for Stable Community Detection
authors: Akram Karimi Zarandi, Ali Fahim
abstract: Modularity-based community detection is highly sensitive to edge weights, often producing unstable partitions under small perturbations. While edge reweighting is commonly used to influence community structure, the relative impact of stochastic and learned reweighting strategies on partition stability remains unclear. We compare two fundamentally different paradigms: martingale-based stochastic perturbation and graph neural network-based learned edge weighting derived from contrastive node representations. Experiments on five real-world and synthetic networks reveal a clear modularity-stability trade-off. On the BA_500 network, stochastic reweighting slightly increases modularity (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.382 \rightarrow 0.385$</tex>) but reduces stability (NMI <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.331 \rightarrow 0.232$</tex>). In contrast, learned edge reweighting improves both modularity (0.398) and stability (0.546). On the Karate Club network, stability increases from 0.836 to 0.922 under learned weighting while maintaining competitive modularity. Overall, learned edge reweighting consistently yields more stable community partitions while preserving or improving modularity, particularly on large and noisy graphs. These results highlight the role of structural learning in enhancing robustness for stable community detection.

title: Design and Evaluation of ChordVDB: A Distributed Vector Database System for Augmented Retrieval
authors: Danial Choupan, Arash Deldari, Setayesh Edalati Rad
abstract: In recent years, Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as one of the most significant advancements in artificial intelligence and natural language processing. Leveraging the Transformer architecture and training on massive datasets, these models offer high-accuracy capabilities in understanding and generating human language. However, they remain susceptible to a phenomenon known as hallucination, in which the model produces inaccurate or fabricated information. One of the most effective solutions to mitigate this issue is the use of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), which grounds the model's responses in real and verifiable data. In this research, we present ChordVDB (Chord-based Vector DataBase), a distributed vector database system built upon the Chord algorithm and the FAISS vector search engine. In this architecture, data are evenly distributed across independent nodes, and each node performs embedding, storage, and similarity search operations locally. The Chord algorithm manages routing and key mapping, while the FAISS index ensures both high speed and high accuracy in vector retrieval. Experimental results using the SQuAD 2.0 dataset demonstrate that ChordVDB, in a nine-node configuration, achieves a throughput of 156 queries per second (QPS) and a Recall@10 of 0.586, while maintaining a 100% response success rate under node-failure conditions. These results indicate that ChordVDB provides a scalable, resilient, and efficient infrastructure for retrieval-augmented systems.

title: Neural Network-Based Simulation Framework for Anthrax Disease Progression in Animal Populations
authors: Vikash Panthi, Nikita Kashyap, Manoj Gupta
abstract: The paper contains a detailed simulation model, which is a neural network-based solution to the study of the anthrax disease development in animal groups. Our multiarchitecture deep learning model allows predicting the severity of the disease, its likelihood of infection, and the likelihood of death at the same time, using animal traits and environmental conditions. The framework is used to execute four different neural network structures, including Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), 1D Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and a hybrid Ensemble model. Our models performed well in all prediction tasks with synthetic data that mimicked the real world epidemiological patterns. The Ensemble model really shone compared to the individual architectures! It delivered impressive results with a 0.0195 error, 0.894 accuracy, and 0.140 loss. This clearly shows the framework's promise for creating early warning systems and planning interventions in veterinary epidemiology. The simulation is the basis of smart computing application in the management of infectious diseases and veterinary population health.

title: Increasing Engagement with Med-Chat using Gamification
authors: Łukasz Sikorski, Piotr Ablewski, Jacek Matulewski
publish: Proceedings of the 63rd ACM Conference on Computers and People Research
