title: A Timing Synchronization Algorithm for Single-Carrier Burst Signals Based on Segmented Smoothing Processing
authors: Yuefu Li, Shigang Liu, Yang Wang, Qinglong Wei
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address the problem of synchronization error caused by the time drift of synchronization parameters in singlecarrier burst signal, a “segment estimation and smooth connection” forward timing synchronization algorithm is proposed. This algorithm segments the signal and based on the quasi-static assumption, achieves precise parameter estimation within each segment. Through the smoothing factor, it balances the historical state with the current measurement value to suppress phase jumps between segments. Through MATLAB simulation and formula verification, the smoothing threshold formulas for four modulation signals (BPSK, QPSK, 8PSK, 16QAM) and the applicable signal frequency offset range were derived. The algorithm can improve the time offset of QPSK signals by 72%, the time offset of 16QAM signals by 54.2% and the MSE performance of the improved algorithm was superior to that of the original algorithm. Moreover, the inter-segment phase jump range of both algorithms is within the constraint range.

title: Robust Hevc Bitstream Stitching for Live Multiview Video Streaming
authors: Zaixi Shang, Yongjun Wu
abstract: Multiview streaming is increasingly used in sports and entertainment, but current solutions struggle to support adaptive bitrate (ABR) streaming and robustness on consumer devices that expose only a single hardware HEVC decoder. Existing HEVC bitstream stitching and cloud-mixing methods [1], [2] can merge multiple streams into one, but they are fragile when one input stream is lost. They also typically assume a fixed set of view resolutions and a static layout, so if ABR logic tries to change the resolution of one view independently, the resulting arrangement may no longer form a valid rectangular HEVC picture, making per-view ABR difficult to support.

title: Comparison of Deep Learning Architectures for Fault Detection in Power Grids: A Study on CNN, Vision Transformer, and Swin Transformer
authors: Himanshu Yadav, Deepanshu Yadav, Raghav Joshi, Nishant Srivastava, Sanjeev Kumar Sharma
abstract: Accurate and rapid fault detection in power systems is increasingly challenged by harmonic distortions and noise introduced by renewable energy integration. While deep learning has shown promise for fault classification, no systematic comparison of CNN, Vision Transformer (ViT), and Swin Transformer architectures exists under realistic noisy conditions. This paper presents such a comparison using a 31-class dataset generated from an IEEE 5-bus MATLAB/Simulink simulation, augmented with white Gaussian noise (SNR 20-40 dB), 3rd/5th/7th harmonic distortions, and fault resistances up to 100Ω. All models were trained identically on an NVIDIA T4 GPU. The CNN achieved 99.24% fault-type and 99.49% fault-location accuracy using only 7,237 parameters at 14 s/epoch—outperforming ViT (43,461 parameters, 21 s/epoch) and Swin (514,693 parameters, 64 s/epoch) on fault-type accuracy and training efficiency. Results confirm that CNNs remain the most practical architecture for real-time power system protection where computational efficiency is critical.

title: A 26.4-to-56-Gb/s 0.38-pJ/bit PAM-4 Reference-less CDR Using Frequency-Offset Quantization Scheme Achieving 65-ns Constant-Band Acquisition Time in 28nm CMOS
authors: Runchen Wang, Yu Lv, Lingao Huang, Jiaxin Sun, Ya Zhao, Guohe Zhang, Li Geng, Chao Fan
abstract: This paper reports a single-loop reference-less clock and data recovery (CDR) featuring a pattern-proportion frequency-offset quantization (PP-FQ) scheme to reduce the locking time. Specifically, the proposed PP-FQ scheme quantize the frequency offset using the sampling pattern proportion, which aids in a fast frequency band acquisition. Besides, the simple implementation of PP-FQ logic eliminates the additional frequency detector while upholding a wideband data rate range. Prototyped in 28-nm CMOS, the proposed reference-less CDR automatically could acquire 26.4-to-56 Gb/s PAM-4 data with a constant 65-ns full-band acquisition time and < 617-ns overall locking time, achieving an energy efficiency of 0.38 pJ/bit.

title: Attractor Matching: A New Paradigm for Structural String Comparison
authors: Simone Faro, Dominik Köppl, Francesco Pio Marino
abstract: We introduce Attractor Matching, a new framework for structural string comparison built upon the theory of string attractors. Given a pattern <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex> of length <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m$</tex> and one of its attractors <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Gamma_{x}$</tex>, the problem asks for all substrings <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y[i. . i+m-1]$</tex> of a text <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y$</tex> such that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Gamma_{x}$</tex> is also an attractor of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y[i. . i+m-1]$</tex>. Unlike classical notions of string matching, which rely on character equality or distance measures, attractor matching focuses on the structural properties that govern repetitiveness and compressibility. Our contribution is fourfold. First, we adapt the IsAttractor algorithm of Béal et al. by combining the DAWG with the slidingwindow technique of Blumer, enabling online attractor verification as the window advances over the text. Second, we reformulate the verification procedure on the Compressed DAWG (CDAWG), obtaining a more compact representation that preserves correctness. Third, we employ the sliding-window CDAWG method of Inenaga et al., which allows efficient attractor matching on sliding-window maintained CDAWGs with incremental updates. Finally, we introduce a relaxed variant, Attractor Matching with Mismatches, where the pattern attractor may be extended by at most <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\rho$</tex> additional positions, enabling structurally tolerant matching. This paradigm bridges compression and similarity, opening new directions for structure-aware pattern matching.

title: Design and Modelling of a Bidirectional ThreePhase Solid-State Transformer for Advanced Power System Applications
authors: Khaled Ghambirlou, Adel Ali Abosnina, Gerry Moschopoulos
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive design and dynamic modelling framework for a bidirectional three-phase Solid-State Transformer (SST) tailored for future powersystem architectures. The proposed SST unifies an actively controlled AC/DC rectifier, an isolated Dual Active Bridge (DAB) DC-DC converter, and a grid-forming DC/AC inverter within a coordinated control hierarchy that enables seamless bidirectional power transfer, adaptive voltage regulation, and intelligent grid-support functionality. A detailed dq-domain representation is developed in MATLAB/Simulink to capture the coupled electrical and control dynamics across all conversion stages under both steady-state and transient operating conditions. The simulated results confirm robust DC-link regulation, high-efficiency bidirectional energy exchange, and precise voltage synthesis during dynamic load and grid disturbances. The modelling framework establishes a validated foundation for the optimal design, coordination, and deployment of SSTs in advanced distribution and transmission networks.

title: IoT-Based Health Monitoring System for Textile Workers
authors: R. Ramya, Sowndar Raj R, Vijay L
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: For workers in physically demanding industries like the textile industry, unintentional falls and unexpected medical emergencies are major safety concerns. The risk of falls, which can result in serious injuries and delayed medical aid, is increased by fatigue, slick flooring, extended standing, and constant machine operation. Slow emergency reaction is caused by traditional safety systems' lack of real-time fall detection and fast alert methods. In addition to the physical risks, textile workers are subjected to dangerous environmental factors including loud noises and dust in the air, which have an adverse effect on their health and productivity. This project suggests a wearable safety and health monitoring system for textile industry workers that is integrated with the Internet of Things. Through a two-stage verification procedure that includes vibration alerts and manual cancelation to reduce false alarms, the wearable device employs motion sensors to identify fall- like actions. Supervisors or family members automatically receive emergency messages with location and health information if no response is received. Noise and dust sensors operate in tandem with fall detection to continuously monitor workplace conditions and send out alarms when safety criteria are exceeded. The suggested system promotes a safer and healthier textile manufacturing environment, guarantees prompt intervention, and enhances worker safety.

title: Real-Time AI-Driven Multi-Store Sales Forecasting and Automated Inventory Optimization System with Role-based Retail Intelligence
authors: K.V.N.S. Mahendra, Nukala Rohith, Rajaram V, Balakiruthiga B
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Retail companies have difficulties in keeping the optimal level of inventory because of the dynamic nature of demand, which is affected by seasonality, pricing, and promotions. This research work presents a Real-Time AI-Based Sales Forecasting and Inventory Optimization System that improves retail decision-making through the use of predictive analytics and automation. The system combines real-time sales and inventory data from cloud-based platforms such as Point-of-Sale (POS) systems and Google Sheets APIs, removing the need for manual uploading of the dataset for analysis. A hybrid forecasting approach that combines Random Forest, AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) models is used to make accurate predictions of demand. The system uses automation techniques like Economic Order Quantity (EOQ), safety stock, and reorder point models to make inventory decisions, automatically generating purchase orders when certain levels of inventory are reached. The system is capable of managing multiple stores through centralized analytics and uses Role-Based Access Control dashboards for Admin, Manager, and Supplier users. Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) tests show that the system has high predictive accuracy.

title: The Neurological Code: Deciphering Parkinson's with Deep Generative Models and Attention Mechanisms
authors: Pakanati Chandana Reddy, I.V. Shashikala, T. Aditya Sai Srinivas, K. Dora Babu, M. Ramu, B. Sahadeva Reddy
abstract: This paper examines how the scarcity of medical imaging datasets hinders the development of deep learning models for the diagnosis of Parkinson's Disease (PD). Although Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have been used for data augmentation in the past, they have been found to produce synthetic images of low quality while suffering from a phenomenon called mode collapse. Our research presents a new methodology in which the first step employs a Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DDPM) to produce images of PD. This is followed by the use of a Vision Transformer (ViT) image classifier to facilitate PD detection. Our diffusion-based generator has created images of healthy individuals and individuals with Parkinson's Disease. Compared to GAN-based models, our images have a Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22.4 \pm 0.3$</tex> and are therefore of a much higher quality. After being trained on synthetic data supplemented with real-world images, the ViT classifier achieved an accuracy of 98.5% and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> score of 0.984, which demonstrates efficient generalization to new, clinically unencountered patient data. We further use Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) for model interpretability which generates visual explanations for Grad-CAM aligned with the clinical indicators. Our research demonstrates that diffusion models are a better option for medical image synthesis compared to GANs, and presents a new, scalable framework for the early diagnosis of Parkinson's Disease that is interpretable and scalable.

title: Research on Curing Degree Detection of Fiber-Wound Plastic-Lined Gas Cylinders Using RFID and Physical Information Neural Networks
authors: Zudong Cao, Kun Mao, Xuelin Zhang, Xiangwei Zuo
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The cure degree of plastic-lined fiber-wound cylinders is a critical indicator affecting their mechanical properties and service life. Traditional testing methods are often time-consuming, inefficient, or destructive. To address this issue, this study proposes a cure degree detection system based on a Radio Frequency Identification resistive resonant circuit array and a Physical Information Neural Network. This system utilizes an RFID resistive resonant circuit array to continuously capture the electromagnetic response of polymer materials during curing. Data normalization enhances sensitivity to curing degree variations. Building upon this foundation, a PINN model was constructed and optimized, deeply integrating the physical principles of curing kinetics into the neural network architecture. Comparative evaluations against pure LSTM and standard PINN models demonstrate that the proposed optimized model exhibits outstanding predictive performance: Mean Absolute Error of 0.067, Root Mean Square Error of 0.084, and a high Coefficient of Determination of 0.960. This significantly outperforms both the pure LSTM model and the standard PINN model, demonstrating the critical role of integrating physical information and optimizing network architecture in enhancing curing degree prediction accuracy and model interpretability. This study provides an efficient and feasible solution for non-destructive, precise, and intelligent monitoring of the curing degree in fiber-wound cylinders, holding significant implications for quality control in composite manufacturing processes.

title: A Passivity Criterion for Event-Triggered Communication Control of Manipulator for Bilateral Teleoperation Systems with Time-Varying Delays
authors: Yunli Gong
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The stability problem of bilateral teleoperation systems subject to time-varying delays was considered in this paper. First of all, a type of new event-triggered communication mechanism which based upon sampling control is presented to fulfil the satisfactory stability performance of teleoperation systems. And it is obvious that the sampled-data-based event-triggered control strategy is absolutely avoiding the Zeno behavior. Secondly, we utilized reciprocally convex combination lemma (RCCL) and adopted an extend reciprocally convex matrix inequality without introducing more constraints to deal with time-varying delays exist in system and develop a stability criterion for teleoperation systems. Finally, several simulation results are provided to demonstrate the validity of theorem and the efficiency of control protocol proposed in this paper.

authors: N/A

title: Research on Reinforcement Learning Decision of UAV Air Combat Based on Improved PPO Algorithm
authors: Hongyu Chen, Ruiwen Bian, Xinyi Wang, Zheshun Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The autonomous decision-making capability of drones has become pivotal in modern air combat systems, where traditional decision-making methods struggle to adapt to complex and dynamic environments. Reinforcement learning's trial-and-error mechanism demonstrates significant advantages in tackling intricate decision-making challenges. The Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm achieves a balance between training stability and sampling efficiency through its policy update pruning mechanism. Building upon PPO, this study addresses its limitations by: establishing mathematical models for drones and missiles to construct an air combat confrontation decision-making framework; redesigning multi-reward functions and developing a hybrid action space Actor network architecture to resolve low training efficiency and sparse reward issues; and creating a reinforcement learning-based air combat simulation platform to validate the algorithm. Simulation results demonstrate that the improved algorithm excels in both win rate and reward value, effectively responding to dynamic air combat scenarios.

title: Lightweight Hybrid Attention for Rock CT Image Super-Resolution
authors: Junqiang Liu, Chen Qiu
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: High-resolution rock CT images are crucial for revealing rock microstructures and supporting detailed geological analysis. Most current rock image super-resolution methods rely on CNNs, which excel in efficient local feature extraction while maintaining low computational cost. However, CNNs often struggle to model long-range dependencies in rock CT images, leading to the loss of high-frequency details and overly smoothed edges. In contrast, Transformers possess strong global modeling capabilities but typically incur high computational overhead. To address this issue, this paper proposes a lightweight rock superresolution reconstruction model, RockSwin, based on hybrid attention. This model introduces a lightweight hybrid attention module that integrates CSWin and Swin Transformers for efficient local-global feature modeling, reducing computational complexity while enhancing edge detail fidelity and overall image reconstruction quality. Experiments on two publicly available rock CT datasets demonstrate that RockSwin outperforms existing mainstream super-resolution models.

title: Physics-Constrained LSTM for Long-Term Operational Rule Extraction in IES
authors: Bowen Xiao, Jie Chen, Tingjun Li, Quanzhang Liu, Mao Tan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The long-term operation of integrated energy systems (IES) is challenged by PV output uncertainty and multienergy coupling. Conventional data-driven methods often ignore physical constraints, leading to infeasible dispatch decisions. To address this, we propose a Physics-Constrained Long Short-Term Memory (PCLSTM) framework that extracts operational rules via a simulation-optimization-learning pipeline. First, PV scenarios are generated using a SARIMAX model; then, a cost-minimization problem is solved to obtain optimal dispatches. Finally, a PCLSTM network is trained with physical constraints-such as equipment capacity, storage dynamics, and energy balanceembedded in the loss function, enabling it to learn a dispatch policy from historical optimal solutions. Case studies show the method effectively learns adaptive, conversion-aware dispatch rules, offering reliable intelligent support for the secure and economical long-term operation of PV-IES.

title: Co-Evolutionary Pruning and Dynamic Weight Aggregation Enhanced L-SHADE for Cuffless Blood Pressure Estimation
authors: Tingyu Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Continuous blood pressure (BP) monitoring is crucial for effective cardiovascular health management, as it enables the early detection of adverse BP changes. However, the conventional cuff-based method is unsuitable for continuous monitoring due to its intermittency and discomfort caused by cuff compression. To address these limitations, the success-history based adaptive differential evolution with linear population size reduction (L-SHADE) algorithm is enhanced by integrating coevolutionary pruning (CP) and dynamic weight aggregation (DWA). A novel framework is introduced: a dendritic neuron model (DNM) optimized by the CP, L-SHADE and DWA (CLD) algorithm. The complete framework is designated as DNM-CLD. It is evaluated on MIMIC database against a wide range of baselines, including MLP, EIman, LSTM, NODE and FTTransformer. The DNM-CLD achieves a superior balance between accuracy and sparsity.

title: An Automated Text Summarization Agent Framework Based on Asymmetric Role Collaboration
authors: Dihan Zheng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable progress in the field of natural language processing. However, when handling summarization tasks for long and complex texts, the conventional single-agent generation paradigm often faces challenges such as the omission of critical information, poor logical coherence, and unstable generation quality. To address these limitations, this paper proposes an automated text summarization agent framework based on asymmetric role collaboration. Rather than relying on computationally intensive swarm intelligence algorithms, the framework is designed around low-resource prompt-based interactions. It innovatively introduces two asymmetric roles with distinct divisions of labor—the “Generator” and the “Critic”—to establish an efficient “Write-Review-Revise” closed-loop iterative mechanism. To verify the effectiveness of the proposed framework, we conducted extensive evaluations on two mainstream text summarization datasets, XSum and DailyMail. Experimental results demonstrate that compared to the single-generation baseline model, this agent collaborative framework achieves significant performance improvements in both LLM Judge-based comprehensive semantic evaluations and traditional objective metrics such as BLEU. Furthermore, ablation experiments reveal a key characteristic of the iterative mechanism: moderate one-round feedback maximizes summary quality while effectively avoiding text redundancy and computational overhead associated with excessive multi-round iterations. This research provides a new methodology and empirical reference for constructing efficient, lightweight, and stable multi-agent text generation systems.

title: A Bi-Credit Evaluation and Revolving-Credit Settlement for Distributed Energy Trading
authors: Weiyi Tian, Zhe Yuan, Qiu Jing, Jiafeng Lin, Zongyu Yao, Junhua Zhao
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: Distributed renewable energy is growing fast, and it lets users both produce and sell power. This change is creating new types of local energy markets. However, most current trading systems only care about balancing power. They often forget that a user's financial health and credit history also affects how they trade. So, this paper suggests a new way to trade energy that focuses on credit. We built a system that checks a user's credit from two sides and manages their credit limits. To do this, we use a scoring method similar to FICO. The system tracks how people pay their bills and use electricity to set their credit levels. Then, the model uses these credit scores to decide electricity prices and how much energy people can use. Also, we added a “revolving credit” feature. This helps make trading more flexible and uses optionbased tools to lower risks. If someone pays late, they face a penalty, which keeps the market stable. We tested this idea on a 34-bus system. The results show that the market manager earns more, and users become 40 % more self-sufficient. In the end, the whole system costs less to run. This framework shows a simple, useful way to bring financial credit into energy markets to make them more sustainable.

title: Sliding Mode Observer-Based Detection and Reconstruction of Cyber Attacks in Cyber-Physical Power Systems with HIL Simulation
authors: Jianzhang Qu, Song Gao, Xinqing Song, Yucan Zhang, Miao Li
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: Cyber-Physical Power Systems (CPPS) tightly integrate power grid physical processes with information networks, bringing both improved efficiency and new vulnerabilities. This paper presents a comprehensive CPPS security study focusing on modeling, attack detection, and experimental validation. First, a state-space modeling approach for CPPS is developed, capturing the interaction of physical and cyber components in a unified mathematical framework. Second, a sliding mode observer (SMO) based attack detection scheme is designed to reconstruct and detect malicious actuator and sensor injection attacks in real-time. The SMO uses discontinuous injection to estimate the unknown attack inputs, and a threshold-based logic triggers an alarm when reconstructed attack signals exceed predefined limits. Finally, a three-generator nine-bus CPPS model is implemented on a Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) platform to evaluate the proposed method. HIL simulations demonstrate that the SMO can rapidly and accurately reconstruct attack signals with minimal error, enabling timely detection of both actuator and sensor attacks. The results confirm the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed observer-based attack detection approach for CPPS.

title: A Hybrid Convolutional Neural Network and Vision Transformer Architecture for Lung Cancer Classification
authors: Bishes Martha, H.S. Behera
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: One of the most serious diseases leading to a rise in death rates worldwide is Lung cancer (LC). Patient survival chances are greatly increased by early diagnosis and effective treatment. As it provides a thorough image of the anatomy and progression of the tumor, CT is the ideal imaging method for identifying lung cancer. The automatic recognition of LC from CT images has improved with the development of DL. This research deploys the ConViT framework for LC classification (LCC). The process begins with image augmentation followed by pre-processing that is performed via Median filtering. The classification of LC is performed with a hybrid model termed ConViT. From the study, the developed ConViT displays a high accuracy of 0.95.

title: High Speed Datapath Circuit Design Using Domino Logic
authors: T. Kalavathidevi, M. Madhan Mohan, K.N. Baluprithviraj, Balachandru S, Chandrabharathi M, Harshavarthini R, Joswika S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Combinational circuits are essential for data n path cells in order to maximize performance and guarantee that integrated circuit designs are proper. Combinational circuits enable effective data processing and manipulation by facilitating a variety of arithmetic operations that are crucial to microprocessors. Their incorporation into data path cells improve performance and functionality. This study uses domino logic to construct and analyze a number of important digital components including adders, subtractors, encoders, decoders, multiplexers, and demultiplexers. Compared to conventional static CMOS designs, this dynamic CMOS logic architecture delivers faster speed and reduced power usage. The fundamental idea of pre-charge and assessment phases regulated by a clock signal is used to construct each circuit. Performance is observed to be enhanced when the circuits are constructed using optimized pull-down networks. Simulation of the logics at transistor level is designed in LT-SPICE and the netlist is used in the VIVADO for implementation in FPGA BASYS board. Overall, simulation results show that these domino logic-based designs achieve around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0}-\mathbf{4 0 \%}$</tex> lower power consumption and up to 1.5 times improvement in speed, low-power, high-performance VLSI applications.

title: Hybrid Convolutional Orientation Encoder for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Cheng Su, Xiaohui Luo
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address the issue of terminal vessel loss in retinal vascular segmentation tasks, this paper proposes a bio-inspired network modelled on biological visual mechanisms. The network employs a hybrid convolutional encoder that combines Gabor convolutions with standard convolutions at different stages, simulating the directional perception and high-level feature extraction mechanisms of the V1 visual cortex. It further replaces conventional operations with Haar wavelet subsampling. By performing directional feature encoding at native resolution, Gabor wavelet extraction captures the directional structural information of vessels, effectively reducing redundant features. Experiments demonstrate that this approach achieves outstanding results across three public datasets DRIVE, STARE, and CHASE-DB1 with a significant advantage in sensitivity (Se) metrics.

title: Research on Hierarchical Configuration Strategy of Grid and Load-Side Energy Storage for Improving Urban Regional Distribution Network Capacity
authors: Xu Zhang, Yongwen Yang
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: This paper focuses on the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$220 \text{kV}-110 \text{kV}-35 \text{kV}$</tex> threelevel chained distribution network, and proposes a hierarchical allocation strategy for grid-load-energy storage to enhance the distribution network hosting capacity. Models for the individual and joint hierarchical allocation of grid-load-energy storage are developed, with the objectives of maximizing hosting capacity and minimizing total operation cost. Case studies demonstrate that the proposed strategy increases the voltage qualification rate to 99.1%, reduces power loss by 2.0% and voltage deviation by 1.93%, and significantly lowers operation cost. When the wind-PV penetration reaches 1.5 times the benchmark, the optimal tradeoff between energy storage economy and grid operation efficiency is obtained. The proposed strategy can effectively improve the hosting capacity of urban distribution networks. (Abstract)

title: Cluster Analysis of North Carolina’s County-Level Crime Rates
authors: Sebastian Potuondo, Eljilani Hmouda
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Crime disproportionately affects certain communities, reducing the quality of life, property values, and increasing stress, which is why it is essential to understand what features drive increased crime rates. This potentially allows governments and policymakers to intervene before crime rates spike. This paper examines, at the county level, which features predict court cases per capita as a proxy for crime rates in North Carolina over 5 years. Our study uses PCA, UMAP, and HDBSCAN techniques to cluster 99 of the 100 North Carolina counties based on multiple domains. Our XGBoost prediction model was able to capture $72.96 \%$ of the variance on test data ($60.86 \%$ in cross-validation). Our predictive model had overfitting concerns created by our limited sample size ($n=495$). Results showed that the socioeconomic domain was the most predictive ($34.1 \%$), followed by geography ($23.1 \%$), demographics ($15.2 \%$), and health ($\mathbf{1 4. 4 \%}$). Crime clusters added only $\mathbf{2. 5 \%}$ predictive power, further suggesting county structure is a larger predictor than previous crime rates. Our findings support social disorganization and strain theories, suggesting policy reforms on county structure rather than just policing and court reforms.

title: Design of a Low-Cost Transfer Function Based MPC-Controlled Synchronous Buck Converter
authors: S. Eswaran, Hari Charan Nannam, K. Vinoth, Majji Sai Manohar, Vuddanti Sandeep
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: This paper presents a low-cost model predictive control approach for a synchronous buck converter. A MATLAB transfer function model is developed based on the dynamic responses of the inductor current and the output voltage. The control is simplified for the real time execution on the Arduino board and to make the predictions for switching states with minimal computational load. To evaluate the performance of the system, MATLAB/SIMULINK is used to evaluate various system cases for different load conditions. The experimental validation is performed in the laboratory by designing a prototype for real time analysis.

title: Thermal-mechanical coupling simulation of Chiplet 2.5D packaging based on chiplet distribution and Full Copper Interconnection
authors: Shizhen Li, Chenshan Gao, Xu Liu, Huaiyu Ye, Guoqi Zhang
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: The semiconductor industry is facing significant challenges, including physical limitations such as heat dissipation, rising manufacturing costs, and the difficulty of further shrinking transistor sizes while maintaining performance. To address these issues, chiplet technology has emerged, integrating smaller, specialized chips into a single package. This approach improves modularity, manufacturing yields, and allows mixing different technology nodes for optimal performance. However, challenges like ensuring high-speed, low-latency interconnects and effective thermal management persist.This study explores several key aspects of chiplet technology using a simplified dual-chip model, focusing on the integration of nanocopper materials. By comparing nanocopper with traditional solder, we analyze differences in mechanical behavior and thermal management. Additionally, we investigate variations in chiplet layout, examining the impact of spacing and the number of chiplets on performance under identical power conditions. Our findings indicate that nanocopper interconnects provide lower stress and better thermal conductivity than traditional solder. Adjusting chiplet spacing and increasing the number of chiplets both enhance heat dissipation. Future work will involve larger-scale simulations and fatigue analyses to optimize chiplet packaging for improved performance and reliability.

title: Rural E-Commerce and Digital Platforms for Entrepreneurship: A Case Study in Northeast China
authors: Jinyong Zheng, Panom Gunawong, Ping Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: Rural e-commerce is a key driver of poverty alleviation and entrepreneurship in China. This study examines how Wuchang Taobao Village (WCTV) uses digital platforms to enhance livelihoods, guided by the Sustainable Livelihoods Framework (SLF) expanded with digital capital. A qualitative case study combines documentary analysis and consumer interviews. Findings show that digital platforms help WCTV mobilize natural, social, and digital assets, strengthen branding, and improve market access, thereby raising incomes, fostering entrepreneurship, and expanding social networks. However, weak human capital, financial constraints, and limited replicability remain challenges. The study contributes by extending SLF to the digital era and offering policy lessons on localization, product differentiation, and talent retention for rural e-commerce in underdeveloped regions.
