title: Highly Stretchable, Fully Screen-Printed Resistive Strain Sensor for Apple Fruit Growth Monitoring
authors: Michele Gullino, Dieter Reenaers, Lennert Purnal, Pietro Ibba, Paolo Lugli, Luisa Petti, Walter Guerra, Luigi Manfrini, Wim Deferme
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Fruit size at harvest is a critical quality attribute in the global apple market, directly influencing both market value and growers’ income. Traditional monitoring tools such as vernier calipers and fruit gauges, while widely used, are labor-intensive, costly, and require regular maintenance, making them unsuitable for large-scale use in commercial orchards. This paper introduces a novel, fully screen-printed, stretchable resistive strain sensor designed for continuous, non-invasive monitoring of apple fruit growth under field conditions. The sensor is fabricated by printing a stretchable conductive silver paste onto a thermoplastic polyurethane substrate, followed by encapsulation, laser-cut, and crimping onto terminal pads. It is secured around the fruit using a mechanically interlocking clamp that functions as a buckle, ensuring stable placement at the fruit’s maximum equatorial diameter. Experimental results demonstrate that the sensor performs reliably across a fruit diameter range of 40–80 mm, corresponding to 100% strain. The best-performing sample exhibited a 247-fold increase in resistance across the full strain range (0–100%). These findings confirm the feasibility of the proposed approach under laboratory-simulated field conditions.

title: Detection of Gas Emission from the Ground by Drone Using Fountain Flow over Rough Terrain
authors: Kyohei Yamashita, Takeru Kaji, Kasumi Yoshida, Daiki Akaogi, Haruka Matsukura, Hiroshi Ishida
abstract: The high maneuverability of multi-rotor drones makes them an attractive platform for methane emission monitoring in landfill sites. However, the strong downwash generated by the rotors poses a serious problem. When a drone equipped with a gas sensor is hovering over the place of methane emanation, the downwash from the drone blows away the methane released from the ground, and therefore, the gas sensor shows almost no response. In this series of work, we propose to use the fountain flow to solve this problem. When a multi-rotor drone is hovering close to the ground, the downwash from the rotors is deflected at the ground and an upward air current called a fountain flow is generated. In our previous reports, we have presented the results of flow simulations and experiments over a smooth flat floor to demonstrate that the fountain flow can be utilized for ground gas detection: it helps transporting the gas released from the ground surface to height of the gas sensor on the drone. Here we show results of experiments conducted over a rough ground surface. The results show that ground gas detection using the fountain flow is hardly affected by the presence of artificial turf with a height of 50 mm or balls with a diameter of 80 mm.

title: A Method for Enhancing the Clarity of Sea Surface Targets Based on Generative Diffusion Models
authors: Guangmiao Zeng, Rongjie Wang, Wanneng Yu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Ships and Electromechanical System (ICISES)
abstract: In maritime monitoring, image blurring caused by motion and vibration of cameras severely degrades the accuracy of sea surface target recognition. This paper presents a Diffusion Model-based Motion Deblurring (DMB) method that enhances image clarity under complex sea conditions. The proposed model integrates a multi-scale U-Net+ network with residual and self-attention mechanisms, enabling the preservation of critical image features and capturing long-range dependencies. Moreover, an improved fast sampling strategy derived from DDIM is introduced, which dynamically adjusts the noise variance to accelerate the reverse denoising process while maintaining reconstruction quality. Experimental results on the GoPro dataset show that the DMB method achieves 5.43% higher PSNR and 6.01% higher SSIM compared to DDPM. Real-world tests on actual sea surface images further demonstrate that DMB restores blurred ship targets more effectively, significantly improving recognition accuracy using YOLOv3 and YOLOv7 detectors. The proposed approach provides a robust and efficient solution for real-time image clarity enhancement and maritime visual perception applications.

title: LADE-YOLO: A Lightweight Attention-Based Instance Segmentation Network for Edge-Computing Logistics Robots
authors: Zijie Ma, Zhijie Yang, Yiming Fang, Zhanpeng Liang, Le Zheng, Yunhui Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Autonomous Mobile Robots (AMRs) and Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs) face severe perceptual challenges in dynamic logistics warehouses. In visual SLAM systems, interference from dynamic objects (e.g., walking personnel, moving forklifts) frequently leads to localization drift. To address this issue under strict edge-computing constraints, this paper proposes LADE-YOLO, a lightweight instance segmentation network. Specifically, we reconstruct the backbone utilizing MobileNetV3 to substantially alleviate computational redundancy. To mitigate mask aliasing in lightweight feature maps, we introduce bilinear interpolation for optimized upsampling, remarkably recovering segmentation precision. Furthermore, we design the C2f_CA module by embedding Coordinate Attention (CA) into the feature fusion neck, enhancing the localization of key dynamic targets. Evaluated on the full COCO dataset, LADEYOLO reduces parameters by 10.0% and GFLOPs by 14.0% compared to the baseline. Extensive edge deployments demonstrate an inference speedup of 16.0% while maintaining highly competitive accuracy on proxy dynamic classes (“Person” and “Car”), providing a highly efficient perceptual front-end for robust dynamic SLAM systems.

title: Irrigation-Driven Vineyard Resilience to Climate Variability: A Sentinel-2-Based Preliminary Approach
authors: Alessia Cogato, Alessandro Zanchin, Marco Sozzi, Lucia Bortolini, Francesco Marinello
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Understanding the drivers of interannual variability in vineyard spectral response is critical for optimising precision viticulture practices under changing climatic conditions. Precision irrigation relies on models based on the interaction between weather conditions and crop responses. This study aimed to evaluate how interannual weather variability influences spectral stability in vineyards and whether irrigation management can mitigate these effects. Using six years (2017–2022) of Sentinel-2 multispectral data and meteorological records from the Veneto Region, we assessed Normalised Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) and Normalised Difference Red Edge Index (NDRE) values across vineyards with different irrigation strategies and across years. Regression analysis revealed that NDVI and NDRE were most sensitive to thermal accumulation and rainfall patterns during July and August as well as the annual global radiation, particularly in drip-irrigated vineyards. In contrast, vineyards irrigated with sprinkler systems exhibited greater spectral stability. Analysis of variance confirmed that differences in water application volumes significantly influenced interannual spectral variability. These findings highlight the critical role of irrigation strategy in mitigating the effects of weather variability and support the integration of remote sensing and meteorological data for adaptive vineyard management.

title: Integration of Power Electronics in Hydroponic Chamber
authors: A. Alice Hepzibah, Lohith S, Lokesh P
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Hydroponic farming has gained significant importance as a sustainable agricultural technique that enables soil-less cultivation in controlled indoor environments. With increasing urbanization and decreasing arable land, controlled environment agriculture provides a practical solution for consistent crop production. Hydroponic chambers rely on artificial lighting, nutrient circulation pumps, and environmental monitoring systems to maintain optimal plant growth conditions. However, maintaining stable lighting intensity, nutrient flow, temperature, and humidity require reliable and efficient power management. Conventional hydroponic systems often use basic electrical connections without proper voltage regulation or energy optimization. This results in power fluctuations, inefficient energy consumption, overheating of components, and reduced lifespan of lighting and pump systems. Existing setups lack integrated control mechanisms, leading to inconsistent performance and higher operational costs. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes the integration of power electronics into the hydroponic chamber. The proposed system incorporates regulated AC-DC conversion, DC-DC converters, PWMcontrolled LED drivers, and sensor-based feedback control. This approach ensures stable voltage supply, improved energy efficiency, and automated environmental regulation. By implementing intelligent power management and closed-loop control, the system enhances reliability, reduces energy losses, and provides consistent growth conditions, thereby improving overall plant productivity.

title: IoT-Enabled Precision Public Distribution System with Servo-Controlled Commodity Dispensing and Real-Time Transparency
authors: Vimalnath S, Arul Kumaran A, Dhanush P, Gowtham M, Anguraj Karthick V
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Public Distribution Systems (PDS) in the economically disadvantaged areas are still struggling with such problems as manual errors, diversion of resources, late reporting, and accountability. The given paper describes an IoT-enabled Smart Public Distribution System that is aimed at improving the transparency, accuracy and efficiency of functioning. The suggested architecture involves RFID-based authentication of beneficiaries, precision dispensing by the use of servos and cloud monitoring in real-time. RFID identification will guarantee security of entitlement enforcement and avoid duplication and unpermitted access. Robotic-servo-controlled dispensing, it is a method of precisely dispensing staple products like rice, wheat, sugar and minimizing the use of human resources and wastage of materials. The IoT connectivity will enable a constant monitoring of inventory, transactional data, and the performance of the system whereas an LCD-based interface will give instant feedback to beneficiaries and administrators. The experimental assessment shows that the dispensing errors and the transaction time have reduced significantly relative to the traditional manual PDS operation. The system is able to scale in a low cost, better governance and tamper proof record management. The suggested framework presents an effective and energy-saving route to update the food distribution infrastructure and especially the rural and resource-limited conditions.

title: 68.22 TOPS/W Full Boolean Logic Supportable eDRAM-Based Digital CIM with Energy-Efficient Input-Aware Toggle-Rate-Reduced Adder
authors: Ik-Hyeon Jeon, Ho-Sung Lee, Eun-Bi Koh, Junsung Park, Joo-Hyung Chae
abstract: We present an eDRAM-based digital compute-in-memory architecture achieving 68.22 TOPS/W that supports full Boolean logic while enabling energy-efficient computations. This design utilizes a row-sharing full Boolean logic input driver to minimize the area, allowing for Boolean functions and multiply-and-accumulate operations, thereby yielding a multi-computation figure-of-merit (FoM) of 27.1. For energy efficiency in high toggle-rate input activation environments, we implement an input-aware toggle-rate-reduced adder, attaining an efficiency FoM of 4366.08.

title: A 5nm 91.43 TOPS/W 4-Chiplet Generalizable-Rendering-Processor with UCIe-Enabled Cross-Die-Cache and Balance-Aware Progressive Multi-Level Sparsity
authors: Yonghao Tan, Songchen Ma, Pingcheng Dong, Peng Luo, Zhiyuan Lei, Wencai Lu, Guangxi Ying, Man-To Yung, Haibo Zhao, Lan Liu, Yuzhong Jiao, Xuejiao Liu, Yu Liu, Li Li, Luhong Liang, Mao Liu, Kwang-Ting Cheng
abstract: This paper presents a multi-chiplet generalizable rendering processor. Key innovations include: 1) a UCIe-enabled cross-chiplet interconnect combined with dynamic sample grouping and projection-area aggregation to form a unified source view cache, reducing external memory access by 95.78%; 2) a balance-aware hierarchical sparsity pipeline that integrates coarse ray skipping, adaptive source view pruning, and tile-level fine-stage sharing, minimizing computation burden by 4.69×; and 3) a progressive-resolution dataflow with selective patch-grained super-resolution, further achieving an 8.97× improvement in energy-delay product. The fabricated 5nm 4-chiplet processor achieves 91.43 TOPS/W energy efficiency, 0.29 μJ/pixel rendering energy, and real-time performance at 55.43 FPS. It outperforms the prior SOTA in energy efficiency by 3.39× for multi-chiplet processors and in rendering energy by 3.45× for generalizable NeRF accelerators.

title:
A 1.6-to-16 GHz Sub-1-LSB INL
                    <sub>pp</sub>
                    7-bit Phase Interpolator Using Constant-Load Unit with Trimming-Free Digital Calibration in 28-nm CMOS
authors: Xianting Su, Xiaoteng Zhao, Zekai Yang, Zhicheng Dong, Miao Zhang, Jie Liu, Hongyu Su, Bowen Wang, Shubin Liu, Zhangming Zhu
abstract: This paper presents a 7-bit current-mode phase interpolator (PI) characterized by high linearity, wide operation range, low jitter, and compact area. The proposed PI utilizes constant-load units (CLUs) in synergy with a trimming-free digital calibration, suppressing the nonlinearity from circuit non-idealities and systematic interpolation error in both analog and digital domains. Thanks to the balanced topology of the CLU, the input load variation is 13.5× less than conventional unit and thus achieves a more than 7× input amplitude variation reduction. Fabricated in 28-nm CMOS technology, the prototype achieves a sub-1-LSB peak-to-peak integral nonlinearity (INL<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">pp</inf>) over 10:1 relative bandwidth (1.6~16GHz) with an area of 3490 μm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. When operating at 8 GHz, the proposed PI achieves an INL<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">pp</inf> of 0.79 LSB, a peak-to-peak differential nonlinearity (DNL<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">pp</inf>) of 0.95 LSB, and an integrated jitter of 34.55 fs<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rms</inf> from 10 kHz to 1 GHz, while consuming 5.83 mW power.

title: A Fully Integrated SPAD Sensor with Photoluminescent Encapsulation and Die-Bonded μLEDs for Implantable Oxygen Monitoring
authors: Julian A. Singer, Anton Geläschus, Ute Schmidt, Andreas Bahr, Matthias Kuhl, Qiang Li
abstract: Continuous monitoring of oxygen concentration in biological tissue is critical for adaptive therapies and long-term implant diagnostics. This work presents a heterogeneously integrated photoluminescent oxygen sensing system combining CMOS single-photon avalanche diodes (SPADS) with pulse- and current-controllable micrometer-sized LEDs (μLEDs) die-bonded onto the chip. A single-step encapsulation using an optically active polymer integrates both the sensing and protecting layers. The system achieves a concentration resolution of 0.03 to 0.5 % O2 with an energy consumption of 73.4 nJ per measurement at up to 6.6 ksps, outperforming state-of-the-art systems by 3x in accuracy, 20x in sampling rate, and requiring only one-third of the power.

title: Benefits of a ComSoc Membership
authors: N/A

title: IEEE Women in Engineering
authors: N/A

title: ROM-LTE: A 28nm 16.1TOPS/W ROM-Based LUT Tensor Engine with Complementary ROM and Switching-Suppressed Latch-Based PE for Fine-Tuning-Free ML Inference
authors: Sangsu Jeong, Jeongkyu Park, Dongsuk Jeon
abstract: This paper presents a ROM-based LUT tensor engine that supports an output-stationary dataflow with an area-efficient complementary ROM structure, fully exploiting unstructured sparsity for fine-tuning-free ML inference. A latch-based PE efficiently exploits irregular zero patterns while avoiding redundant switching, minimizing energy for both dense and sparse inputs. The design achieves peak energy efficiency of 16.1 TOPS/W, outperforming prior LUT-based CIM macros and delivering 1.99× improvements compared to a state-of-the-art tensor engine.

title: A Hybrid Intelligence Framework for Systematic Symptom Reasoning and Precision-Oriented Diagnostic Inference
authors: M. Gowri, G. Hepsiba, G. Pavani Durga, R.S. Amshavalli, B. Gracelin Sheena
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Predictive maintenance in Industry 4.0 environments requires scalable and privacy-preserving learning mechanisms capable of handling heterogeneous industrial data. Conventional centralized approaches suffer from data privacy risks, limited cross-site generalization, and communication constraints. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a Digital Twin-Assisted Federated Learning (DT-FL) framework for distributed predictive maintenance. The proposed method integrates digital twin-based degradation modelling with federated learning to enable collaborative model training across multiple industrial nodes without sharing raw sensor data. Each local node employs a digital twin to estimate degradation states and enrich feature representations before federated aggregation. The global model is constructed using federated averaging, enabling cross-site knowledge sharing while preserving data confidentiality. The framework is evaluated on the NASA CMAPSS turbofan engine dataset for both Remaining Useful Life (RUL) estimation and fault classification. Experimental results demonstrate superior performance compared to traditional machine learning and deep learning baselines, achieving 94.8% classification accuracy and reduced RUL prediction errors (MAE of 6.3 hours and RMSE of 9.5 hours). The results confirm that integrating digital twin modelling with federated learning improves prediction accuracy, scalability, and privacy preservation, making the framework suitable for nextgeneration intelligent maintenance systems

title: A Framework Integrating Agile Software Development Principles for Co-Design and Participatory Cost-Benefit Evaluation in Digital Agriculture
authors: Fabio Lepore, Daniele Vergamini, Livia Ortolani, Chiara Mannari, Alessio Ferrari, Manlio Bacco, Gianluca Brunori
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Digital innovation in agriculture often struggles to meet real operational needs due to limited stakeholder involvement and insufficient assessment of context-specific costs and benefits. To bridge this gap this paper introduces AGILE-CBA, a methodological framework that (a) integrates co-design practices, (b) is structured through a Scrum Agile development process, and (c) includes a participatory cost-benefit evaluation. The framework organises co-design into a seven-step iterative cycle, embedding a five-step participatory assessment loop within each sprint. This dual structure enables the continuous and situated evaluation of both expected and observed costs and benefits, encompassing tangible and intangible aspects. By aligning key Scrum practices, such as backlog management, sprint reviews and retrospectives, with facilitated dialogue and collective reflection, AGILE-CBA can support more informed prioritisation, enhances context relevance, and reduces adoption risks. Facilitators play a crucial role in mediating communication and adjusting the pace and content of participatory activities to seasonal workloads and user capabilities. The approach is particularly suited to farming systems characterised by variability, environmental and seasonal dependency, and multi-actor complexity, offering a flexible and replicable pathway toward more inclusive, context-aware, and sustainable digital agriculture.

title: Modulated red/blue Balance from LED Lighting Affects Growth and Quality Parameters in Greenhouse Grown Crops
authors: Karl-Johan Bergstrand, Geni C. Zanol, Jorge Solis, David Olsson, Magnus Nilsson
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Light-emitting diode (LED) based greenhouse lighting systems are energetically more efficient than high intensity discharge lamp systems and are therefore currently replacing those in greenhouses all over the world. However, another interesting feature of LED based systems is the possibility to modify the spectral composition of the light and, thus affect light-dependent physiological reactions in the plants such as photosynthetic activity, floral initiation, and stem elongation. In the present study, the impact on the red/blue balance on growth and development of the horticultural crop Pak Choi was evaluated under greenhouse conditions. The red/blue balance was either R:B 86:14 or 98:2. The fresh- and dry weight was the highest for the treatment with the lower proportion of blue light. Chlorophyll Content Index was higher for the treatment with high proportion of blue light. For the photosynthesis, measured as the yield of photosystem II (F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">v</inf>/F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf>), there were no significant differences between treatments in the present study. Also for plant height, no significant differences were present. It was concluded that increasing the proportion of blue light from 2% to 14% will generally decrease fresh mass production, but not significantly reduce stem elongation.

title:
An H-Band CMOS Frequency Doubler with 6.01dBm Single-Element P
                    <sub>out</sub>
                    and 1.49%-DC-to-RF-Efficiency Using Dual-Harmonic Continuous-Mode Tuning Technique
authors: Weiping Wu, Yanjun Ma, Xun Bao, Lei Zhang, Aoyang Zhang
abstract: This paper presents an H-band frequency doubler in 65-nm CMOS technology with an f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">max</inf> of 250 GHz. It consists of a multi-stage driver amplifier and a push-push frequency doubler (PPFD) with the proposed dual-harmonic continuous-mode tuning technique. The proposed PPFD enables fundamental and second-harmonic tuning in both differential- and common-modes along the feedforward and feedback paths. Firstly, it reuses the differential-mode fundamental current and suppresses the feedback of the common-mode second harmonic of the PPFD simultaneously, thus improving the conversion gain and efficiency in the band approaching or beyond f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">max</inf>. Secondly, the proposed technique minimizes the common-mode fundamental signal feedthrough, resulting in an improved HRR. As a proof of concept, the prototype frequency doubler achieves an output power of 6.01 dBm in single-element and a DC-to-RF efficiency of 1.49% with a 3-dB bandwidth of 220-to-276 GHz. Moreover, the fundamental harmonic rejection ratio is better than 65.2 dBc across the entire band.

title: A Unified Simulation and Hardware Framework for Path Planning in Wheeled Mobile Robots with 2D Augmented Reality-Based Sensing
authors: Meer Shadman Saeed, Pritam Pun, Subhas Chandra Mukhopadhyay, Chris R. Reid
abstract: This paper presents an interactive robotic test platform simulator for developing and evaluating path planning algorithms in wheeled mobile robots. The framework operates in three modes-simulation, predefined waypoint testing, and real-time execution-providing flexibility for both research and education. In simulation mode, the platform models differential drive and mecanum robots with modular forward and inverse kinematics, enabling customizable dimensions and velocity inputs. Waypoint testing mode supports repeatable path evaluation for benchmarking algorithms. In real-time mode, an overhead camera with ArUco marker-based pose estimation maps robot motion into a 2 D environment, using camera calibration and homography to convert frame waypoints into real-world coordinates. A key feature of the system is its 2D augmented reality capability, which enables the creation of virtual obstacles and sensor fields that interact with the robot as if they existed physically, enhancing experimentation without additional hardware. Waypoints can be generated algorithmically or interactively and transmitted as wheel velocity commands to the robot. By combining simulation, structured testing, augmented reality, and hardware integration, the platform bridges the gap between theoretical algorithm design and practical implementation.

title: Combating Employment Scams to Safeguard Jobseekers from Fraudulent Postings Using an Interactive AI Chatbot Framework
authors: Mohebbanaaz, R Himabindu, Madhusudhana Reddy Barusu, G Vishnu Vardhan Rao, M Jyothirmai, B Geetharani
abstract: Although the rise of internet job boards has led to more job openings, it has also made job searchers more vulnerable to fraud and false advertising. These scams can lead to financial loss, identity theft, and decreased trust in digital recruitment channels. This study presents an interactive AI chatbot framework designed to protect jobseekers by identifying suspicious job listings and providing real-time guidance. Utilizing natural language processing and Artificial Intelligence (AI) learning techniques, the chatbot evaluates job postings for common red flags such as upfront fee requests, unverifiable companies, and phishing attempts. Through simulated interactions and pilot testing, the system demonstrates its ability to educate users on safe job-seeking practices while flagging potential scams before users engage. By combining automated detection with a conversational interface, the framework empowers jobseekers to make informed decisions and mitigates exposure to fraudulent opportunities. This approach highlights the potential of AI-driven tools to enhance cybersecurity awareness within recruitment systems and offers a scalable solution for fostering safer online job search experiences.

title: Energy and Optical Assessment of Glazing Systems Equipped with Solid-Solid Phase Change Materials in Cold Climates
authors: Hossein Arasteh, Wahid Maref, Hamed H. Saber
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: This research explores the thermal and optical behavior system of a double-glazed window (DGW) integrated with a Solid-Solid Phase Change Material (SSPCM) layer, evaluated under the climate conditions of Montreal, Quebec, Canada. Transient numerical simulations are performed for representative extreme days for both the hottest and coldest days of the year under clear and overcast sky. A parametric study is carried out to assess how SSPCM behavior and window orientation (North, East, South, and West) affect the energy and daylighting performance of the system. The simulations account for Natural Convection (NC) within the air gap to model buoyancy-induced airflow and compare to none NC. Findings reveal that ignoring NC in winter can lead to an overestimation of heat transfer by approximately 12% to 19%. In contrast, the influence of NC in summer is negligible, enabling its exclusion to significantly reduce computational time without loss of result accuracy. In summer, the SSPCM undergoes full phase transitions in all orientations, remaining transparent throughout standard working hours; however, this optical shift does not translate into notable thermal improvement. On the other hand, Winter results show that the latent heat storage capacity of the SSPCM effectively limits indoor heat loss, leading to measurable energy savings. Among all orientations, the south-facing configuration achieves the highest performance, with an 8.2% reduction in heating energy demand and the longest period of visual transparency. The most effective solution identified is a south-facing DGW design with a 15 °C transition temperature SSPCM layer installed on the interior surface. This configuration offers a strong balance between improved thermal efficiency during the heating season and sustained daylight availability, making it a compelling choice for energy-conscious commercial buildings aiming to optimize year-round comfort and performance.

title: μxer: Cross-Domain Split Mixer-Based Deep Learning Approach for Healthcare and Eco-Health
authors: Diogen Babuc, Teodor-Florin Fortiş
publish: 2025 24th International Symposium on Parallel and Distributed Computing (ISPDC)
abstract: This paper presents $\mu$ xer, a deep learning architecture designed for cross-domain image classification in both healthcare and eco-health applications. The model leverages split mixer blocks and a hybrid SiReLU activation function to achieve efficient spatial and channel-wise feature extraction. This will not affect the maintenance of computational efficiency. The $\mu$ xer is tailored for edge AI deployment and addresses critical challenges such as domain shifts, limited labeled data, and inference constraints. It integrates multitask learning and CycleGAN-based domain adaptation. This enables a robust generalization across heterogeneous datasets without extensive fine-tuning. Experiments conducted on five binary classification tasks (malaria detection, plant disease recognition, biomedical textures, colorectal polyps, and cervical cells) demonstrate $\mu$ xer’s superior performance compared to ZF Net, ViT-base-patch16, and MobileNet in terms of accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and latency. The results confirm that $\mu$ xer delivers high diagnostic precision with low resource consumption. This makes it a scalable solution for real-time medical and environmental monitoring on low-power devices.

title: Performance Comparison of MPI Collectives in Various Offloading Modes
authors: Daniel Coleridge, Ehab Saleh, Amir Raoofy, Josef Weidendorfer
publish: 2025 24th International Symposium on Parallel and Distributed Computing (ISPDC)
abstract: Considering the increasing need for high degrees of parallelism through latency footprint reductions of message passing interface (MPI) collective backends, recent offloading techniques aim to schedule and partially offload communication steps. These offloads leverage the flexible and highly programmable SmartNICs to execute communication portions. This work examines and compares performance across various MPI-defined collectives using rendezvous tag-matching acceleration and SmartNIC collective offloading. The comparison is considered from several setups using SmartNIC-based collective offloads through Unified Communication X (UCX) and Unified Collective Communication (UCC) extensions. The offloads are evaluated through synthetic micro-benchmarks and real numerical applications. The comparison reveals insights into the strengths and weaknesses and underscores the scalability and behavior of a well-defined set of collective offload modes. This work discusses the latency behavior exhibited across several collective operations, focusing on SmartNIC offloading on a 4 -node setup. The offloaded MPI_Alltoall collective through Nvidia’s Data Processing Unit (DPU) SmartNIC has shown more scalability and is consistently outperforms the tag-matching acceleration variant by approximately 40%. Furthermore, the MPI_Scatter collective highlighted a remarkable latency advantage by DPUoffloaded UCC compared to the other offloading modes. Although the discrepancy is substantial, it is later revealed that it does not hold in a non-blocking context.

title: Computational Fluid Dynamics Modeling for Thermal Efficiency Enhancement in Parabolic Trough Collectors for Solar Stills
authors: Mammar Bouhelal, Amar Rouag, Abdelhamid Bouhelal, Yousef Belloufi
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: The thermal efficiency of parabolic trough collectors (PTCs) is influenced by several design and operational parameters, including length, diameter, and mass flow rate. In this study, two-dimensional steady-state Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) simulations are employed to investigate the heat transfer mechanisms within PTCs, with the aim of enhancing their overall performance. Different collector configurations are assessed, with lengths varying between 0.5 and 3 m and diameters ranging from 10 to 60 mm, based on prior research dedicated to improving solar still systems. The analysis considers both laminar and turbulent flow regimes, emphasizing the effects of Reynolds number and mass flow rate. Findings reveal that the collector diameter exerts a greater influence on thermal efficiency than the mass flow rate, while the length has only a minor effect. Specifically, increasing the diameter by 50% leads to an improvement in efficiency exceeding 60% under both laminar and turbulent conditions. Conversely, reducing the mass flow rate by 60% enhances efficiency by approximately 50% to 60% in both flow regimes. These results suggest that optimizing PTC design requires prioritizing smaller diameters and reduced mass flow rates, with length playing a secondary role, while also tailoring the configuration to the requirements of the intended application.

title: AI-Driven MRI Classification of Alzheimer's Disease: A Transparent Pipeline with Systematic Preprocessing and Hybrid Model
authors: Subhieh El-Salhi, Mohammad Aljaidi, Amal Issam Alalami, Nadera Aljawabrah, Khaled Moneef Jebril, Shrouq Amer Salameh
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is chronically progressive neurodegenerative condition associated with substantial diagnostic and therapeutic challenges, particularly in its early stages. Current clinical approaches relying on MRI scans often face variability in image quality and subjective interpretation, limiting early and accurate detection. This study proposes a transparent, AI-driven diagnostic framework that integrates systematic MRI preprocessing with state-of-the-art hybrid deep learning models and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI). We compare preprocessing techniques, including contrastlimited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE) and Gaussian blur, and evaluate three model families: convolutional neural networks (CNNs), region-based CNNs (RCNNs), and hybrid models combining VGG16 feature extraction with XGBoost classification. Experiments on two benchmark datasets-the imbalanced AD dataset and Alzheimer's disease national initiative (ADNI) dataset-highlight the impact of preprocessing and model architecture on classification accuracy. Our findings reveal RCNN paired with CLAHE at a 16x16 tile size achieves superior performance on the challenging imbalanced dataset, while all models perform near-perfectly on the balanced ADNI dataset. The integration of Grad-CAM and SHAP enhances model interpretability by linking predictions to known clinical markers. This study underscores the critical role of data preprocessing, hybrid modeling, and explainability in developing reliable AI tools for Alzheimer's diagnosis, while emphasizing the need for further validation on diverse real-world data. [1], [2].

title: A Multimodal Adaptive Path Optimization Algorithm for Path Selection
authors: Jingwen Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP) is a classic NP-hard combinatorial optimization problem with important practical significance in transportation, logistics and intelligent path planning. This paper proposes a Multimodal Adaptive Path Optimization (MAPO) algorithm to solve TSP, which constructs a two-stage optimization framework by combining multimodal heuristic search strategies with deep reinforcement learning to achieve global path exploration and local precise refinement. Simulation experiments on 500 and 1000-city TSP instances show that MAPO generates shorter paths than traditional GA and PSO algorithms, effectively avoids local optima and has better optimization performance. This research enriches the solution methods of large-scale dynamic TSP, and promotes the development of intelligent path planning technology and the application of multi-algorithm fusion in combinatorial optimization.

title: GW-SegNet: A Real-Time Semantic Segmentation Network for Autonomous Driving Integrating Frequency Perception and Geometric Priors
authors: Liheng Gao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Real-time semantic segmentation is pivotal for autonomous driving safety but faces a persistent trade-off between parsing accuracy and computational efficiency. Existing high-precision models are computationally prohibitive for edge deployment, while lightweight networks often compromise structural details and lack 3D spatial awareness. Here, we present GW-SegNet, a lightweight architecture that harmonizes frequency-domain perception with geometric priors. By integrating a Wavelet Convolution (WTConv) module, we achieve an exponential expansion of receptive fields with minimal parameter growth. Furthermore, our Geometric Self-Attention (GSA) mechanism leverages monocular depth cues to resolve semantic ambiguities in complex urban scenarios. Empirical results on the Cityscapes benchmark demonstrate that GW-SegNet achieves a state-of-the-art <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$77.51 \% \text{mIoU}$</tex> with only 2.53 million parameters. Crucially, it sustains an inference speed of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 1}$</tex> frames per second on resource-constrained NVIDIA Jetson Nano devices. These findings validate that synergizing spectral analysis with geometric constraints offers a robust, efficient solution for high-fidelity environmental perception on low-power embedded hardware.

title: Few-Shot Transfer Learning for Fault Detection and Classification in Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: B Indran, V T Kaushikan, B Victoria Jancee
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) work under dynamic resource-limited conditions where it is often not possible to obtain large labeled datasets to represent all of the interesting categories of faults emerging. The current paper suggests a new few-shot transfer learning-based classification model that would facilitate users to identify rare and invisible failures with few samples. The system incorporates four hybrid learning models, namely, Adaptive Meta-Transfer Graph Attention Network, Hierarchical Few-Shots Temporal Convolutional Transformer, Contrastive Prototype Alignment Network and Self-Adaptive Domain-Invariant Meta Learner to represent spatial dependencies, temporal behavior, class discriminability, and domain-invariant features. The framework, through learning generalized network behavior by learning base classes and optimization through few-shot training per episode, exhibits an improvement of convergence and generalization. The experiments done on NSL-KDD data show that they have considerably improved in comparison to the traditional baselines, which guarantee a reliable real-time fault detection when using heterogeneous WSN conditions.

title: Design of a Mobile Intelligent Data Acquisition and Monitoring System based on Web Network Architecture
authors: Shuai Wang, Renjie Liu, Qingfang Zhang, Jiayue Fang, Yufan Zhao, Zixiang Zhao, Mingyang Wang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: In response to the current issues of low informatization and intelligence levels in experimental data acquisition and monitoring systems. A mobile intelligent data acquisition and monitoring system based on Web network architecture was designed in this paper. The system was integrated with large language models, and a workflow from template customization to data transmission was established. Meanwhile, frontend technologies and backend frameworks were utilized, enabling the implementation of intelligent parsing, automatic classification, anomaly detection, and trend prediction for experimental data. Mobile data collection, real-time monitoring, and automatic report generation were enabled, thereby enhancing the efficiency and accuracy of data collection. A comprehensive technical solution for the intelligent transformation of experimental data management was presented in this study. Both theoretical value and practical application prospects were demonstrated by the research findings.

title: Flexible Load Self-Optimizing Voltage Regulation Method for Distribution Network Substation Area
authors: Dawei Liu, Yu Lu, Senlin Lan, Jiejie Dai, Jie Fu, Qi Mei
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: To address the challenges posed by the high proportion of renewable energy integration to the frequency and voltage stability of urban power grids, this paper investigates the collaborative optimization control problem of massive flexible loads participating in the dynamic restoration of the power grid. A multi-objective self-optimizing control method for massive flexible loads is proposed to address the limitations of traditional generation side regulation capabilities and existing load regulation methods in dealing with complex power grid structures and multi-dimensional coordination of source grid loads. By analyzing the impact of load power transmission on the operation of the power grid, combined with the topology and operational constraints of the power grid, an optimization model considering frequency and voltage dynamic balance recovery was established. This method comprehensively considers the dynamic response characteristics of source grid load, with the goal of suppressing voltage and frequency deviations during the control process, effectively improving the dynamic recovery capability of the power grid. The simulation results show that the proposed strategy can coordinate and optimize load resources, significantly enhance the resilience and stability of urban power grids to cope with power disturbances, and provide a theoretical basis and methodological support for building a safe and efficient new urban power grid.

title: MPC-Based Cooperative Search Path Planning for Cross-Domain Heterogeneous Unmanned Swarms
authors: JunHao Hu, Bin Zhao, RuiMin Jiang, YiCheng Su
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Cross-domain air-sea unmanned swarms exhibit significant potential for underwater target detection applications. Focusing on the cooperative search for stationary underwater targets, this paper proposes a receding-horizon decision optimization method that enables cross-domain collaborative path planning for multiple unmanned systems under heterogeneous maneuverability constraints. Specifically, a grid-based environmental model incorporating prior target information is constructed to formulate an objective function tailored to heterogeneous swarm cooperative search. Furthermore, a distributed model predictive control framework is integrated with a genetic algorithm to develop an online grid-jumping decision-making strategy that satisfies the maneuverability constraints of cross-domain unmanned swarms, thereby achieving receding-horizon optimization and dynamic path updating. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared with a baseline method restricted to adjacent-grid movements, the proposed approach reduces global uncertainty by at least 8.71% and reduces the estimated probability of target existence by at least 25%.

title: Research on Comprehensive Evaluation and Fault Diagnosis Methods for GIS Insulation Status Based on Gas Detection
authors: Shaoming Pan, Yi Su, Wei Zhang, Yufeng Lu, Yuan Ma
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: As a core transmission and transformation device in power grids, the operational status of GIS directly impacts the safe and stable operation of power systems. Addressing the technical challenges in assessing GIS insulation conditions, this paper proposes a comprehensive evaluation approach integrating the Extensification-Entropy Method with the Gas Ratio Method. This approach is grounded in the decomposition principles of SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf> gas. It first establishes an evaluation index system for gas composition, then uses extended analysis to build correlation functions. The entropy method is applied to determine the weighting of each index, enabling a quantitative assessment of GIS insulation status. When an insulation fault occurs in the GIS, the gas ratio method can be further integrated to identify the specific fault type. Results demonstrate that this integrated assessment method not only effectively evaluates GIS insulation status but also accurately identifies fault types, validating the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Design Method of a Simulation-Based Configuration System for Distributed Communication Devices in Rural Distribution Networks
authors: Xueshan Ni, Haitao Lan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: Rural distribution networks in high-latitude regions face severe communication challenges due to long feeder spans (exceeding 30 km), sparse load density, and frequent extreme weather events-such as blizzards with snow depths over 30 cm and ambient temperatures below −30° C. Conventional deployment strategies for distributed communication devices (e.g., distribution terminal units, DTUs), which rely on empirical rules, often result in coverage gaps or excessive capital expenditure. To address this issue, we propose a simulation-based configuration system that integrates geographic information, radio propagation physics-including snow-induced attenuation correction validated against recent field measurements [1], [2]-and multi-objective optimization. The method determines the optimal combination of fixed LoRa gateways and V2X-enabled mobile relays, where the latter are utility service vehicles equipped with multi-mode communication terminals that function as temporary data hubs during outages. The hybrid architecture explicitly balances persistent wide-area coverage (via LoRa) with on-demand emergency backup (via V2X relays). A case study on a real 42-km rural feeder in Songyuan, Jilin Province-which experienced prolonged blackouts during the January 2023 blizzard-demonstrates that the proposed approach reduces deployment costs by 22% and improves communication availability during snowstorms by 35% compared to baseline practices. The formulation extends the classical set covering model [3] by incorporating environmental constraints and stochastic risk under extreme events, in alignment with multi-objective design principles in telecommunication planning [4]. Moreover, the system is designed to be compatible with State Grid's “Digital Twin Grid” initiative, ensuring seamless data exchange with enterprise-level asset management platforms through standardized APIs. This alignment not only accelerates regulatory approval but also facilitates integration into provincialwide smart grid roadmaps.

title: A Deep Learning Ensemble Framework for Accurate Peripheral Blood Cell Classification from Microscopic Image
authors: Alaa A. Elagrody, Ahmed S. Eltrass, Hassan M. El-Kamchouchi
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Automating the classification of peripheral blood cells from microscopic smear images is a vital task in clinical hematology and diagnostic pathology. This study introduces an advanced deep learning (DL) framework that leverages transfer learning using nine pre-trained convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures, including VGG19, MobileNetV2, InceptionV3, Xception, multiple ResNet variants, and ConvNeXtTiny. The model evaluation was conducted using the publicly available Peripheral Blood Cell (PBC) dataset, which comprises over 17,000 labelled images across eight distinct cell types. To address class imbalance and enhance the model's generalization capability, a range of data augmentation techniques was employed during training. Among the individual models, ConvNeXtTiny delivered the highest mean classification accuracy at 99.68 %, with Xception and InceptionV3 following closely behind. To enhance diagnostic accuracy, a soft-voting ensemble was constructed by combining the three bestperforming models. The resulting ensemble achieved a state-of-the-art accuracy of 99.81 %, accompanied by a low standard deviation, reflecting strong consistency and robustness across test splits. It also maintained superior performance in terms of all evaluation metrics across all blood cell categories. These findings establish a new empirical benchmark for blood cell classification using DL and underscore the potential of integrating AI-driven diagnostic tools into routine clinical workflows.

title: Experimental Comparison of PI and Model Predictive Control for Level Regulation of a Nonlinear Conical Tank
authors: Otman Tawerghi, Youssef Arebi, Mohamed Zayed, Esam Maried
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Nonlinear dynamics are inherent in many industrial process control applications, making controller robustness to operating-point variations a critical design consideration. This paper investigates the control of liquid level in a nonlinear conical tank using linear control strategies. A proportional-integral (PI) controller optimized via particle swarm optimization (PSO) and a linear model predictive controller (MPC) are designed based on a linearized model obtained at a selected design operating point (DOP). Both controllers are implemented in real time and experimentally evaluated under reference tracking and disturbance rejection scenarios at the DOP and at two additional operating regions away from the design point. Experimental results show that, for small setpoint changes and disturbances, both controllers exhibit comparable performance across all operating regions. However, as the operating point deviates from the design condition or when large setpoint changes are applied, the PI controller experiences significant performance degradation due to plant-controller mismatch, whereas the MPC controller maintains consistent transient performance. In particular, the PI rise time increases from approximately 12 s near the DOP to about 162 s in Region A, while the MPC controller maintains rise times of approximately $10-12 \mathrm{~s}$ across the tested regions. These results highlight the robustness advantage of MPC for nonlinear processes when operated over a wide range of conditions, while also demonstrating that properly tuned $\mathbf{P I}$ control remains effective for local operation around a nominal point.

title: A LoRaWAN-based Landslide Early Warning System using Unsupervised AI
authors: Ushaloy Chakma, Nasrin Afsarimanseh, Charmil Alee, Ajalawit Chantaveerod, Ahmad-Aduwa Da-Oh, Md Eshrat E. Alahi
abstract: Landslides are devastating natural disasters, making robust early warning systems essential for mitigating their impact. This study details the development of a low-cost, low-power Internet of Things (IoT) system for real-time landslide monitoring. The system utilises prototype nodes, each integrating sensors for rainfall, soil moisture, geophones, temperature, and humidity. Data is transmitted via a Long-Range Wide-Area Network (LoRaWAN) to cloud storage, with a local SD card for backup. Data quality was rigorously validated using metrics such as Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE). An unsupervised machine learning model, Isolation Forest, was deployed to detect anomalies from sensor data indicative of potential landslide events. The model proved effective in identifying high-risk conditions, achieving an F1-score of 0.73 for detecting critical events. While this unsupervised approach is promising for remote areas with limited baseline data, future work will involve developing supervised models using labeled field data to further improve predictive accuracy.

title: Optimization of Intra Prediction Filter for AVS3
authors: Han Wang, Yangang Cai, Rongjie Wang
abstract: In the AVS3 reference software HPM, intra prediction filtering (IPF) is employed in inter prediction filtering (Interpf). However, since IPF was originally designed for spatial boundary pixels, its direct application to Interpf is suboptimal. To address this issue, we propose a Temporal IPF technique (Temp-IPF). Temp-IPF compensates for the lack of temporal correlation when IPF is used in inter prediction, thereby improving the performance of inter prediction filtering in AVS3. Concurrently, we have redesigned the filtering parameters of IPF. Experimental results on the AVS3 720p test sequences show that Temp-IPF and the adjusted-IPF (with the redesigned parameters) achieve average BD-rate reductions of 0.025% and 0.13%, respectively.

title: FSFVE: Few Shot Compressed Face Video Enhancement
authors: Varun Ramesh Jois, Antonella DiLillo, James Storer
abstract: Videocalling has become a popular form of communication in the world today, with many companies providing free services for it. However, there are still millions of people around the world that experience poor quality videocalls due to limitations in bandwidth. This despite, most people having the required hardware. In this paper we present a novel framework for enhancing highly compressed videocalls. We show, that with as little as 10 frames of the face, we can rapidly (in under 100 seconds) train a model to enhance that instance of the videocall. The model can be trained either prior to or during the call, enhancing the rest of the call by producing better quality video. The video conferencing application need not be modified - it can be off the shelf with our system as a layer on top that trains quickly then simply lets the video conferencing application (e.g. Zoom) run as usual, where our system intercepts and improves images before they are displayed. The model is designed to run in realtime on low-compute devices such as a typical laptop CPU. Experimentally, we show that the model significantly improves quality of compressed face video both quantitatively as well as perceptually. Code can be found at https://github.com/varun-jois/FSFVE.

title: Construction of a Deep Learning-Based Model for Sports Injury Risk Prediction and Early Intervention
authors: Xianghong Meng, Na Yu, Shuxin Mu
abstract: Predictive models for risk of injury in sport today rely on poor-quality data and provide the sport professional with limited/simply the wrong information, which hinders their ability to make informed decisions regarding injury prevention until an injury has already occurred. Temporal sequence alignment of human motion recorded by IMU sensors and visuospatial sensors was performed. The ST-GCN captures joint motion spatially (the ST-GCN) while the Bi-LSTM approaches long-term dependencies (the Bi-LSTM) and the Attention mechanism focuses on the highest probability of an individual being injured (the Attention mechanism). The final output of the model provides risk class labels for the event along with actions for the coach to take to help reduce the risk of injury based on a framework of gradient-weighted class activation mapping. The ultimate outcome of the testing of the model yielded an average accuracy of 94.12% and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> score of 0.932. The increase in target muscle group activation averaging 17.66% and joint load distribution rationality scores averaging an increase of 22.6% in a controlled experiment where intervention suggestions from the model were incorporated assures the confidence of the model for risk prediction and intervention.

title: LoRa-Based Long Range Communication System for Rural and Disaster Affected Areas
authors: Aditya Wairagade, Renuka Suryawanshi, Radha Tadas, Vikas Desai
abstract: In many natural disasters, conventional communication systems fail very quickly, causing the users in the disaster area to suffer the immediate damaging of internet lines and mobile network, and loss of contact with rescuers. These relief activities are delayed because rescuers are unable to receive timely information from victims. This motivated us to design a low cost, portable communication system that does not require any supporting infrastructure and which is based on the LoRa communication modulation technique. LoRa is a long-range, low-power radio-based system, capable of sending and receiving messages up to several kilometers away from the control center. The prototype developed is basedon an ESP32 microcontroller, an SX1278 LoRa module for sending data and receiving signals, and a GPS module for getting the current position. Theunit has a 2.8 -inch TFT touchscreen interface. A virtual keyboard lets the user create emergency messages with more flexibility than standard fixed SOS devices. The user in trouble can send a message containing their position to a search and rescue (SAR) agency, which has a compatible receiver or base station. The outdoor test results show the proposed system provides strong communications greater than 3 km with an RSSI greater than -115 dBm and high packet delivery ratio at low energy consumption. Thus, the system can be utilized in actual emergency and disaster response situations where reliable communications are required in the absence of conventional communication networks to reduce response times and improve rescue efficiency.

title: Paths toward efficient hydrogen energy production: innovative fuel cell technology
authors: Guerbazi Mohamed El Amine, Chine Adel, Khaldi Fouad, Mohammedi Abdallah, Boutaghane Ayoub, Zereg Alaeddine, Bencherif Hichem, Lebaal Nadhir
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: Amid increasing apprehensions regarding fossil fuel depletion and worsening environmental degradation, hydrogen energy has surfaced as a feasible and sustainable alternative. This article examines the utilization of hydrogen fuel cells, namely Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cells (PEMFC), within the automotive industry. PEMFC are acknowledged for their exceptional efficiency, scalability, and zero-emission performance, making them ideal for both mobile and stationary power applications. The research examines their operational principles, classifies primary fuel cell kinds, and contrasts performance attributes. The environmental advantages are assessed in conjunction with the life cycle effects of Fuel Cell Vehicles (FCV), encompassing hydrogen generation to end-of-life procedures. Despite the scientific advancement of PEMFC, numerous obstacles impede their widespread implementation, including the exorbitant expense of platinum catalysts, material deterioration, and insufficient hydrogen storage and refueling infrastructure. Confronting these problems is crucial for facilitating a sustainable hydrogen economy driven by renewable energy and for actualizing the complete potential of fuel cell vehicles as a fundamental element of decarbonized mobility.

title: DSTSFN: A Dual-Path Dynamic-Static Feature Fusion Network for Robust Epileptic Seizure Detection
authors: Zixin Yang, Xiang Li, Zhaonian Guo, Chuqi Yang, Ke Zhang, Changmiao Wang, Shixiong Chen
abstract: Epilepsy, a critical neurological disorder with sudden, recurrent seizures, severely impacts patients’ daily lives, cognitive abilities, and emotional well-being, necessitating advanced detection methods for timely interventions. We propose the Dynamic and Static Temporal-Spatial Feature Network (DSTSFNet), a dual-path network that integrates dynamic temporal variations and static spatial patterns from EEG signals, using spatio-temporal attention mechanisms to accurately capture complex seizure dynamics. The model employs Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN) to model short-term changes and Spatial-Attention with Pearson Correlation Coefficients to highlight inter-channel relationships, ensuring a thorough EEG analysis. Evaluated on the CHB-MIT dataset, comprising EEG recordings from 22 pediatric subjects at 256 Hz, DSTSFNet achieves an accuracy of 99.19%, a sensitivity of 99.30%, and a specificity of 98.97%, showing its potential for clinical diagnostics and improved patient care.

title: ICISES 2025 Content Announcement Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Ships and Electromechanical System (ICISES)

title: Prompt-Augmented BERT Pseudo-Labeling for Unsupervised Topical Text Classification
authors: Luepol Pipanmekaporn, Akara Prayote, Kanabadee Srisomboon, Wilaiporn Lee
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: Topical text classification plays a crucial role in organizing large-scale textual data based on subject matter. However, it remains challenging in the lack of labeled training data due to ambiguous topic boundaries and inconsistent textual content. This research introduces an innovative pseudo-labeling framework for topical text classification, leveraging BERT-based topic representation and similarity-based matching without manually labeled data. The approach first constructs semantic topic representation by extracting topic-related keywords from an unlabeled corpus using BERT’s masked language modeling in conjunction with prompt augmentation. These representations are then used to assign pseudo-labels to the documents through semantic similarity. We propose two matching strategies: centroid-based matching and nearest-neighbor matching. A BERT-based classifier is then trained on the pseudo-labeled data and subsequently enhanced through iterative self-training on the remaining unlabeled documents. Experimental results on four benchmark datasets, including 20Newsgroup, AGNews, DBPedia, and Yelp Review, indicate that the proposed method demonstrates robust performance, with centroid-based matching achieving an average Macro F1 score of 84.21%. The results indicate that the proposed framework substantially narrow the performance gaps between unsupervised and fully supervised classification, especially in scenarios lacked annotation data.

title: Video Face Recognition Using Video Vision Transformer (ViViT): An Efficient Pathway Towards Enhanced Security and Surveillance
authors: Sanyam Sah, Saurav Devkota, Shreya Mallik, Shruti Rouniyar, Rinku Datta Rakshit
abstract: Video face recognition is an advanced technology that is capable of verifying and identifying a person from video footage in an automated or semi-automated way. This powerful technology provides its outcomes by analyzing faces and facial features in continuous video streams captured from different angles as well as different lighting conditions. It is a challenging task due to some natural unconstrained conditions like motion blur, variable lighting, different facial expressions, occlusions and non-uniform pose. The classical models are not capable of addressing all challenging situations and fail to capture essential spatio-temporal dependencies across video frames. This paper proposes an approach that is capable of addressing different challenges of Video face recognition by using Video Vision Transformer. This approach follows a strategic transfer learning pipeline, starting with a pre-trained model for action recognition and further fine-tuning it for facial identity tasks. This framework utilizes a technique for robust face extraction using MediaPipe, intelligent frame sampling and class-balancing mechanisms for handling data imbalance. This paper demonstrates the efficacy of the proposed model on a selected subset of the YouTube Faces dataset and achieves an accuracy of 97.76 % with robustness in capturing identity-specific dynamics across video sequences. The obtained accuracy is convincing and encouraging and it validates the potential of transformer-based architectures for video-based facial recognition, thus opening a novel path towards enhanced surveillance and security.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning Framework for Intelligent BotNet Intrusion Detection in IoT Networks
authors: Khamithkar Ranjitha Bai, T. Venkata Naga Jayudu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The fast rise of Internet of Things (IoT) has also introduced its own set of security threats, in particular, due to the increased popularity of botnet-based attacks, which exploit poorly secured devices with limited resources. The traditional Intrusion detection mechanism is often not effective in the IoT because of diversity of the devices, changing traffic patterns and emerging sophisticated and zero-day botnet attacks. In order to address them, this paper shall present a clever hybrid machine learning algorithm to identify botnet intrusion in the internet of things networks. The proposed approach combines a two-track functionality of feature extraction that relies on the statistic of traffic and the deep time representations obtained via CNNLSTM network. Dimensionality reduction algorithm (Auto encoder) is employed to eliminate the redundant characteristics and decrease the intensity of the computation and an ensemble-based classifier is employed to offer robust and economical detection. Millions of experiments using benchmark data sets of IoT botnets demonstrate that the proposed framework offers a high detection rate, greater precision and recall, and very low false positive rate compared to the existing machine learning and deep learning techniques. The results prove the usefulness and practicality of the proposed hybrid model in real-time security monitoring in the IoT.

title: Design and Optimization of MEMS Variable-Area Capacitive Pressure Sensors for Improved Sensitivity and Linearity in Fingertip Tactile Sensing
authors: Rhiyan Mae Ladlad, Jarod Soriano, Walton Matthew Kuo, Ralph Maru Grande, Rafael Pangilinan, Maria Theresa De Leon
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: This paper presents the design, modeling, and optimization of MEMS-based capacitive pressure sensors utilizing three variable-area mechanisms: a movable dielectric, an asymmetrical movable plate, and a symmetrical movable plate. By utilizing changes in capacitor area instead of changing the distance between plates, these configurations, these configurations minimize the trade-off between sensitivity and linearity found in conventional diaphragm-based sensors. Analytical modeling based on solid mechanics and electrostatics, alongside finite element simulations in COMSOL Multiphysics, was used to characterize diaphragm deflection and capacitance response. Parametric analysis revealed that a symmetric movable plate with a diaphragm thickness of 0.04 mm and a pillar width of 0.1 mm yielded optimal performance, achieving sensitivities of 2.3811 fF/kPa (analytical) and 2.72805 fF/kPa (simulation), with nonlinearities of 0.0697% and below 2%, respectively. A 10×10 sensor array with specifications based on Harmon’s design criterion demonstrated consistent performance across varying pressures, validating the design’s suitability for robotic, prosthetic, and biomedical applications requiring high sensitivity, linearity, and array compatibility.

title: IEEE Computer Graphics and Applications
authors: N/A

title: An ANN-based Digital Twin for PCB Temperature Monitoring: Development and Validation
authors: Jakub Korta, Krzysztof Hołoń, Grzegorz Wyszyński
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: In recent years, digital twins have been gaining growing attention among researchers across academia and industry, as they can provide information about physical assets that is often unattainable through traditional methods, due to physical or budgetary limitations. In microelectronics, this technology can be implemented to provide information useful for control, maintenance, and optimization activities. The present paper describes an early study of a digital twin designed to forecast the temperature of a simplified prototype PCB, equipped with resistive heat sources, operating under varying electrical loads and changing forced-air cooling conditions. The prototype was intentionally designed and operated to provide a fully controllable means of mimicking the thermal behavior of a generic automotive electronic control unit. The developed digital twin was based on neural networks, specifically a long short-term memory network. It was further optimized to improve prediction accuracy and limit its size and the number of input features necessary for successful forecasting of the outputs. The trained and optimized digital twin was evaluated in a number of test scenarios, yielding a mean absolute error below 2 K across all predictions. Additionally, the same optimized network architecture required the second-smallest number of neurons in the input and hidden layers among all the investigated alternatives. The results obtained in this study pave the way for industry-ready implementation of a digital twin enabling thermal monitoring of a PCB assembly operating in the automotive environment.

title: Development of a Web-Based Document Management System for Quality Assurance Office of Earist Cavite Campus Based on ISO/IEC 25010 Software Quality Assessment
authors: Rowee M. Marfil, Apple M. Rapada, Catherine D. Dumpit, Ma. Lee De Los Reyes, Gerlyn M. Domingo, Melvin G. Eballe
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Accreditation are essential for all Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) in the Philippines. Being accredited by Accrediting Agency of Chartered Colleges and Universities in the Philippines (AACCUP) one prestigious accrediting body in the Philippines not only guarantee for quality standards observance, but also maximizing public confidence in academic services that mirrored a world class learning-environment which enhance employability of students and school credibility. The process of preparing document for accreditation involves a laborious documentation, monitoring and reporting, ensuring that all evidences of different benchmark per area has completed. Thus, these significant challenges encountered by Eulogio “Amang” Rodriguez Institute of Science and Technology (EARIST) Cavite Campus with no centralized and integrated document management system, leading to inefficiencies, serious issues in administering accreditation documents, narrowing down to tracking, filing, retrieving, and authenticating them. These concerns have made streamlining the documentation process, demanding improvements in documentation management. In order to resolve these issues, this research sought to develop a webbased document management system (DMS) specifically designed for managing academic accreditation documents for the Quality Assurance Office of EARIST Cavite. The study used a descriptivedevelopmental design using the Agile approach. Respondents were selected using a purposive sampling technique. ISO/IEC 25010 software quality assessment and Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) framework was utilized to evaluate the system using a descriptive statistics like mean and ranking. Results show that all TAM variables were rated as Strongly Agree, and ISO/IEC 25010 rated Excellent for all criteria. These results prove that the system is operational, well-accepted, and of quality software standards, and thus a useful tool for enhancing the accreditation documentation process at EARIST Cavite. The findings suggested that the system includes an automatic tracking mechanism for document preparation, thus aiding EARIST Cavite in having better practices.
