title: IoT-Integrated WCIP-Based Modeling and Machine Learning Optimization of a THz Hydrogen Sensor for Green Hydrogen Monitoring
authors: V. Kalaipoonguzhali, Azath Mubarakali, Abdulrahman Saad Alqahtani, U. Arun Kumar, Aymen Hlali
abstract: This article presents a high-sensitivity terahertz (THz) hydrogen smart sensor based on three laterally cascaded spiral resonators functionalized with graphene, black phosphorus (BP), and vanadium dioxide (VO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>). The electromagnetic behavior is rigorously modeled using an extended wave concept iterative process (WCIP), enabling efficient full-wave characterization of tunable 2-D materials without volumetric meshing. Hydrogen detection is achieved through resonance frequency shifts induced by refractive-index perturbations over concentrations spanning trace ppm levels to sub-lower-explosive-limit (LEL) regimes. The optimized architecture achieves sensitivities ranging from 8.4 to 9.03 THz/RIU while preserving high quality factors and sharp spectral features. To enable rapid parametric exploration, a gradient boosting regressor (GBR) surrogate model is developed to capture the nonlinear coupling between material conductivities and sensing performance, demonstrating strong predictive accuracy (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2} \gt 0.996$</tex-math> </inline-formula>). The proposed smart sensor is designed for seamless integration with Internet-of-Things (IoT) frameworks, enabling real-time data acquisition, intelligent processing, and scalable deployment in advanced industrial environments. This smart sensing platform supports real-time hydrogen monitoring, hydrogen storage safety, and intelligent inspection of hydrogen infrastructure. Furthermore, integration within a 6G-enabled IoT ecosystem facilitates predictive analytics, automated monitoring, and efficient infrastructure management. The proposed system contributes to sustainable infrastructure development by enabling reliable hydrogen monitoring, predictive maintenance, and enhanced industrial safety. Overall, the proposed platform establishes a scalable pathway toward next-generation THz smart sensor technologies by synergistically combining physics-based electromagnetic modeling and data-driven optimization for industrial innovation and smart infrastructure applications.

title: 1 Transistor-Dynamic Random Access Memory as Reservoir for Temporal Signal Processing_supp1-3690766.docx
authors: Yasir Bashir MD

title: 3D thermal-hydraulic modeling an in-cryostat Loss-of-Coolant Accident from a superconducting magnet
authors: Mauro Sprò, Antonio Froio, Andrea Zappatore
abstract: Accidental transients in superconducting magnet systems for tokamak fusion reactors require thorough analysis, as they can jeopardize the integrity of critical, expensive, and often irreparable components like Toroidal Field (TF) coils. The in-cryostat Loss-of-Coolant Accident (LOCA) is particularly critical among these scenarios. This event is triggered by the release of pressurized Supercritical Helium (SHe), a phenomenon observed in experimental facilities, including ITER Central Solenoid Module (CSM) testing and JT-60SA commissioning. The SHe is discharged into the vacuum cryostat, forming an underexpanded supersonic jet; this rapid expansion results in a multiphase jet comprising supercritical, vapor, and liquid phases. Assessing potential magnet damage and developing diagnostics requires detailed knowledge of the 3D evolution of pressure and temperature distributions. Therefore, a 3D Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) model is essential for capturing the complex spatial and temporal dynamics of cryostat pressurization and jet propagation. In this work, a 3D transient analysis of the in-cryostat LOCA is performed, using a simplified ITER CSM geometry. The model simulates the SHe jet propagation from the moment of rupture for several minutes, allowing sufficient time for the module to heat-up. The physical models, especially the multiphase model, are validated against a 2D benchmark problem before application to the 3D scenario. In addition, an Adaptive Mesh Refinement algorithm dynamically tracks the expected shock fronts. Particular attention is paid to the temperature distribution in the CSM, with its evolution monitored from the onset of the accident to the end of the transient.

title: Enhancing Automatic Berthing of Unmanned Surface Vessels: A Finite-Time Observer Approach with Flapped Rudder Actuation
authors: Zaiji Piao, Shuang Sun, Yingying Yu
abstract: The increasing frequency of coastal accidents has drawn growing attention to vessel maneuvering in near-shore areas. Consequently, autonomous berthing of unmanned surface vessels (USVs) has emerged as a prominent research focus, though it faces significant challenges due to low-speed navigation, shallow water, and bank effects. This paper presents an automatic berthing control method for USVs equipped with flapped rudders, utilizing a finite-time observer-based backstepping controller. The proposed approach integrates a finite-time disturbance observer to estimate and compensate for external disturbances, such as wind and waves, in real time, while the backstepping control framework ensures precise trajectory tracking and stability throughout the berthing process. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves rapidity and disturbance rejection compared with conventional control strategies. The finite-time observer enables faster convergence of estimation errors, and the backstepping controller guarantees robustness against system uncertainties. Numerical simulations under various sea states validate the effectiveness of the control scheme, confirming its superiority in achieving accurate and stable autonomous berthing for USVs. The proposed combination of a finite-time observer and backstepping sliding-mode control, together with the high maneuverability provided by the flapped rudder, effectively addresses complex environmental disturbances and system nonlinearities during automatic berthing, enabling high-precision and highly stable autonomous docking.

title: A 3D-Printed GRIN Lens Array for Hybrid Beamforming: Design and Experimental Validation
authors: Yubo Wen, Shiyao Zhu, Zhaoqi Cui, Lizhao Song, Hao Zhang, Peiyuan Qin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a 13 GHz hybrid beamforming system integrating a gradient-index (GRIN) lens array, radio-frequency (RF) switches, and a digital beamforming processor. Compared with conventional phased arrays, the proposed system significantly reduces the number of phase shifters required. For the lens array design, a beam-interleaved architecture is proposed to effectively suppress grating lobes in both linear and planar array configurations, thereby enabling robust scanning performance with low sidelobe levels (SLLs) and high aperture efficiency. To validate the proposed architecture, a hybrid beamforming system based on a 4-lens linear array was fabricated and measured. The measurement results show that the lens array achieves a realized gain of approximately 22 dBi and a 5° beamwidth. The overall system demonstrates a scanning range of ±36° with a scanning loss below 1 dB. The aperture efficiency of the scanning system is over 48%.

title: A Triangular Pyramid Geometry-Guided Hand-Eye Calibration Framework for Line Laser Cameras With Constrained Optimization_supp1-3693129.mp4
authors: Jianliang Mao
abstract: Hand-eye calibration of line laser cameras (LLCs) is a prerequisite for accurate 3D measurement and reliable welding path execution in robotic welding systems. However, calibration methods based on planar or spherical targets often exhibit fitting ambiguity, sensitivity to illumination variations, and limited robustness under industrial operating conditions. To address these limitations, this paper presents a novel hand–eye calibration framework guided by the geometry of a triangular pyramid target for LLCs. By exploiting the surface normal discontinuities of the triangular pyramid, the proposed approach induces stable and unique laser extremum features during scanning, enabling consistent feature extraction and well-posed geometric calibration constraints. Under these constraints, the hand-eye transformation is formulated as a constrained optimization problem and solved using sequential least squares quadratic programming (SLSQP), which enforces rotational orthogonality and bounded translation. Experiments on a real robotic welding system show that the proposed method achieves a calibration error of 0.648 mm based on the bootstrap 95% confidence analysis, and maintains reliable performance across both comparative and field evaluations.

title: Unilateral Upper Limb Fine Motor Imagery Recognition via Symmetric Cross-Modal Attention EEG-EMG Fusion_supp1-3680124.docx
authors: Xueliang Bao
abstract: Precise sensing and decoding of unilateral upper limb fine motor intentions are critical for developing robust brain–computer interfaces (BCIs). However, noninvasive electroencephalography (EEG) faces inherent limitations, including low spatial resolution and significant signal overlap. These constraints hinder unimodal methods from effectively decoupling complex motor features. To address these challenges, this article proposes a cross-attentional temporal convolutional network (CATA-Net), a multimodal fusion architecture combining EEG and electromyography (EMG). The model operates on an intention–execution synergistic mechanism to exploit the complementarity between central neural and peripheral muscle signals. The framework constructs modality-specific feature extractors. The EEG branch employs depthwise separable convolution with atrous spatial pyramid pooling to capture multiscale spatiotemporal dependencies. The EMG branch utilizes a dilated residual temporal convolutional network to extract dynamic muscle activation patterns. Crucially, unlike standard cross-attention frameworks that merely perform generic semantic feature exchange, the proposed symmetric cross-modal attention (SCMA) module explicitly addresses the inherent spatiotemporal misalignment caused by variables. It establishes a bidirectional channel between cortical intention and muscle execution to actively anchor EEG spatial representations to EMG temporal dynamics for mutual feature calibration. Experiments with 25 subjects covered three real movement (RM) tasks and two motor imagery (MI) tasks. CATA-Net achieved 98.3% accuracy in RM scenarios. Furthermore, in challenging MI grasping and twisting tasks, it outperformed the unimodal ATCNet baseline by 7.7% and 14.7%. Analysis confirms the effective integration of EEG spatial coding with EMG fine force control. This study provides a high-precision decoding solution offering novel perspectives on multimodal neural synergies.

title: A novel Physically Enhanced Real-Time Visual Navigation method for UAVs
authors: Zhijie Chen, Hongbo Li, Long Wang, Yong Chen, Yuxuan Yuan, Tianxing Li
publish: IEEE Journal on Miniaturization for Air and Space Systems
abstract: To address the degradation of visual navigation and positioning accuracy in UAVs under hazy weather conditions, this paper proposes a TANet dehazing model as the baseline framework. By introducing a dark channel attention mask and a dark channel consistency loss, a deep fusion framework integrating physical guidance and data-driven image restoration is constructed. Firstly, during the feature extraction stage of TANet, a dark channel attention mask is applied, transforming the traditional dark channel prior into a differentiable spatial weighting scheme. This explicitly enhances the network’s perceptual capability for regions with dense haze through feature modulation. Secondly, in the training optimization stage, a dark channel consistency loss is introduced, which imposes physical-statistical constraints to ensure the output according to the physical characteristics of haze-free images. Experimental results demonstrate that the enhanced TANet model not only preserves its original computational speed and performance but also significantly improves the quality of image restoration across varying haze scenarios. The restored images provide more reliable inputs for UAV visual navigation systems, which improves the accuracy of feature matching and pose estimation, and offers technical support for autonomous navigation in complex meteorological conditions.

title: Heat Treatment for Bi-2212 CICC-type Insert Coil
authors: Meng-Liang Zhou, Min Yu, Ming Deng, Dong-sheng Yang, Hua-Jin Zhu, Zhen-Chuang Zhang, Huan Jin, Chao Zhou, Jing-Gang Qin
abstract: A heat treatment system, which is used in combination with a stepwise heat treatment method, has been constructed at the Institute of Plasma Physics, Chinese Academy of Sciences (ASIPP) for the primary Bi-2212 (Bi2Sr2CaCu2Ox) CICC (cable-in-conduit conductors)-type high-field insert coil. The design and manufacturing of the first Bi-2212 CICC-type insert coil has been completed. Prior to the final heat treatment for the CICC-type coil, a validation experiment is required to demonstrate the effectiveness and feasibility of the system and method. The validation experiment was concluded with the implementation of a heat treatment process on the model coil and the witness samples. In the course of the validation experiment, the various parameters of the heat treatment process – including temperature, pressure and oxygen content – were subjected to meticulous monitoring by a range of measuring instruments. The temperature evolution and uniformity of the process meet the technical requirements of the heat treatment process for the Bi-2212 CICC-type coil. It is evident that the critical performance of the witness samples meets the requisite value. The results of the study demonstrate the efficacy and feasibility of the system and method of heat treatment employed in the final heat-treatment process of the first Bi-2212 CICC-type coil. Consequently, the concluding phase of the heat treatment process was initiated on the inaugural Bi-2212 CICC-type insert coil. It is evident from the findings of the experiment that the coil displays a critical current of 5.4 kA at 4.2 K and 20 T background field, thus indicating its superior capacity. The success of the heat treatment for the first Bi-2212 CICC-type coil establishes a solid foundation for the heat treatment of the future large-scale and full-size Bi-2212 cable-in-conduit conductor.

title: A Rescheduling Mechanism for Cloud Data Centers Leveraging Dynamic Fault-Tolerant Classification
authors: Bin Liang, Shaohui Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: The proliferation of cloud computing has prompted leading technology providers to establish extensive cloud data center (CDC) infrastructures. Balancing service quality and profitability is a crucial goal due to the intense competition brought about by constantly upgrading user demands. As a result, suppliers are increasingly relying on complex fault-tolerant mechanisms to ensure the reliability of CDC. The traditional rescheduling method is a commonly used approach. However, this often leads to longer completion times or increased compensation, thereby damaging reputation. We investigates operational inefficiencies attributable to virtual machine (VM) failures and failed cloud task (FCT) rescheduling in CDC. Our contribution includes three aspects. First, we present a dynamic classification rule of FCT according to the attribute for the FCT. Second, building upon this rule, we propose a rescheduling mechanism for CDC leveraging dynamic fault-tolerant classification (RMDFC) algorithm to concurrently enhance failure repair rate and minimize compensation overhead. Finally, perform performance tests on the RMDFC algorithm. The failure repair rate of RMDFC algorithm has increased by an average of 3.8 times compared to the comparative algorithms. At the same time, compensation decreased by an average of 58% and profit increased by an average of 9% for cloud service providers in CDCs. The test results show that the RMDFC algorithm exhibits excellent performance.

title: The Study of Molecularly Imprinted Polymer-Based Sensor for Sensitive Detection of Glyphosate Pesticide
authors: Juthamas Hongoeb, Subhas Chandra Mukhopadhyay, Kamonrat Phopin, Tanawut Tantimongcolwat
abstract: The excessive usage and persistent environmental presence of the hazardous pesticide, glyphosate, lead to significant contamination of the food chain and various ecosystems. Studies indicated the potency of this chemical to exert several severe life-threatening complications, raising health concerns throughout the globe. Unfortunately, conventional methods often suffer from expensive instrumentations, complicated operation, and lengthy analysis time. Therefore, this study proposed a simple, user-friendly, and cost-effective molecularly imprinted polymer (MIP)-modified electrode for sensitive glyphosate detection. Preliminary studies using electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) demonstrated the possibility of this approach as a promising alternative. The developed sensor revealed a working range of 0.005 to $5 \mu \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mL}$, achieving an excellent coefficient of determination (R 2) of 0.992 when utilizing the resistance parameter at a particular frequency of $\mathbf{5 ~ k H z}$ for analysis. The calculated limit of detection (LOD) of $0.437 \mu \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mL}$ was also obtained. As a result, this proposed MIP-modified sensor is able to be further developed into a robust method for glyphosate detection, ultimately facilitating regulatory enforcement and safeguarding human well-being.

title: Strategizing for Digitalization and Sustainability in Manufacturing: A Practice-Based Process Model
authors: Dörte Foit, Tetiana Lutsenko, Thorben Scholz
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)
abstract: Manufacturing companies increasingly face the dual imperative of digitalization and sustainability – two transformation processes that are deeply interlinked yet often pursued separately. This fragmentation can lead to inefficiencies, strategic misalignment, and untapped synergy potential. Although the need for an integrated approach to dual transformation is increasingly acknowledged, there is a notable lack of actionable, strategy-oriented process models to support organizations in managing this complexity. This paper addresses this gap by introducing the Dual Strategy Loop – a practice-oriented process model building on a systematic literature review and a conceptual Reference Model. The Dual Strategy Loop combines four strategic content dimensions (guiding principles, value creation, organizational development, design principles) with a five-phase process logic (initiation, analysis, planning, implementation, evaluation). The model integrates key practical requirements such as agility, iteration, participation, and systems thinking, enabling adaptive and goal-oriented transformation strategies. To enhance practical applicability, it is complemented by the Dual Strategy Map and a set of enabling infrastructures. By connecting theoretical insights with strategic practice, the Dual Strategy Loop provides a coherent framework to guide manufacturing firms through integrated and iterative strategy development.

title: Efficient EV Charging Station Management Using Battery SoC and BMS
authors: M. Venkatachalam, A. Faridul Arshad, S. Kovardhana, R. Anbu Thamizh, T. Logeswaran, P. Tamilarasu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The Electric Vehicle (EV) market is growing exponentially, the Electric Vehicle charging stations that already exist in the market are experiencing an inefficiency in the First Come First Served (FCFS) queuing logic. The queuing logic that is being followed is static and does not consider the State of Charge (SoC) of the Electric Vehicles. The Electric Vehicles that are completely drained of power have to wait longer for charging compared to Electric Vehicles that have enough power. This is creating a major roadblock for the efficient Electric Vehicle charging stations. A new Electric Vehicle distribution system is proposed in the research that completely changes the chronological queuing logic to the queuing logic that is based on priority. The new queuing logic that is proposed is based on the dual module system that consists of the client interface that provides the Electric Vehicles with the facility of real-time telemetry. The new queuing logic that is proposed is different from the traditional queuing logic as it treats the Electric Vehicle charging slots as a fluid system that is based on the concept of necessity.

title: Hybrid Graph Neural Network Model for Energy-efficient Route Planning in Autonomous EV Mobility Networks
authors: Sunith Babu L, K Rakesh, K Srinivasan, Shubhangi Nishikant Gurav, R. Manoj Kumar, S. Sathish
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: A major challenge in autonomous electric vehicle mobility networks is the optimisation of a renewable energy route, which is affected by varying traffic conditions, complex road layouts and constraints on battery size. The traditional shortest-path and rule-based routing techniques are not tailored enough to solve these related problems for energy efficient but short EV routes. In this paper, we propose a hybrid model for representing road networks by energy-aware graphs and inferring optimal routing decisions with spatial representation learning and physics-guided energy inductive modelling. The proposed system integrates the junction, edge and global attributes such as traffic congestion and route geometry with vehicle energy dynamics. A coherent optimization methodology enables to compose routes on-the-fly reacting to the mobility variations. Experimental results show that the proposed approach achieves superior performance, with 98.7 % accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 7 \%}$</tex> F1-score and a low RMSE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 0 4 1}$</tex>, outperforming traditional routing, reinforcement learning and independent graph-based learning models. The outcomes confirm that the incorporation of graph neural networks to ecacy-aware decision making significantly helps in perfecting optimal routes and reducing overall energy consumption. This work presents a scalable and adaptable solution for autonomous electric vehicle (EV) tracking, and supports greener intelligent transportation systems.

title: A Fast-Transient Dual-Domain Learning Digital LDO with Droop Timing Prediction and Proactive Timing-Aware Control for Adaptive Droop Mitigation
authors: Minkyu Song, Yunbeom Hwang, Taeho Lee, Jisu Kim, Jun-Eun Park
abstract: This paper presents a digital low-dropout regulator (DLDO) that employs a dual-domain learning scheme for enhanced droop mitigation and fast transient response. The proposed architecture incorporates a dual-domain learning self-adaptive compensation (DL- SAC) scheme, which performs real-time learning in both the voltage-current (V-I) and time domains. The V-I domain learning path identifies the optimal compensation current for a given droop magnitude, enabling rapid one-shot recovery, while the time-domain learning path predicts recurring droop intervals and proactively strengthens the droop detector for faster detection. A prototype was fabricated in a 28-nm CMOS process with a fully integrated 900 pF on-chip output capacitor. The prototype supports an input voltage V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">IN</inf> of 0.8 to 1.2 V. Measurement results demonstrated a voltage droop of 120 mV and a droop settling time TS of 8.6 ns for an 82 mA load step with an on-chip test load, and a 59 mV droop with an 11.1 ns droop TS under a clock distribution emulation board integrating a clock buffer array at V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">IN</inf> range of 1.1 V and V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OUT</inf> of 1.05 V. The proposed DLDO achieved droop reductions of 64 mV and 21.9 mV and TS improvements of 3.6 ns and 4.6 ns for the on-chip and emulation board condition, respectively.

title: A Low-Power 1.2 TFLOPS/W Merge-Split RVV Processor for Mixed Scalar-Vector Workloads
authors: Mattia Sinigaglia, Marco Bertuletti, Gianmarco Ottavi, Amirhossein Kiamarzi, Matteo Perotti, Giuseppe Tagliavini, Luca Benini, Davide Rossi
abstract: Near-sensor workloads mix scalar and vector tasks under strict power and area constraints, requiring flexibility that neither multi-core nor wide-vector designs guarantee. We present a runtime-reconfigurable dual-core RISC-V vector SoC (GF 22FDX) switching in 5 cycles between Split-Mode (two vectors) and Merge-Mode (wide-vector + scalar). Consuming <155mW, it achieves SoA-leading FP8 1.2TFLOPS/W (@85.64GFLOPS) on highly data-parallel tasks. Split-Mode is 1.9× faster than Merge-Mode on modest parallelism, while Merge-Mode reaches 1.46× speed-up on mixed scalar-vector workloads.

title: E2ETMPN: An End-to-End Template Matching Prediction Network for Intra Coding
authors: Qijun Wang, Shiyu Xie, Feng Gao
abstract: Intra coding plays a critical role in video compression, yet conventional directional intra prediction mainly relies on boundary pixels and fails to capture complex textures and long-range spatial correlations. Template Matching Prediction (TMP) alleviates this issue by searching similar templates in reconstructed regions without motion-vector signaling; however, its MSE/MAE-based matching and simple averaging fusion are sensitive to noise and often unreliable for complex content. This paper proposes E2ETMPN, an end-to-end template matching prediction network for intra coding. E2ETMPN jointly models template matching and multi-candidate fusion under a unified loss function. It consists of two components: (1) a CNN-based multiscale matching sub-network that searches candidate predictions from reconstructed regions at different spatial scales; and (2) a Transformer-based fusion sub-network that adaptively fuses multiple candidates with template information using attention mechanisms to generate the final prediction. Experimental results demonstrate that integrating E2ETMPN into the reference encoder achieves consistent BD-rate reductions over conventional TMP and standard intra prediction methods on standard test sequences. Notably, E2ETMPN yields larger gains on screen content and scenes with rich non-local repetitive structures, validating the effectiveness of end-to-end learning for template matching prediction in intra coding.

title: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Audio-Driven Emotional Talking-Head Generation
authors: Yewei Xiao, Wei Zeng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Current audio-driven talking-head generation methods face significant challenges in achieving emotional control, often requiring fine-tuning of the entire model, relying on emotional reference videos, or suffering from insufficient generation quality. To address this, we propose a parameterefficient fine-tuning method for emotional talking-head generation, named Emo-PEFT. Emo-PEFT introduces three innovative lightweight modules: an Emotional Portrait Adapter (EPA) for decoder feature refinement, a Hierarchical Feature Refinement Network (HFR-Net) to enhance robust emotional mapping across different identities, and a Gated Emotional Adaptation Module (GEAM) to balance emotional intensity. By incorporating these novel designs, Emo-PEFT requires training only approximately 7.54% of additional parameters to efficiently adapt a pre-trained emotion-agnostic model to emotional generation tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that, using only emotion labels, Emo-PEFT can generate talking-head videos with superior visual quality, accurate audio-visual synchronization, and high emotional fidelity, outperforming baseline methods.

title: Multi-Source Decision Support System for Cold Stress Management in Dairy Buffalo Farming Using Heterogeneous Data, IoT Sensors, and Milking Robot Information: Development and Validation
authors: Maria Teresa Verde, Francesco Bonavolontà, Annalisa Liccardo, Matteo Santinello, Roberta Matera, Gianluca Neglia
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Cold stress is an often underestimated factor affecting buffalo welfare and productivity, with direct consequences on milk yield and farm sustainability. This paper presents the development and validation of a multi-source decision support system (DSS) designed to detect and mitigate cold stress in Italian Mediterranean buffalo farming. The system integrates data from an automated milking robot and environmental sensors through an edge–cloud architecture, applying specific indices (THI<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">COLD</inf>, WHI, and daily thermal variation) to quantify risk conditions. Results from field validation showed that cold stress events significantly reduced milk production, with yield drops exceeding 40% in a substantial proportion of animals. The DSS proved effective in identifying early risk conditions and enabling timely interventions, demonstrating the potential of IoT-driven monitoring to improve both welfare and economic performance. Future developments will involve large-scale validation across different farms, integration with herd management platforms, and coupling with automated barn infrastructures to create a digital twin for resilient and sustainable livestock management.

title: Hybrid Decision Tree-ELM Model for Structural Fault Classification and Crack Severity Detection
authors: R. Ruthra, Sriram Sankar V, Vijay I T, Ganapathi K, Sivaramakrishnan G
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Traditional diagnostic procedures have poor efficiency and high costs, making it difficult to classify and predict structural defects like cracks in power systems. Power systems currently use data-driven methods for structural fault classification and fracture severity diagnosis, thanks to the emergence of big data technologies. In order to improve the precision of fault classification and prediction, this research suggests a hybrid data mining framework that combines clustering, association rules. In order to help personnel locate and characterise faults, real-time voltage data and alarm device statuses are provided. To start, the bearing vibration data is preprocessed using FFT to extract intrinsic defect signals. These signals are essential for finding structural anomalies and crack propagation. A hybrid model called DT-ELM, which stands for Decision Tree and Extreme Learning Machine, is introduced to enhance feature extraction and classification tasks. While the ELM optimises the weights of features without iterative training, the decision tree chooses features with great classification potential. According to the experimental data, the suggested DT-ELM outperforms the current methods with an accuracy rate of 96.46%. Using this approach, current power systems may perform intelligent, data-driven fault diagnostics with a scalable, efficient, and dependable solution for classifying structural faults in real-time and detecting the severity of cracks.

title: A Multi-Modal Ensemble Framework for Imbalanced Dermoscopy: Metadata Fusion and Energy-Based Routing
authors: S Sowmyadevi, B. Deepika, S. Deekshitha, M.Akash Kumar, K. CharanSaiReddy, Anandaraj B
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Automatic skin-lesion classification from dermoscopic images remains difficult because public benchmarks are strongly imbalanced, several disease categories are visually similar, and clinical metadata can be missing or inconsistent in practice. This research proposes a calibrated multimodal ensemble that combines EfficientNet and ConvNeXt image encoders with a lightweight metadata-fusion branch to improve discrimination while preserving deployment-oriented reliability. The training pipeline uses class-balanced focal loss, standard geometric and photometric augmentation, and MixUp regularization to improve minority-class sensitivity. Experiments are reported on the publicly available ISIC 2019 and HAM10000 datasets using patient-wise train/validation/test splits to reduce information leakage. A post-hoc temperaturescaling layer is used to improve probability calibration, and an energy-based routing score is used to flag uncertain or out-ofdistribution cases for abstention rather than forcing overconfident closed-set predictions. On ISIC 2019, the proposed framework achieves 91.4% accuracy and a macro AUC of 0.95 with a log loss of 0.31; on HAM10000, it achieves 93.7% accuracy and a macro AUC of 0.97 with a log loss of 0.27. The revision further clarifies reproducibility, computational complexity, recent literature baselines, metadata robustness design, and the practical implications of calibrated uncertaintyaware routing for clinical decision support.

title: Car Point Cloud Model Based on the Improved PointNet++ Algorithm
authors: Chen Wang, Chunjiang Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address the limitations of PointNet++ in processing automotive orthographic point clouds-specifically, the loss of feature details, degradation of edge precision at boundaries, and failure to capture critical contextual relationships-this paper proposes an improved PointNet++ algorithm. We conduct a comparative analysis against the MVCNN and PAConv algorithms. To mitigate the loss of local features, our improved PointNet++ algorithm integrates a KNN mechanism and incorporates bias feature extraction. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed automotive orthographic point cloud segmentation model achieves an average performance improvement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 \%}$</tex> over the standard PointNet++ model and 16 % over the PointNet model. This indicates that the improved PointNet++ algorithm effectively enhances the processing performance for automotive orthographic point cloud segmentation tasks.

title: Automatic Detection and Localization of Decapped Chips in Single-Event Effects Testing
authors: Shuyun Wen, Gang Guo, Jiancheng Liu, Yanwen Zhang, Yongle Zhao, Peng Su
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of electronic devices across numerous fields, the importance of radiation testing has become increasingly prominent. However, traditional methods for locating decapsulated chips primarily rely on manual operation, which suffer from significant issues such as low speed and limited accuracy. To address this, this paper proposes an automatic identification and localization algorithm for decapsulated chips based on multi-scale enhancement. The system consists of three core modules: image preprocessing, decapsulated chip detection, and coordinate transformation, enabling efficient automatic identification and precise localization of decapsulated chips. During training, a hybrid dataset comprising 3,786 images-formed by combining public and self-compiled datasets-was utilized. Compared to the baseline algorithm, the proposed method achieves a 2.4% improvement in Precision and a 2.1% improvement in Recall, with a particularly notable 43% increase in the recall rate for small objects. It also demonstrates certain performance advantages over other algorithms and traditional manual methods. This approach can be widely applied in various radiation testing scenarios, significantly reducing the preparation time required for localization before irradiation, thereby improving equipment utilization efficiency and providing efficient and accurate technical support for the radiation testing of electronic devices.

title: A Flexible Defect Demodulation Method Based on FPGA for High Speed Mixing-Frequency Electromagnetic Railway Inspection
authors: Jiwei Huo
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Mixing-frequency electromagnetic rail inspection has the characteristics of non-contact, fast, and no need for coupling agents, and is suitable for high-speed online rail inspection scenarios. But, this method need demodulate amplitude information of each elected frequency component from detection signal and requires a large amount of computation. To reduce computational complexity, a FPGA based flexible algorithm for rail defect demodulation is presented in this paper. The feasibility of apply this method to mixing-frequency electromagnetic railway inspection is proved through theoretical analysis and the implementation method based on FPGA is given. An experimental system is built and the effectiveness of apply the algorithm for rail defect inspection under high-speed condition is verified through experiment.

title: ACF-MATD3 Formation Control for Multi-Agent Wheeled Mobile Robot Systems
authors: Zhanfeng Hu, Junnian Gou
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Multi-agent wheeled mobile robot systems have been widely applied in tasks such as cooperative operations, environmental monitoring, and formation transportation. As such, formation control becomes an important topic to study for the area of unmanned systems. However, conventional formation control is based upon graph theory and consensus theory, requiring accurate modeling and complex mathematical analysis that restricts its flexibility in handling dynamic and high-dimensional settings. In this paper, the formation control problem is formulated as a multiagent deep reinforcement learning (MADRL) problem, and an Action Consistency-Formation Multi-Agent Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (ACF-MATD3) algorithm is developed to address formation control in an obstacle-free environment. Based on a leader-follower scheme, the algorithm introduces the action consistency loss and the formation maintenance loss to improve the coordination between agents as well as the robustness of their formations. In the square formation task, compared to the original MATD3 algorithm, the proposed approach reduces the mean squared error (MSE) by approximately 28.9 % and the standard deviation by 81.6 %. In the formation transformation task, a multi-branch input fusion architecture achieves an overall 47.4 % reduction in collision rate with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 2. 4 \%}$</tex> drop in global MSE. Additionally, when combined with Adaptive B-Spline Network (ABSN), the collision rate drops to zero while the global MSE decreases by another 20.7 %. Simulation and real-world experimental results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm outperforms existing deep reinforcement learning methods in terms of accuracy, stability, and robustness, indicating strong potential for practical engineering applications.

title: Electric Vehicle Cluster Charging Load Forecasting Based on Improved Q-Learning Prediction Algorithm
authors: Gubo Zhang, Jianyi Yang, Dingchao Qiu, Ziying Wang, Kuan Fu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The rapid increase in the number of electric vehicles has introduced new challenges for power grids in managing load fluctuations. To address this, this paper proposes a machine learning-based method for predicting the charging load of electric vehicle clusters. First, a comprehensive charging model covering disordered, scheduled, and intelligent charging scenarios is established. Second, an improved Q-learning algorithm is designed based on recurrent neural networks (RNN) and artificial neural networks (ANN), achieving precise charging load prediction through the construction of a targeted reward function. Finally, comparative experiments are conducted under three charging scenarios, demonstrating that the proposed improved Q-learning algorithm significantly outperforms both standalone ANN and RNN methods in prediction accuracy, with performance improvements reaching up to 36%, while also exhibiting strong adaptability to diverse charging scenarios.

title: 2025 18th International Conference on Sensing Technology (ICST)
authors: N/A

title: Two-Stage Progressive Anomaly Segmentation Framework for Complete Industrial Surface Defect Detection
authors: Shaojiang Yuan, Chenglizhao Chen, Shiyu Hu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: In industrial visual inspection, existing anomaly segmentation frameworks often suffer from incomplete defect detection, where large or spatially separated defect regions cannot be fully segmented as a whole. This limitation mainly stems from the widespread use of a single-stage anomaly segmentation pipeline, which directly converts anomaly scores into segmentation masks based on a predefined threshold. As a result, some anomaly regions may be partially missed or fragmented, hindering downstream processes such as automated defect cutting or manual removal that rely on complete and precise segmentation. To address this issue, we propose a two-stage progressive anomaly segmentation framework that refines coarse predictions through superpixel-guided anomaly enhancement and progressive anomaly fusion. In the first stage, a coarse anomaly heatmap is generated using an existing anomaly segmentation model. This heatmap, along with the original image, is subjected to a superpixel extraction process to obtain localized and semantically consistent regions. In the second stage, we apply smoothing filters to the non-anomalous regions to suppress normal texture patterns and thereby highlight potential anomalies. The amplified anomaly image is then fused with the coarse segmentation result to produce a progressively enhanced and more complete segmentation result. Extensive experiments on industrial anomaly benchmarks demonstrate that our framework significantly improves segmentation completeness, especially in cases with large, fragmented, or low-contrast defect regions.

title: Proposed Smartwatch for Early Detection of Reptile Bites During Sleep: VenomAlert
authors: Khalid Aznag, Khayya El Kharrachi, Ahmed El Oirrak
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: In rural and desert areas, scorpion and snake bites are a real threat, particularly while you are asleep. For example, in Morocco, epidemiological data from the Moroccan Center for Poison Control and Pharmacovigilance suggests that the number of cases has increased from 1999 and 2024, an average of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 5, 0 0 0}$</tex> scorpion stings and more than 250 snakebites were recorded annually, with mortality rates particularly affecting children under the age of fifteen. In this innovation, we propose a smart device worn on the wrist before bedtime. It is equipped with biological and physical sensors that can detect sudden changes in skin temperature, increased heart rate or venom secretion. It then triggers an audible and vibrating alarm to immediately wake the sleeper. The device relies on an integrated algorithm to distinguish between normal changes and serious conditions, aiming to reduce mortality rates and delayed treatment.

title: Optimizing Crop Selection Based on Soil and Climatic Compatibility
authors: Ahmad Alshobaki, Belal Zaqaibeh
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Identifying crop types that are optimally suited to specific soil and climatic conditions remains a critical challenge in contemporary agricultural science. Recent advancements in Artificial Intelligence (AI) have significantly enhanced agricultural practices by promoting greater efficiency, sustainability, and productivity. A novel hybrid AI framework which is Identifying Crop Types compatible with Soil and Climate (ICTSC) is proposed. The ICTSC model employs a quantitative applied research methodology, integrating two robust machine learning algorithms: Random Forest, utilized for crop classification based on soil composition and nutrient profiles, and XGBoost, applied for forecasting climatic trends using historical weather data. The proposed model demonstrated a high predictive accuracy of 97.13%, underscoring its potential to deliver precise agronomic recommendations and improve the reliability of agricultural forecasting systems.

title: A Bidimensional Generalization of the Suffix Automaton
authors: David Alegre-Alarza, Ivan Erill, Armando J. Pinho, Joan Serra-Sagristà
abstract: Suffix-based data structures are widely used for pattern recognition. In one dimension, there exist suffix automata and suffix trees, whereas in two dimensions only suffix trees are known [1]. A two-dimensional suffix at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(i, j)$</tex> is the largest square submatrix starting there and may not be unique. We introduce the CDAIG, an automaton that recognizes such two-dimensional suffixes analogously to the Isuffix Tree [1] and is built by adapting [2]. We evaluated the complexity of the CDAIG by aligning 200 randomly selected 1000 bp-long coding chunks from the Escherichia coli genome with homologs from Enterobacteriaceae species identified by BLAST [3]. Each alignment was truncated to 750 bp, all differences (substitutions and gaps) were counted, and a CDAIG was built for each pair. Figure 1 shows that edge and suffix counts increase with the number of differences, capturing the rise in sequence divergence and reflecting the phylogenetic distance between E. coli and the compared species. Datasets are available at [E. coli dataset], and the full implementation at [CDAIG data structure].

title: Cover Page
authors: N/A

title: Navigating Cruise Tourism via AI-Based Social Media Analysis: A Study of Taiwanese Consumers’ Travel Decisions
authors: Wen-Yu Chen, Kuo-Chuan Wu, Yu-Chien Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: With the rise of AI-driven analytics and social media influence, understanding consumer behavior is vital for the cruise tourism industry. This study investigates how Taiwanese consumers make travel decisions by analyzing 226 Facebook reviews and 535 Google Maps posts from 2022 to 2024. Using web crawling, text mining, and expert interviews, the research identifies platform-specific focuses: Facebook content centers on cruise brands, itineraries, and onboard services, while Google Maps emphasizes infrastructure, transportation, and port facilities. Six key decision-making factors emerge—itinerary, price, service, facilities, time, and brand awareness. The findings demonstrate how AI-based social media analysis can reveal consumer preferences and support data-informed strategies in cruise tourism development.

title: IEEE Xplore Digital Library
authors: N/A

title: Tomato plants instance segmentation via numerical uniform colour space for drought stress resilience estimation
authors: Massimiliano Nitti, Angelo Cardellicchio, Francesco Cellini, Angelo Petrozza, Stephan Summerer, Filomena Carriero, Vito Renò
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Ensuring crop yields in a world with a changing climate and increasing population is of utmost importance. To achieve this goal, genotypical and phenotypical techniques can be exploited to evaluate whether specific mutations prove to be more resilient to drought stress or extreme climate phenomena. This process can be further improved by providing domain experts with automated analytics tools to assist them in the analysis of a large amount of data, therefore providing more statistical reliability and robustness. To this end, this work proposes an image processing pipeline for helping practitioners in the automatic evaluation of the response of tomato plant mutations to drought stress. The pipeline is based on an efficient implementation of a colour space conversion, aiming at reducing the dependency on illumination of the a and b components of the CIELab colour space and providing statistical uniformity, and anisotropic Gaussian mixture model clustering for improved feature extraction and analysis. The results were evaluated over a total of 388 mutations in three time instants, highlighting the effectiveness of the workflow in supporting domain experts in assessing the most resilient tomato variants.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Actuator Disk and Actuator Line Models for Wind Turbine Aeroacoustics Prediction
authors: Abdessabour Amoura, Mohammed Nadjib Hamlaoui, Abdelhamid Bouhelal, Arezki Smaili, Sofiane. Khelladi
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: Accurate prediction of wind turbine noise is essential for mitigating environmental impacts, improving public acceptance, and guiding the design of quieter turbine systems. This study aims to assess the impact of aerodynamic modeling fidelity on wind turbine noise prediction by comparing the Actuator Disk and Actuator Line approaches. The analysis is conducted on the New MEXICO rotor under uniform inflow conditions using a hybrid aeroacoustic framework. Aerodynamic simulations are performed using Large Eddy Simulation (LES) with the Smagorinsky subgrid scale model, while acoustic predictions are carried out using the Farassa 1A formulation based on the permeable Ffowcs Williams– Hawkings acoustic analogy. Results show that both actuator models reliably predict the overall noise levels. However, the Actuator Line model demonstrates superior accuracy in capturing tonal noise features, particularly the blade passing frequency.

title: An 83-F2 2T PUF Featuring Isolated Hot-Carrier Injection and Lightweight Majority-Voting-Based Strong PUF Mode
authors: Zhenzhe Chen, Kunyang Liu, Weirong Dong, Quan Cheng, Jiamin Li, Longyang Lin, Takashi Sato
abstract: This work presents a high-density two-transistor (2T) physical unclonable function (PUF) featuring isolated hot-carrier injection (HCI) stabilization and a lightweight strong PUF mode. A StrongARM-like sense amplifier is implemented for energy-efficient readout near the subthreshold region. By employing a single-bit-controlled, isolated HCI burn-in, device mismatch is selectively enhanced while avoiding stress on non-targeted transistors, thereby improving stability without cell loss. Furthermore, a compact strong PUF mode resistant to machine-learning attacks is designed through analog majority voting with simultaneous triple-wordline activation, effectively expanding the challenge–response space. Fabricated in 180-nm CMOS, the weak PUF mode achieves an 83 F2/bit density, 17.3 fJ/bit energy at 0.6 V, and 113 Mbps throughput at 0.8 V. A 15-minute HCI burn-in reduces the bit error rate to nearly zero across −20 °C to 100 °C. Both weak and strong modes pass the NIST SP 800-22 and other statistical tests, while the strong mode remains resilient against machine-learning attacks trained on up to 30M CRPs. These results demonstrate the proposed 2T-PUF’s suitability as a hardware root of trust for energy- and area-constrained IoT systems.

title: Ship power grid reconstruction optimization algorithm integrating GBNN-CS-ROA strategy
authors: Qirun Hao, Baoming Yang, Jiazheng Sun
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Ships and Electromechanical System (ICISES)
abstract: Aiming at the high-dimensional nonlinear optimization problem in the reconstruction of ship power grids, an improved algorithm (ROGCS) integrating discrete bio-inspired neural Network (GBNN), Cuckoos Algorithm (CS), and Rider Optimization Algorithm (ROA) is proposed. The convergence speed and accuracy are improved through dynamic Levy flight adjustment and multi-population cooperative strategies. Experiments show that ROGCS outperforms the comparison algorithms on four types of benchmark functions and the CEC2014 test set.

title: Real-Time IIoT Cloud-Based Monitoring and Analytics for Extrusion and Cooling Systems
authors: Manuel Cardona, Bryan Vásquez-Pineda, Rubén Torres, Jonatan Barahona
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: This paper presents a cost-effective, real-time Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) monitoring and analytics system designed for cable extrusion machines and cooling systems in a manufacturing environment at Enerwire, a leading Salvadoran cable manufacturing company. The system utilizes existing machine encoders to capture extrusion speed pulses and thermocouples which are processed by a Siemens S7-1200 PLC. Data from five extrusion machines, including temperature measurements, are aggregated and transmitted to an industrial IoT gateway (IOT2050). The gateway forwards the data to a MySQL cloud database hosted on Microsoft Azure, enabling live visualization through Grafana dashboards. The system also incorporates alert mechanisms for production stoppages and abnormal extrusion speeds, providing timely notifications to operators. Future work will focus on applying machine learning techniques to the collected data for predictive analytics and process optimization. This approach offers a scalable and affordable solution for digital transformation in industrial manufacturing.

title: A Comparative Evaluation of Deep Learning Based Methods for Detection and Classification of Paddy Plant Diseases
authors: Sivakumar R
abstract: This work offers a comparative analysis of deep learning (DL) methodologies for the detection and classification of rice leaf diseases utilizing picture data. Rice is a global staple crop, and its yield is significantly impacted by foliar diseases such as blast, bacterial blight, and leaf smut, necessitating early and accurate diagnosis for food security. A meticulously compiled dataset of healthy and diseased paddy plant is created, thereafter standardized and expanded to accommodate changes in illumination, backdrop, and symptom severity. Various state-of-the-art DL methodologies are examined, including VGG19, Xception, and MobileNet, which integrate transfer learning with efficient feature selection. Models are evaluated using several performance indicators, with MobileNet systems attaining accuracies over 85% on binary paddy plant disease classification tasks. This comparative analysis offers pragmatic recommendations for the selection and design of paddy leaf disease detecting systems tailored for real-time smart farming applications and proactive disease control.

title: Making AI Ethics Guidelines Work: Readability as a Driver of Personal Data Protection Policy Implementation
authors: Jae-Seong Lee, Juyeon Lee
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: This study examines how the readability of cybersecurity policy guidelines influences their practical applicability, focusing on South Korea’s AI Personal Information Protection Self-Assessment Checklist. Although the checklist is distributed as a cybersecurity self-regulation tool, it is grounded in broader AI ethics frameworks that emphasize principles such as privacy, accountability, and transparency. However, while such ethics-based documents are increasingly used to guide security practices, they often remain difficult to implement due to legalistic language, technical jargon, and ambiguous phrasing. To analyze the document’s linguistic and structural features, this study applies the Hemingway Editor for computational readability assessment and collects revision feedback. Through this approach, the study identifies recurring usability barriers, including overly complex sentence constructions, inconsistent terminology, and a lack of concrete, actionable examples. The findings suggest that enhancing readability is not merely a matter of stylistic refinement, but a necessary condition for the effective implementation of ethical and secure practices in AI systems. The study highlights four key revision strategies: simplifying language, standardizing terminology, incorporating practical examples, and improving visual formatting. Ultimately, it argues that document design functions as a critical policy lever in cybersecurity governance, shaping the enabling conditions under which responsible AI can be realized in practice.

title: An Energy-Efficient 197-to-210GHz 4×4 Beam-Steerable Radiator Array with Integrated Microbump Radiation and 1.35% DC-to-RF Efficiency in 65-nm CMOS
authors: Huibo Wu, Xuan Liu, Xingcun Li, Yuqun Liu, Liang Gao, Yue Li, Wenhua Chen
abstract: This paper presents a 197-to-210GHz 4×4 beam-steerable radiator array in TSMC 65-nm CMOS. In this array, a scalable standing-wave loop is proposed for minor locking errors and robust coupling. Meanwhile, a low-cost compact integrated microbump array is proposed to achieve high-efficiency radiation and beam steering. The array achieves 28.3dBm lensless EIRP, 11.1dBm radiated power, 1.35% DC-to-RF efficiency, and 56° beam steering. Ultimately, millimeter-level terahertz SAR imaging is achieved based on this radiator array.

title: AI-Driven Potato Disease Detection and Yield Optimization Through Leaf Image Analysis
authors: Kavita Jindal
abstract: Planting of potatoes around the globe is under severe threat due to diseases Phytophthora infestans and Alternaria solani which cause massive losses in crop. These diseases can be diagnosed early enough hence their effects on global food security systems can be mitigated adequately. The study examines the application of Convolutional Neural Networks under deep learning algorithms to detect diseases in potato leaf images. The CNN-based model with EfficientNet architecture did the classification of the potato leaves under the healthy and two disease conditions of the early blight and the late blight. The developed model registered a high level of success in its operation as it demonstrated an almost perfect performance on both training and validation data sets. The efficacy was also confirmed by the fact that it had rigorous evaluation criteria that comprised of precision metrics as well as recall metrics and F1-score analysis along with the evaluation of the confusion matrix. The model that has been assisted with the methods of transfer learning and data augmentation to attain effective generalization in real-life setting. The model was trained and validated on a dataset of 2152 potato leaf images across three classes, achieving an overall classification accuracy of 99%, with precision, recall, and F1-score also reaching <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8 \%}$</tex>. The study findings indicate that CNNs are providing a viable solution to early diagnosis of disease that can enhance potato crops to enhance food security mechanisms.

title: Visual Marker Search for Autonomous Drone Landing in Diverse Urban Environments
authors: Jiaohong Yao, Linfeng Liang, Yao Deng, Xi Zheng, Richard Han, Yuankai Qi
abstract: Marker-based landing is widely used in drone delivery and return-to-base systems for its simplicity and reliability. However, most approaches assume idealized landing site visibility and sensor performance, limiting robustness in complex urban settings. We present a simulation-based evaluation suite on the AirSim platform with systematically varied urban layouts, lighting, and weather to replicate realistic operational diversity. Using onboard camera sensor—RGB for marker detection and depth for obstacle avoidance—we benchmark two heuristic coverage patterns and a reinforcement learning-based agent, analyzing how exploration strategy and scene complexity affect success rate, path efficiency, and robustness. Results underscore the need to evaluate marker-based autonomous landing under diverse, sensor-relevant conditions to guide the development of reliable aerial navigation systems.

title: Validation of conductive resin as electrode material in neuromonitoring with 3D-printed probes
authors: Marvin Schmid, Sumith Thayambath, Hans-Peter Landgraf, Volker Bucher, Constanze Kiese
abstract: We conducted a proof-of-concept study for an additively manufactured complex 3D electrode structure for anal neuromonitoring using silver resin. Recent developments in additive manufacturing promise significant advantages for prototyping medical sensors. These prototypes are scalable and customisable, with a variety of biocompatible 3D-printable materials available at reasonable manufacturing costs and an increasing range of printable conductive materials. However, difficulties arise in reaching stable conductivity in complex electrode configurations. Since reliable conductive properties are key characteristics of biomedical sensors, we aimed to find an experimental setup that mimics the signal propagation of connective tissue and allows for spatial resolution of signals for rapid self-testing of multi-electrode sensors.

title: Deep Residual Segmentation Architecture with GBO-Tuned Hyperparameters for Accurate Oil Spill Extraction in Maritime RGB Imagery
authors: K Siva Kumar, Md Ahammad Sharif, Venkata Ramana Avula, Arun Kumar, Arji Sai Kumar, Malla Haswanth
abstract: This research focuses on the development of a Deep Learning (DL) System that is intended to provide accurate detection of Oil Spills in Port Images (RGB) using a DL framework. The method overcome issues such as noise and poor visibility. The first step of this process involving using an Adaptive Scharr Filter (ASF) to preprocess the image so that image contrast is improved while removing the majority of the noise but keeping important edge information in the image. After preprocessing the image, a Residual Segmentation Network (Res-SegNet) is created using a deep residual architecture that connects the multiple layer of neural network together to create connections that incorporate multiple scales of spatial data into one final output. The Grizzly Bear Optimization (GBO) is used to further improve Res-SegNet accuracy by tuning hyperparameters and eliminating segmentation errors caused during image processing and training. In addition, a clustering module incorporating into the final network architecture that refines and combines previously segmented region into larger with larger groups with more consistent classifications of oil spill areas while reducing the number of false positives. The findings of the experiments shows that Res-SegNet is more accurate than standard models at finding oil spills in a variety of port images, with an overall accuracy of 95%.

title: Explainable Deep Transfer Learning for Multi-Class Knee Osteoporosis Diagnosis Using Grad-CAM and DenseNet121
authors: Ahmad Aburomman, Khaled Hamad, Suhaila Abuowaida, Adai Al-Momani, Raed Al-Azaidah, Omar Alidmat
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Osteoporosis is a chronic bone disease characterized by decreased bone mineral density and is frequently unrecognized until patients suffer debilitating fractures. Early detection—differentiating between normal, osteopenia, and osteoporosis—is crucial for minimizing clinical risk. This study presents an interpretable deep learning model for classifying knee osteoporosis using X-ray images. Leveraging transfer learning, this research fine-tuned several pre-trained Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures, with DenseNet121 achieving the highest classification accuracy of 92.50% on the Multi-Class Knee Osteoporosis X-Ray dataset. To enhance interpretability, the proposed work employed Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) to visualize the salient regions in the Xray images that contributed most to the model's predictions. Our approach not only surpasses state-of-the-art benchmarks in classification performance but also enhances model transparency, facilitating its potential deployment in clinical settings. These findings underscore the promise of explainable AI in medical imaging and support the use of our DenseNet121-based model with Grad-CAM as an effective tool for early and interpretable assessment of osteoporosis.

title: An Efficient $\mathrm{O}\left(\mathrm{N}^{\mathrm{2}}\right)$ Cubic Spline Smoothing Technique
authors: Taleb Moazzeni, Javeed Hussain
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Smoothing splines are powerful nonparametric tools for curve fitting and density estimation, prized for their flexibility and smoothness in modeling complex data. However, their computational complexity, typically <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O\left(N^{3}\right)$</tex> for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N$</tex> observations due to matrix inversion, limits their applicability to large-scale datasets prevalent in fields like genomics, climate modeling, and machine learning. Recent advances have reduced this burden to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O\left(N^{2} \log N\right)$</tex> or even <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O(N)$</tex> through adaptive basis sampling and space-filling designs, yet these methods often trade off accuracy or impose restrictive assumptions. This paper introduces a novel cubic spline smoothing approximation technique that achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O\left(N^{2}\right)$</tex> complexity while preserving the accuracy of traditional smoothing splines. By leveraging a low-rank tensor product decomposition of the penalty matrix and iterative preconditioning, our approach eliminates the need for full matrix inversion, yielding up to 90 % CPU time savings compared to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O\left(N^{2} {\operatorname {log}} N\right)$</tex> benchmarks. We demonstrate its efficacy through extensive simulations and real-world applications, where it consistently delivers robust, smooth estimates with minimal computational overhead. Our method thus offers a scalable, accurate solution for modern big data challenges, extending the practical reach of smoothing splines.

title: The Impact of Influential Figures on Social and Cultural Life
authors: Khawlah M. AL-Tkhayneh, Abdeldjalil Bouzenoun, Moustafa Elmetwaly Kandeel
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Recent years have witnessed a profound transformation in modes of communication and social interaction due to the spread of social media, which has become an essential part of the lives of millions around the world. These platforms have significantly reshaped how individuals and communities interact by making communication faster and more efficient and by enabling the broad exchange of ideas and information. Despite their benefits in strengthening social relationships and community participation, they have also produced new problems, such as addiction and a reduction in face-to-face social interaction. Social media has likewise affected mental health, as social comparisons with idealized content have increased anxiety and depression among some users. Politically, culturally, and economically, the phenomenon of “social influencers” has emerged; these figures now exert strong sway over public opinion and use it to steer issues that extend beyond commercial marketing into politics, culture, and the economy. This raises questions about the potential risks of such influence for social and political stability. The study adopted a descriptive approach to outline the phenomenon and explicate points related to it. It analyzed content from the following creators as empirical material to reach general conclusions about the topic: (chef) Burak Özdemir, (chef) Abeer Al-Saghir, the Portuguese footballer Cristiano Ronaldo, and the Brazilian footballer Neymar Jr. The study found that audience engagement depends on how far the content aligns with followers' cultural and social values and interests; followers tend to support content that reinforces shared values and humanitarian causes, whereas content that conflicts with cultural traditions encounters negative reactions.

title: 2026 12th International Conference on Web Research (ICWR)
authors: N/A

title: A Format-Preserving Encryption Method for Securing Geospatial Vector Map Data
authors: Ahmed Abubahia, Adel Rahuma, Hisham AbouGrad, Salem Alsaid, Mohamed Alrayes
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: In recent years, advances in Geographic Information System (GIS) tools and applications have significantly enhanced the usability of spatial data. GIS has become a vital resource for mapping and visualising spatial information. Additionally, increased research in the GIS field has resulted in a wide availability of digital spatial data, which influences various reallife applications. The high value of spatial vector map data entails risks of unauthorised access, modification, or distribution. Therefore, it is crucial to maintain the confidentiality of this data and protect it from unauthorised access. To address this challenge, numerous research efforts have explored cryptographic methods as a promising solution. Encryption methods can be broadly classified into two main categories: frequency domain implementation and spatial domain implementation. Spatial domain implementation is faster and less computationally intensive compared to frequency domain encryption. However, many proposed methods fail to preserve the data format and maintain the accuracy of spatial coordinates. Additionally, most existing methods do not consider computational time performance, which extends their applicability to many real-time applications, such as drone surveillance systems. In this paper, we propose a spatial domain polygon-based encryption approach that encrypts spatial vector data while preserving the data format and improving computational time performance efficiently. The experimental results demonstrate the efficiency and performance of the proposed encryption approach compared to existing methods.

title: CyberShield V2.0: An Enhanced Hybrid Detection Framework with Autoencoder-Based Anomaly Detection and Temporal Intelligence
authors: Vigneshwaran J, Tharun C, Swaminathan K
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Based on the continuously increasing complexity of cyber threats, traditional signature-based intrusion detection systems and static machine learning-based models become ineffective and obsolete in current network settings. Despite the improvements brought by hybrid machine learning models that enhance the detection capability for known attack samples, they struggle with zero-day attack detection, temporal attack patterns, and explainability. In this article, we introduce the enhanced version of the hybrid attack detection system, namely CyberShield v2.0, an improved version of the existing system that introduces additional components such as anomaly detection based on the use of an autoencoder, temporal knowledge, the use of a meta-learner for combining models, and SHAP-based explainability. Indeed, the system utilizes an autoencoder for learning normal traffic in the network for detecting unknown attacks, while temporal aggregation of traffic parameters helps in identifying effective characterization of burst attack, port scan, and anomalous peaks. Moreover, this system utilizes an online meta-learner that combines the outputs of several supervised and unsupervised models, thus enhancing robustness and detection capability. Additionally, this system applies SHAP-based explainability for providing transparency into the decision made by the system based on specific attributes.

title: Feature Extraction and Refined Modeling of Complex Roadway Environments Based on Visual Images
authors: Jiuhong Wang, Yaomeng Xiao, Canguang Zheng, Yang Jiang, Mingchao Du, Kun Zhang, Tongzhu Yu, Dainxiang Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The spatial environment of coal mine roadways is complex. To restore the true appearance of underground roadways, this paper proposes a method for extracting the features of complex roadway environments and conducting refined modeling based on visual images. This paper pairs a working face intelligent lighting control system based on wireless Mesh networks, uses a tracked transfer robot equipped with an Intel D455 depth camera to collect three-dimensional data, and synchronously captures depth images and color images of the simulated roadway through infrared structured light and RGB cameras to record the geometric information and texture features of the roadway surface. It then performs denoising on the original depth images to eliminate random noise and outliers. Subsequently, the SIFT feature detection algorithm is used to extract image feature points and generate the initial point cloud of the roadway. Finally, three-dimensional contour reconstruction experiments are conducted on simulated rectangular and arched roadways, which can fully reflect the surface features of complex roadways and achieve refined modeling.

title: Design and Implementation of an Automatic Pressing Test System for Remote Controllers Based on Machine Vision and Mqtt
authors: Haoran Guo, Shangji Nong, Changlian Chen, Ge Zhu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This paper presents a movable intelligent automatic pressing test system for remote controls, based on a three-layer architecture of Raspberry Pi-based image recognition, cloud data interaction, and ESP32-controlled actuation. The system achieves high-precision key positioning via machine vision and MQTTbased communication, enabling remote and unmanned testing. Experimental results show that the system significantly improves testing efficiency and accuracy compared to manual and traditional methods.

title: Large-Scale MEMS Micro-Hotplates: A Fabrication and Characterization Approach Based on Eight-Cantilever Beam Structure
authors: Feixiang Cheng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive study on the fabrication and performance characterization of large-scale MEMS micro-hotplates featuring an eight-cantilever beam structure, designed for enhanced mechanical stability and uniform thermal distribution in gas sensing applications. The proposed design addresses the limitations of traditional micro-hotplates, such as small working areas and mechanical instability during extended operation, by incorporating a suspended platform with dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$300 \mu \mathrm{m} \times 300 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>, spiral heating electrodes, and circular interdigital electrodes. Fabrication processes include LPCVD and PECVD for layer deposition, photolithography for patterning, and wet etching with KOH and TMAH for structure release. Performance evaluation encompasses voltagetemperature characteristics, power consumption, temperature uniformity, stress, and displacement analyses across multiple devices and configurations. Results demonstrate consistent temperatures around 301° C at 3.5 V with power consumption of approximately 56 mW, high uniformity exceeding 98%, robust mechanical properties, and long-term reliability. Comparative analyses with state-of-the-art configurations highlight improvements in efficiency and stability, achieving up to 15% better performance relative to baselines. This work provides a reliable technical pathway for scalable production of high-performance MEMS gas sensors.

title: Fault Identification and Prediction in a Wind Turbine using Digital Twins
authors: S. Anbumalar, Parthiban B, M. Thogesh, S. Hemachandasanam, R. Iyyappan, S. Madhan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The paper describes an end-to-end system which integrates physical simulation and machine learning to predict inevitabilities within wind turbines before they happen. A Python based simulation platform represents the entire turbine system aerodynamics, mechanical subsystems and electrical elements creating synthetic operational data that encompasses normal operating conditions and several fault scenarios. This is the direct solution to one of the major wind turbine fault prediction challenges: the lack of actual fault data. By generating high-volume, balanced, and labelled training data, the framework facilitates building of quality machine learning models that would otherwise prove extremely hard to develop on actual turbine operations. A framework classifier trained on this synthetic dataset achieves high accuracy in the classification of various types of faults. The framework demonstrates a total end-to-end pipeline physics driven data generation verified through successful machine learning deployment compared to actual operational data of an NREL 5-MW reference wind turbine. The Random Forest model offers interpretable fault predictions and extensive feature-importance extraction, enabling operators to discover which sensor variables are most indicative of certain fault types, such as bearing deterioration, gearbox failures, generator failures, and structural blade damage.

title: Real-Time on-Site Grid Fault Detection and Automated Load Management Using Arduino with GSM Alert for Voltage and Frequency Thershold Monitoring
authors: V. Muneeswaran, S. Venkata Jeevan, G. Yashwanth Goud, U. Gowtham
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
