To reduce subjectivity and improve accuracy, this paper proposes a lightweight convolutional Transformer network with high–low frequency attention (Hilo-L-Conv-ViT) for real-time gold roughing froth image analysis. The lightweight convolutional module reduces computational cost, while the high–low frequency attention replaces standard multi-head attention to enhance both detail and global features in froth images. Experiments show that the proposed method achieves 97.17% accuracy, 98.82% recall, and 98.00% F1-score, outperforming VGG19, ResNet50, and ViT. Industrial deployment further confirms its effectiveness, increasing gold recovery from 83.79% to 86.27%.

title: Edge AI Architecture for Real-Time Arrhythmia Detection Using Wearable ECG Devices
authors: R. Sundar, Avinash Deyyam, Anusha N, Kalisetti Purushotham Prasad, Shoyimkulov Asror, Jami Venkata Suman
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: As the world's population continues to age rapidly, more sophisticated IoT (Internet of Things) frameworks and smart home health assistance technology have emerged to help people remain independent while living at home. While strides have been made in the development of smart homes and robotic assistants that make it easier to monitor individuals in their homes, there are still limitations when it comes to constantly tracking people's body functions through traditional means of monitoring their cardiovascular system using new wireless medical technologies. The use of a centralized cloud computing architecture to process ECG data continuously creates a number of problems for monitoring cardiovascular conditions using on-device ECG devices. Therefore, this research presents an Edge AI architecture specifically for real-time arrhythmia identification using wearable ECG devices. Thus, this architecture reduces the latency associated with dying of a heart rhythm disorder to only milliseconds from the time it takes for the ECG device to send the ECG data to the centralized cloud server for processing, yet provides real-time detection of abnormal heart events while dramatically reducing power consumption and protecting user privacy. In addition to providing a complete health monitoring solution for those aging in place, the proposed Edge AI architecture can be easily incorporated into existing multi-agent smart home and Care Bot architectures.

title: Research on the response of underwater explosion shock wave and cavitation effect to stiffened plate structure
authors: Jinlei Wang, Qilong He, Mingmin Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Ships and Electromechanical System (ICISES)
abstract: Taking the stiffened plate as the research object, its dynamic response under the secondary loading of underwater explosion shock wave and cavitation effect is discussed. By using the coupled acoustic-solid method in ABAQUS software, the displacement response, acceleration response and velocity response of well-shaped, cross and double-cross stiffened plates under different blasting depths and different explosive amounts are studied and analyzed, and the variation law of each response is summarized. The results show that the secondary loading under the cavitation effect will have a greater impact on the stiffened plate, and the structural vibration will be stronger, especially on the acceleration response of the stiffened plate. The performance of the stiffened plate is better than the other two. The research results provide a theoretical basis for scholars to analyze the influence of the cavitation effect on the response of the ship under the action of the shock wave load, and the influence of the cavitation effect cannot be ignored.

title: Optimizing Crop Selection and Fertilizer Usage with a Hybrid SVM-Genetic Algorithm Framework in Precision Agriculture
authors: Venkata Kiran Kumar Ravi, Rachel Nallathamby, Shanmuga Priya S, Tamilarasi Rajamani, M. Abinaya, Nimisha Amrutkar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The concept of precision agriculture emerged in the 1980s as a means to tailor fertilizer rates and mixtures to individual growing sites, with positive results for a wide range of crops and regions. Inadequate baseline data, limited sample methods, lack of site-specific fertilizer recommendations, and a paucity of qualified specialists are agronomic issues; high costs and limited experience are socioeconomic constraints. As a result, adoption remains uneven despite global interest. In order to address these challenges, datasets for crop selection and fertilizer optimisation were cleaned and prepared using data preprocessing. The optimal feature selection was achieved via RFE. A structurally reduced hybrid model that combined a GA and an SVM was used to select the crop and usage of fertilizers, an important measure of crop health and nutrient status. When it came to complicated nonlinear connections, the GA-SVM model achieved an accuracy of 94.69 %, which was higher than rival models. They need to overcome socioeconomic and technical obstacles to disseminate advanced machine learning and precision agriculture, which can increase fertilizer efficiency and crop selection.

title: A Progressive Two-Stage Wiener Process Model for Remaining Useful Life Prediction
authors: Jin Xie, Zhizhong Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Modeling equipment degradation and predicting the remaining useful life (RUL) are critical yet challenging, primarily due to the commonly observed multi-stage characteristics of degradation processes. These characteristics arise from variations in internal mechanisms or external environments, making it difficult to accurately predict RUL before a change point occurs. Existing methods mostly assume an abrupt change in the degradation rate at the change point. In practice, degradation patterns often exhibit gradual transitions. To address this, this paper proposes a generalized progressive two-stage stochastic degradation model that unifies the description of both abrupt and gradual changes between degradation stages. Based on this model, analytical solutions for the probability distributions of equipment lifetime and RUL are derived. The simulation results demonstrate that under two-stage degradation scenarios, the proposed method can effectively and accurately predict the RUL, thereby verifying both the effectiveness of the method and the accuracy of its theoretical model.

title: TIME-E 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)

title: A 64-Gb/s QPSK Carrier-Phase-Recovery Loop with Wide IF Range Using Dual-Oscillator Frequency Translation
authors: Mohamed Osama Selim, Mohamed Saad Aly, Mahmoud A. Khalil, Mohamed Badr Younis, Mostafa G. Ahmed, Ahmed E. Abdelrahman, Sujay Patel, Karim Elmeligy, John Burns, Pavan Kumar Hanumolu
abstract: This paper presents an analog carrier-phase-recovery (CPR) architecture that overcomes the fundamental trade-off between IF-offset range and loop bandwidth. A dual-VCO frequency translation loop simultaneously reduces phase noise and corrects ±2 GHz frequency offsets, allowing operation across wide IF variations. Measurements show 64-Gb/s operation with reopened eyes under severe offset and frequency modulation stress, achieving an LO phase deviation of 2.27° rms and a 5.5 pJ/bit efficiency, confirming the feasibility of analog high-speed CPR receivers.

title: Strategic Framework for Human Resource Management in Logistics Companies: Case Studies and Best Practices
authors: Sovannpanha Kong, Sreyneath Chhun, Vannak Seng, Kimseng Tieng
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: This paper create a comprehensive framework for Human Resources Management (HRM) in the logistics industry. The qualitative research is conducted by interviewing three logistics firms in 2023 including a medium-sized firm, a large-sized firm, and a conglomerate firm. Results indicate that the medium-sized firm tends to have less systematic recruitment and selection process. It mainly recruits new employees by advertising jobs on social media and shortlisting them for interviews. For training and development, the firm allows employees to join workshops. For retention and compensation, the firm uses KPIs to measure staff performance and promote existing employees with good KPIs to a higher position. The large firm also implements the above-mentioned practices and including promoting internal staff and building a partnership with educational centers to get highly capable students for recruitment and selection. For training and development, the firm provides extra training on the course needed, provides learning opportunities to attend local and international events, and offer job rotation to build employee capabilities. The firm provides job rotation and maternity leave to employees as a part of retention and compensation. The conglomerate firm it organizes job fairs to acquire talented resources. After recruitment and selection, they offer various training, including on-the-job training, classroom training, E-Learning, Language training, and compliance & safety training. To retain and compensate talented employees, the conglomerate firm offers career development opportunities by offering regular feedback, creating a positive work culture, promoting work-life balance, offering competitive salaries and bonuses, providing health benefits, offering retirement leave and paid leave benefits, and reviewing benefits regularly. Hence, top management of the firm should (1) have a systematic HRM process, (2) implement HRM practices based on the size of their organization, so that their company can effectively and efficiently use their resources, and (3) set up a clear structure of meetings and systematic HRM practices for effectively managing employees in the firm. The study is limited to the logistics industry, where other sectors may adopt different frameworks for HRM practice.

title: LOIC-DDoS: A Machine Learning–Ready Dataset for DDoS Detection Across Diverse Networks Environments
authors: Danny E. Acosta-Tejada, Javier E. Sanchez-Galan
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: Cybersecurity has evolved as a crucial discipline, focusing on safeguarding information and systems from digital threats and attacks. Foundational research, including studies on TCP/IP security issues and the vulnerabilities of servers facing the internet, has underscored the complexity and importance of robust cybersecurity measures. With the advancement of cloud computing and serverless systems, the introduction of the Internet of Things (IoT) has further complicated the digital landscape, integrating a myriad of devices that demand constant connectivity and security. Among various cyber threats, Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks are particularly noteworthy for their capacity to incapacitate systems by overwhelming them with traffic from multiple sources. The evolution of DDoS methodologies necessitates current and realistic datasets for defense strategy development. This paper presents LOIC-DDoS-Dataset, a novel dataset that surpasses existing benchmarks in simulating modern network environments and DDoS attack patterns. To validate the dataset’s effectiveness, we implemented an analytical framework combining statistical anomaly detection, DBSCAN clustering, and advanced dimensionality reduction techniques including PacMAP. Experimental evaluation using Random Forest, XGBoost, Isolation Forest, and One-Class SVM demonstrated the dataset’s discriminative power, with supervised learning models achieving perfect classification performance (0.999 accuracy and F1-score) on the sample of $1,000,000$-record. The substantial performance gap between supervised and unsupervised approaches validates the quality of engineered features and anomaly labels, facilitating the development of advanced DDoS mitigation strategies and contributing to refined Intrusion Detection Systems capable of addressing sophisticated cyber threats.

title: An Energy-Aware Real-Time Task Scheduling Framework for Low-Power Wireless IoT Networks
authors: Amarana Prabhakara Rao, Ramesh Babu Yathirajula, Varun Mishra
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has created unprecedented opportunities for ubiquitous sensing and actuation across diverse applications including smart cities, industrial automation, and healthcare monitoring. However, the battery-constrained nature of IoT devices poses significant challenges for sustainable network operation. This paper proposes a novel intelligent embedded systems framework for energy-aware task scheduling in low-power wireless IoT networks. We formulate a comprehensive optimization problem that jointly considers energy consumption, task deadlines, Quality of Service (QoS) requirements, and network lifetime maximization. The proposed framework integrates real-time operating system (RTOS) principles with machine learning-based workload prediction to enable dynamic task scheduling decisions. We develop a hybrid scheduling algorithm combining Earliest Deadline First (EDF) with Least Laxity First (LLF) policies, augmented by deep reinforcement learning for adaptive parameter tuning. Furthermore, we incorporate dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) techniques coordinated with wireless duty cycling to minimize energy consumption while meeting real-time constraints. Extensive experimental evaluations using ARM Cortex-M4 based IoT nodes demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves 43% energy savings, 58% improvement in task schedulability, 35% reduction in deadline misses, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.7 \times$</tex> enhancement in network lifetime compared to conventional scheduling approaches. The framework provides a practical solution for sustainable IoT deployments in resourceconstrained environments.

title: Development of a Reference Electrode Integrated Ir/IrOx Thin film pH Sensor for microRNA Detection
authors: Kazuya Suzuki, Miyuki Tabata, Yuji Miyahara, Takahiro Arakawa
abstract: Liquid biopsy is a minimally invasive diagnostic method that detects disease markers in body fluids, offering advantages over traditional biopsy such as faster testing and reduced patient burden. Among potential biomarkers, microRNA (miRNA) is promising due to its stability in blood and disease-specific expression. In this study, we developed a reference-electrode-integrated $\mathrm{Ir} / \mathrm{IrOx}$ thin-film pH sensor to detect miRNA using hydrogen ions ($\mathrm{H}^{+}$) released during nucleic acid amplification. $\mathrm{Ir} / \mathrm{IrOx}$ films were fabricated via reactive sputtering, and the sensor formed under $\mathrm{Ar}: \mathrm{O}_{2}=0: 100$ showed a pH sensitivity of -58.5 $\mathrm{mV} / \mathrm{pH}$, close to the theoretical value. For reference integration, AgCl was deposited on Ag thin films using 3-diaminopropane-tetraacetic acid ferric ammonium salt monohydrate (PDTA·Fe(III)). The AgCl electrode formed with 10 mM PDTA and 5-minute immersion showed the best $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$sensitivity of $-51.9 \mathrm{mV} / \log \left[\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right]$and stable performance in the $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$range typical of isothermal amplification. The integrated sensor exhibited a pH sensitivity of $59.5 \mathrm{mV} / \mathrm{pH}$, indicating its potential for real-time miRNA detection in point-of-care testing.

title: A Simultaneous 16-Channel Adiabatic Charge-Tracking Stimulator with 87.4% Stimulator Efficiency and Low Load Sensitivity
authors: Eojin Kim, Janghwan Kim, Jaeseong Park, Seongjae Moon, Geunchang Seong, Su Yeon Jeon, Seonae Jang, Young Sang Cho, Sohmyung Ha, Chul Kim
abstract: This paper presents a simultaneous 16-channel adiabatic charge-tracking stimulator (SACT) for implantable systems. Time-multiplexed stimulation enables parallel operation using a single current-generation block. An on-chip capacitor ensures accurate per-channel charge balance, while an inductor adiabatically recovers energy each cycle. A time-based sensor predicts phase transitions. A stimulator efficiency of 87.4% with <3.7% variation and <0.54% charge mismatch across 500 <inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Ω</inf>–10 k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Ω</inf> and 60–250 nC stimulation charge was achieved. In-vivo multichannel stimulation was successfully demonstrated.

title: Multi-Model Machine Learning Framework for Evaluating Surface Roughness Characteristics in Abrasive Water Jet Cutting of Ramie Fibre Composites
authors: Lavanya. M
abstract: Abrasive Water Jet (AWJ) cutting is an emerging non-traditional machining technique for fibre-reinforced polymer composites because it can cut complex shapes with less thermal damage. However, maintaining consistent surface quality remains a challenge due to the intricate relationship between machining variables and fibre-reinforced polymer composite properties. In this research, a multi-model machine learning approach is proposed to assess and predict the surface roughness properties of ramie fibre-reinforced polymer composites during AWJ cutting. Three machine learning models, namely Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), were created and compared for regression and classification tasks. Among the models compared, XGBoost was found to have the best predictive model with an outstanding <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> value of 0.999 and close to zero MSE and MAE, which showed excellent agreement with experimental values. Furthermore, XGBoost had the best recall and F1-score for surface roughness classification. The results demonstrate the strength of ensemble learning methods, especially the XGBoost algorithm, in modelling AWJM processes with complex nonlinear relationships. The proposed framework, which allows for the accurate prediction of surface quality and process optimisation, thus contributes to the achievement of SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation, and Infrastructure) through the use of intelligent manufacturing technologies.

title: Comparative Evaluation of End-to-End Automated Testing Tools for Web Applications: Cypress vs. Playwright
authors: Mabroukah Amarif, Hisham Imhmed, Fatma Awidat, Mansour Ahwidy, Khamiss Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: The rapid growth of software development has increased the complexity of digital systems, making system reliability and quality critical for success. End-to-End (E2E) testing simulates real user interactions to verify overall system behavior and detect integration issues. While manual testing is time-consuming and prone to human error, automated testing frameworks like Cypress and Playwright provide efficient solutions for E2E testing. This study evaluates these two frameworks in a practical application using the open-source AdminLTE dashboard, focusing on performance, usability, browser coverage, and defect detection. Experimental results indicate that Playwright outperforms Cypress in execution speed and resource utilization, while Cypress offers an intuitive GUI experience for developers. The findings provide actionable insights for developers seeking effective E2E testing strategies in modern web application environments.

title: Smart and Sustainable Water Quality Monitoring System Using Explainable AI
authors: Kalai Selvi T, Ajaykannan R, Boomika M, Dharunkumar B
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Reliable monitoring of water quality is essential for protecting aquatic ecosystems, ensuring sustainable aquaculture production, and safeguarding public health. Conventional monitoring approaches rely on periodic sampling and laboratory analysis, which often result in delayed detection of unsafe water conditions and limited monitoring coverage. This paper presents an interpretable machine learning framework for intelligent water quality monitoring that integrates predictive modeling with Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI). Fourteen physicochemical and biological parameters are used to train an XGBoost-based classification model that categorizes water conditions into SAFE and UNSAFE classes. To enhance transparency, SHAP-based explanations are applied to quantify the influence of individual parameters on the prediction outcome and translate model decisions into human-understandable reasoning. In addition, similarity-based evidence retrieval using a k-nearest neighbor approach provides supporting examples from historical observations to strengthen decision reliability. The system is implemented through an interactive monitoring dashboard that performs range-constrained validation, prediction visualization, and explanation delivery. Experimental evaluation on an aquaculture water quality dataset demonstrates strong classification performance while maintaining interpretability and decision transparency. The proposed framework provides a practical decision-support tool that can assist environmental monitoring authorities and aquaculture practitioners in identifying critical water quality parameters and enabling timely corrective actions in intelligent monitoring environments.

title: Hybrid MPPT with Temperature Compensation for Improved Solar PV Efficiency and Stable Maximum Power Tracking
authors: V. Ravi, S. Ramesh, V. Kiruthigadevi, S. Prashanth, L. Aashiqakram, S. Varshini
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Fluctuations in temperature and sun irradiation often lead to a degradation of the performance of photovoltaic (PV) systems, with the operating point no longer being close to the Maximum Power Point (MPP). To improve power extraction and maintain constant power in a solar <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P V}$</tex> system in changing climatic conditions, this paper proposes a Hybrid Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) system that has temperature correction. This study considers two systems including the proposed Hybrid MPPT with Temperature Compensation system and a conventional MPPT system. The proposed system integrates a hybrid inverter, a DC-DC boost converter, an LM35 temperature sensor, and the Incremental Conductance (INC) algorithm. Both systems have been modeled and simulated using MATLAB/Simulink with different temperatures and different levels of solar irradiation between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100\ \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1000\ \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>. According to simulation results, the proposed Hybrid MPPT system has an efficiency of 98.6 % and a fast tracking response of about 35 ms. In comparison with the conventional MPPT methods, the hybrid inverter is more efficient, has improved tracking capability and stability in the output, and the AC output has the Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) of approximately 3 %

title: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
authors: N/A

title: Film Festival as the Agent of Environmental Behavior Change: The Case of 2023 Jogja-Netpac Asian Film Festival
authors: Rosidah Rosidah, Ekky Imanjaya, Shadia Imanuella Pradsmadji, Dag Yngvesson, Tri Adi Sumbogo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)
abstract: There has been a phenomenon of various film festivals increasing awareness of environmental issues, from zero-waste campaigns on screen and in the canteen areas to waste management. The study investigates how the 2023 Jogja-NETPAC Asian Film Festival (JAFF) serves as an agent of change in the field of environmental issues. Particularly, how the film festival functions as a medium of social marketing to promote pro-environmental behaviors. Using qualitative content analysis of in-depth interviews with the festival organizers, the study identifies key themes surrounding the strategy implementation of the zero-waste policy; challenges and limitations of the implementation; and the improvement plan in the sustainability agenda. Findings highlight the potential of cultural festivals to act as integrated platforms for environmental and social transformation within a community-based social marketing framework. Digital transformation has also become a driving factor that the organizer can leverage to improve the effectiveness of the campaign.

title: A 31.1 pJ/b Human Body Communication Transmitter with Standard-basis CDMA for Multi-Node IoB Systems
authors: Anyu Jiang, Asif Iftekhar Omi, Ratul Kundu, Shreyas Sen, Baibhab Chatterjee
abstract: Voltage-mode Human-Body Communication (HBC) can enable highly energy-efficient medical wearables and implants due to the ability to transmit wideband signals through the body channel with low power consumption. However, with multiple devices envisioned in the emerging Internet of Bodies (IoB) network, the energy-efficiency of conventional 2-device HBC systems degrade due to the need of multiple-access support. In this paper, we introduce the first on-chip synchronous standard-basis (SB)-CDMA implementation for low-power HBC, supporting up to 16 nodes without power-hungry accumulators or ADCs, providing an efficient solution to multiple channel access with HBC. Additionally, for a network of devices, higher drive voltages improve the overall bit error rates (BER), at the cost of higher power consumption. However, realizing that voltage-mode capacitive HBC involves a capacitive load (unlike 50 Ohm radiative communication), we drive it in an adiabatic manner to significantly improve energy-efficiency. For this purpose, we include a symmetric 4-level capacitor-less adiabatic driver in 65 nm, achieving greater than 50% lower power w.r.t. CMOS drivers and 18 times lower area than SoA switched capacitor-based adiabatic designs.

title: AVOA-Based Calibration of an Enhanced Self-Correcting Battery Model for Digital Twin Integration
authors: Walid Merrouche, Boughiout Islam Abd Elsammed, Mahmoud S. Abouomar, Badis Lekouaghet, Hegazy Rezk
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: Digital twin (DT) technology plays a pivotal role in enabling real-time monitoring, predictive diagnostics, and control of energy storage systems within smart grids and electric mobility. The development of accurate battery twins critically depends on robust parameter identification of battery models under dynamic load profiles and varying thermal conditions. This paper investigates the application of the recent African Vultures Optimization Algorithm (AVOA) for calibrating the Enhanced Self-Correcting (ESC) battery model, which incorporates feedback and hysteresis effects. Validation is performed using experimental current–voltage data from an Urban Driving Cycle at four different ambient temperatures. Comparative analyses against Artificial PSO, GWO, and Plett’s original method demonstrate that AVOA achieves higher accuracy (RMSE of 4.5mV at 25°C), 20% faster convergence, and 12% lower computational cost. These results highlight AVOA’s suitability for real-time smart grid applications, enhancing battery DT fidelity and supporting the reliable integration of electric vehicles and distributed energy storage into next-generation smart grids.

title: Development of a Low-Cost Surface Characterization Prototype for Solid and Liquid Samples
authors: Nelson Cisneros, Jorge Cuadra, Carlos Rudamas
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: Atomic force microscopy (AFM) and electron microscopy (EM) are widely used for solid surface characterization at micro and nano scale, but their high cost, operational maintenance, and sometimes destructive nature limit accessibility, especially in resource-constrained environments. These limitations highlight the need for affordable, portable, and easy to maintain alternatives. Moreover, liquid sample surface analysis is also an emerging necessity in fields like water quality assessments and studies. Optical techniques combined with image-based analysis offer accurate measurements without the high costs of AFM or EM. By enabling reflectance-based imaging, such systems can also support environmental monitoring, providing non-invasive, quantitative analysis of pollutants. In this work, we present a low-cost optical prototype for surface characterization based on Fresnel interference analysis. The system integrates a diode laser, a CCD sensor, and an automated XY scanning platform to perform multi-angle reflectance measurements. The proposed non-invasive method was experimentally validated on TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> thin films, and its potential application for monitoring contaminants in water bodies is discussed.

title: Predictive Modeling of El Niño Impacts in El Salvador Using R and Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Joyce Posada, Carlos Alfaro
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: El Niño–Southern Oscillation (ENSO) strongly affects climate patterns and agriculture in El Salvador, often reducing maize yields through drought and irregular rainfall. This work presents a predictive modeling approach using R and machine learning algorithms to estimate maize production under different ENSO scenarios. Historical yield data from the FAO and climate indicators from NOAA were processed and integrated. Models including Random Forest, XGBoost, and Multiple Linear Regression were tested, with XGBoost delivering the highest accuracy. The results offer a reproducible tool to support agricultural planning and climate risk mitigation.

title: A Reconfigurable LP/BP/HP Current-Sensing Front-End Achieving 109.3-dB SFDR and Tolerance to Error Current and Sensor Parasitics with Adaptive Auto-Zeroing Scheme Embedded in SC-CMFB
authors: Yun-Hung Gao, Chun-Ho Fan, Junwen Li, Yuan-Ting Zhang, Ka Nang Leung, Kong-Pang Pun
abstract: We present a current-sensing front-end using a current-integrating (CI) reconfigurable low-pass (LP)/band-pass (BP)/high-pass (HP) noise-shaping successive approximation register (SAR) current-to-digital converter. Sharp, reconfigurable noise transfer functions (NTF) are achieved through simple timing control and with passive components. Regarding precise current digitization, a low-cost auto-zeroing (AZ) circuit, embedded in the switched capacitor common-mode feedback of the integrator, adaptively compensates for input parasitics and error current. The AZ scheme tolerates large input pair mismatch (±6400%) with minimal SNDR degradation (<5 dB) in all modes. Over a 500-Hz bandwidth, the design achieves a SNDR of 94.3/81.5/93.4 dB, an SFDR of 105.7/86.8/109.3 dB, and a DR of 148.8/104.2/103.1 dB with a dynamic CI gain control (and a DC current compensation circuit for the LP mode), while consuming 37.6/38.3/39.8 μW for the LP/BP/HP modes, respectively.

title: Operation Status Analysis of CVT Monitoring by Support Vector Machine
authors: Xu Chen, Chao Zhang, Fei Liang, Yu Yan, Yinzhe Xu, Xuhui Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: This paper proposes a operation status analysis method based on support vector machine to address the shortcomings of traditional methods in capturing early faults in the capacitive voltage transformer (CVT) online monitoring devices. A classification model based on support vector machine is constructed, used kernel function mapping to solve nonlinear problems, and optimized parameters to improve classification accuracy and generalization ability. Experimental verification was conducted on three types of states: healthy, sub healthy, and faulty. The results indicate that the constructed linear kernel support vector machine model exhibits high classification accuracy on the test set; The confusion matrix demonstrates the model's effective ability to distinguish three types of states, especially its sensitive recognition ability for early sub-health states; The ROC curve further confirms the excellent discriminative performance of the model; The classification graph based on PCA dimensionality reduction presents clear decision boundaries, verifying the robustness of the model in complex data. Compared with traditional static threshold methods, this method significantly improves the dynamic adaptability and early warning capability of operation status analysis, providing effective theoretical and technical support for maintenance decision-making of power equipment.

title: Composite Power Quality Disturbances Identification Based on Lissajous Trajectories and WATT-EffNet
authors: Ye Tian, Lei Shang, JinJing Yan, Wei Song, TaiYu Gu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: With the growing complexity of power systems driven by renewable energy integration, the exact Power Quality Disturbances (PQDs) recognition has become vital for sustaining the reliability of power grids. In this thesis, a new identification framework integrating Lissajous Trajectory (LT) visualization and a Wider ATTENTION EfficientNet (WATT-EffNet) is introduced. The proposed LT method transforms two-phase voltage signals (with 120° phase difference) into shape-distinct images, capturing disturbance patterns directly in the time domain without additional time – frequency transformation. Meanwhile, WATT-EffNet integrates width-expanded MBConv blocks and dual attention mechanisms to enhance feature learning efficiency under limited parameters. As shown by experimental results, the proposed method achieves 99.13% recognition accuracy under 20 dB SNR and maintains inference latency in the millisecond range, demonstrating both high precision and real-time performance. This work provides a new, interpretable pathway for intelligent PQD detection and visualization in modern smart grids.

title: An Enhanced Intelligent Server Monitoring System for Predictive Fault Detection and Triage Prioritization
authors: Maria A. M. Abdelsalam, Marwa Y. Mohamed, Ahmed A. M. Ibrahim, Alrahma F. G. Alnour, Abdelmajid M. A. Ali, Rashid A. Saeed, Mamoon M. Saeed, Rania A Mokhtar, Mohammed S. Elbasheir
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: The current digital structures and infrastructures require effective active monitoring solutions that would not be limited to the use of conventional threshold-driven solutions, which tend to create alert fatigue and overlook the signs of failure. The paper presents System Pulse AI an intelligent monitoring system that entails the integration of hybrid machine learning, automated triage, and anomaly detection, with real-time data collection. The system uses a lightweight agent to transport server performance data to a low-latency cache, where deviations are detected via a hybrid detector that combines an isolation forest and an autoencoder. A Random Forest model classifies detected anomalies into priority triage levels. The system showed near-perfect anomalous recall and excellent accuracy when validated on a large-scale dataset, with the triage classifier exhibiting remarkable explanatory power. Case studies validate the system’s efficacy in improving operational decision-making and early fault diagnosis, providing a significant improvement over the traditional reactive approach.

title: A Graph Matching-Based Method for Object-Level Topological Semantic Corridor Mapping and Localization
authors: Yuan Wang, Bin Zou
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To construct topological semantic corridor maps with objects as nodes, this paper proposes an object-level mapping and localization method based on 3D object perception. The approach uses semantic objects and path nodes to build a map integrating semantic and spatial information. Object nodes are obtained via a 3D detection model Omni3D, directly regressing object centers, scales, and semantic categories from monocular images, avoiding 2D detection plus depth estimation and improving geometric consistency. Path nodes are dynamically updated through object-level feature associations and motion constraints, forming a semantic topological corridor network. The resulting map accurately captures object layouts, supports reliable navigation, and emphasizes functional relationships and accessibility over purely geometric representation. The method is computationally efficient, suitable for resource-limited robotic platforms, and provides a scalable solution for indoor corridor navigation and localization.

title: Drone Use of Recycled Materials in Energy Technologies: Design, Applications, and Sustainability Analysis
authors: M. Sreekrishna, Baby Saral G, M. Chitra, Gopika GS
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The rapid advancement of drone technology has greatly influenced various industries, especially the energy production industry and infrastructure survey. Nevertheless, the production of drones entails materials and processes that lead to environmental wastes and depletion of resources. The study suggests recycled materials to be incorporated in drones and energy technology in order to enhance sustainable engineering practices. This paper investigates the possibility of drone designs that use recycled plastics, metals, and composite materials and looks at how drones can aid renewable energy infrastructure including solar power infrastructure and wind power infrastructure. Sustainability in lifecycle, energy efficiency and environmental benefits is also analyzed in the research. Empirical simulations of experimental designs and conceptual models show that drones made with recycles will lower the manufacturing cost by up to 18-25 percent and retain their structural integrity to carry out the energy infrastructure inspection functions. This paper has proved that sustainable drone engineering can be used to enhance circular economy in addition to better monitoring and maintenance of renewable energy systems.

title: Controllability and Structural Stability in Latent Diffusion Image Generation
authors: Shaoyi Guo, Yinan Xu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Latent diffusion models, such as Stable Diffusion, have become a dominant framework in visual AI generation. By conducting the denoising process in a compressed latent space, these models significantly reduce computational complexity while preserving high image quality. However, their structural design introduces both strengths and limitations in visual design applications. This paper analyzes the structural capabilities of latent diffusion models in terms of semantic alignment, compositional generation, and style control, as well as their constraints in layout precision, typographic rendering, and deterministic editing. By linking these characteristics to the underlying diffusion mechanism, we provide a mechanism-driven perspective on their role in contemporary design workflows and outline directions for future visual AI development.

title: Robust Point Cloud Registration via Adaptive Hierarchical Segmentation
authors: Jun Yang, Kangyou Cui, Weijun Wang, Chen Yang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: In the field of three-dimensional inspection and monitoring of large-scale industrial components, point cloud registration technology faces multiple challenges including large initial pose deviations, degraded geometric constraints, and low data overlap rates. Although the traditional Iterative Closest Point (ICP) algorithm performs excellently under ideal conditions, it is prone to falling into local optima when processing components such as wind turbine blades that exhibit significant length-towidth ratios and - surface features. To address these issues, this study proposes a robust registration method based on an adaptive hierarchical strategy. This method innovatively constructs a multidimensional attribute evaluation system, quantifying the sphericity and normal vector variation rate of point clouds through Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to characterize geometric complexity. Additionally, it introduces factors such as mirror symmetry measurement, overlap volume estimation, and point cloud density assessment to dynamically determine the optimal number of segments. Experimental results demonstrate that under extreme conditions including large-angle rotation (90°), axial sliding (20% of full length), and low overlap rate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(40 {\%})$</tex>, the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of the proposed algorithm remains at the sub-centimeter level, significantly outperforming mainstream registration algorithms such as RANSAC, FGR, and SANDRO. This research provides reliable technical support for high-precision three-dimensional reconstruction and deformation monitoring of large-scale complex industrial components.

title: A New Remaining Useful Life Prediction Method based on Physics Informed Transfer Learning under Incomplete Full Life Cycle Degradation Data
authors: Chaoge Wang, Xiangyi Meng, Ziyi Jia, Xiaojun Mei, Tao Xie, Feng Li
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: To address the challenge of predicting remaining useful life under incomplete data conditions in real industrial environments, this paper proposes a transfer learning approach based on physics informed neural network (PINN). The method first trains an encoder using complete source domain data to generate pseudo labels for the target domain. Next, it trains a PINN using complete source domain data and transfers the implicit partial differential equations learned by the network to the target domain. Finally, incomplete data from the target domain is input into the source-domain-trained PINN for remaining useful life (RUL) prediction under the guidance of the implicit partial differential equations. Testing on the Xi'an Jiao tong university dataset demonstrates that the proposed transfer method effectively achieves accurate RUL prediction with incomplete data, providing robust support for equipment fault prediction and health management.

title: Smart Medicine Reminder Box for Healthcare Monitoring Using IoT
authors: Nishanthini R, Sandhiya S, C. Rajasekaran
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Medication non-compliance continues to be a serious issue for elderly individuals and patients with chronic illnesses, frequently leading to reduced treatment effectiveness and greater reliance on caregivers. This paper presents a Smart Medicine Reminder Box designed using Internet of Things (IoT) technology to ensure timely and accurate medication intake. The proposed prototype utilizes an ESP32 microcontroller with integrated WiFi capability and controls servo-driven mechanisms to perform automated medication dispensing. along with audio visual alerts using LEDs and a buzzer. Medication schedules are synchronized using Network Time Protocol (NTP) to ensure precise timing without manual intervention. A mobile application enables caregivers to remotely monitor medication adherence and manage schedules in real time. Experimental evaluation demonstrates reliable dispensing accuracy, effective alert mechanisms, and stable cloud communication, making the system suitable for remote healthcare monitoring.

title: Design of an Integrated Reconfigurable HMSIW Band-Pass Filter Using Metamaterial Technology
authors: Aimen Faraj Abdelhamid, Adel Saad Emhemmed, Abdulbast Kriama
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: This paper presents the design and analysis of a compact reconfigurable Half-Mode Substrate Integrated Waveguide (HMSIW) bandpass filter incorporating Complementary Split Ring Resonator (CSRR) metamaterial structures and varactor diodes. The proposed filter operates over key sub- 6 GHz bands, including 5G n77, n78, n79, and the WLAN band. The filter is designed and optimized using HFSS, starting from a full-mode SIW structure and subsequently transformed into a compact HMSIW configuration. The integration of CSRR cells enables effective miniaturization and improved selectivity, while varactor diodes provide dynamic frequency tuning and WLAN interference suppression. Simulation results demonstrate low insertion loss below 0.5 dB and return loss exceeding $\mathbf{1 5 ~ d B}$ across the operating bands, confirming the suitability of the proposed design for reconfigurable 5 G and WLAN front-end applications.

title: Liquid-Assisted Contactless Adhesive Bonding: Experimental validation and 2D Multiphysics simulation
authors: Ahmed Abdelwahab, Zohreh Farmani, Hans Kuipers, Marcel Tichem, Jiajie Fan, G. Q. Zhang
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: Handling ultra-thin semiconductor dies during assembly remains challenging due to their mechanical fragility and sensitivity to placement forces. Contactless die transfer methods reduce mechanical loading and have potential to enable higher placement speeds and improved throughput but introduce challenges such as uncontrolled impact dynamics, die rebound, and misalignment during landing. This work investigates a liquid-assisted contactless adhesive bonding approach in which droplets deposited on stencil-printed conductive adhesive act as transient damping and alignment media during die landing. Dummy silicon dies with a gold (Au) surface finish were released from a short distance onto the adhesive. Four liquids with different physical and chemical properties—deionized (DI) water, isopropyl alcohol (IPA), acetone, and liquid flux—were investigated to evaluate the influence of key fluid parameters, including surface tension, viscosity, and density, on die impact dynamics, stabilization, and subsequent bonding quality. The results demonstrate that droplet material properties significantly influence die landing dynamics in liquid-assisted contactless assembly. Among the investigated liquids, deionized water enabled reliable die entrapment and damping. In contrast, for the lower-surface-tension solvents isopropyl alcohol (IPA) and acetone, the droplets were not sufficiently confined under the experimental conditions, and therefore no direct die entrapment was observed, although impact damping was still achieved. Die entrapment and stabilization behavior were analyzed using high-speed camera recordings and complementary two-dimensional simulations performed in COMSOL Multiphysics. Furthermore, the impact of the droplets on the resulting bond interface and joint quality was evaluated through post-bond characterization. The study provides insight into droplet-mediated die landing and offers guidelines for selecting liquids for reliable contactless micro-assembly.

title: Digital Health and the Internet of Things in Huntington's Disease Diagnosis and Management: A Systematic Review
authors: Leen Alhassan, Osama Hammad, Hanan Hirzallah, Yousef Alghzawi, Layla Amaireh, Faten Kharbat
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Huntington's disease (HD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder with detrimental motor, cognitive and psychiatric features, and is assessed with conventional methods, such as UHDRS, which are episodic and burdensome. The Internet of Things (IoT) has emerged as a potential diagnostic and management device capable of revolutionizing HD care as we know it. These Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA) guided systematic review identified 23 studies on IoT based interventions in HD, 74% of which were feasibility studies with some contributions from qualitative cross-sectional and case reports. Wearables were found in 10 studies, commonly tested as they can capture motor abnormalities through accelerometers and show moderate to strong agreement with polysomnography for sleep monitoring, although accuracy for nocturnal awakenings was limited. Telehealth achieved excellent reliability for remote, motor, scoring and improved care, access, and novel devices, such as hydrogel sensors and wireless platforms, demonstrated high accuracy as well in laboratory settings, but no real life findings. Feasibility and acceptability were high, but the findings' reliability were limited by small samples, short duration, and heterogeneous outcomes. IoT technologies show strong potential to support continuous monitoring and patient centered care in HD, but larger standardized validation studies are needed.

title: Web Application for Internship Management Using Laravel Framework
authors: Mohammad M. Qabajeh, Dua'a Ezz-Aldeen Jaber, Tawba Khalid Mashni
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Internships as a workplace learning approach are important in preparing students with functional and communicational skills for the labor market. This explains the interest of the high education institutions in employing different approaches to internship programs, including dual, sandwich, and classical. However, internships are not structured or standardized in terms of their outcomes and values. Also, managing huge numbers of interns per year is a challenge, and finding the suitable place of internship is hard for women and people with disabilities mainly in engineering. In the Palestinian context, implementing internships in universities and the private sector faces several challenges. First, students struggle to find suitable organizations for internships, and universities encounter difficulties in guiding them towards credible opportunities. Second, the monitoring of internships lacks standardized assessment criteria and progress tracking. Third, administrative tasks related to internships are excessively time-consuming because of the reliance on paper-based forms and letters from all parties involved. Additionally, faculty supervisors often find it impractical to conduct on-site visits because of time constraints and internship site regulations. To overcome these problems, a digital platform was established for students, universities, and stakeholders to manage, evaluate, and track internships. It is hoped that this platform will make it easier to integrate the stakeholders in the educational system and help the student, employer, and supervising academic to complete a successful internship program.

title: A Survey on Generative Models for RNA Design
authors: Abla Benaiche, Said Gadri
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Ribonucleic acid RNA plays an important role in biological processes. Therefore, its rational design is a major challenge in computational biology. Recent developments in deep learning have proposed generative models that can produce new RNA molecules with novelty and functional potential. This paper provides a review of recent models in RNA design. It starts with a background on RNA and the main categories of generative modeling. Then, existing studies are classified into four categories: sequence-based models, structure-based models, joint sequence-structure models, and function-oriented models. For each category, representative studies are summarized and compared in terms of architectures, datasets used, evaluation metrics, and reported results. The comparison highlights the contributions and distinct strengths of each approach. It also notes several limitations, such as limited data, modest structural validity, and limited functional improvement. Finally, the study discusses future directions, including incorporating RNA features and expanding and diversifying datasets. It also recommends improving multi-objective design through experimental feedback techniques. Overall, generative modeling appears to be a growing paradigm that could accelerate discoveries and therapeutics based on RNA and synthetic biology.

title: An Approach to Explainable Text Summarization for News Articles
authors: Waseem Nazmi Al-Qawasmi, Andrea Corradini
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: With the expansion of digital news content, readers often struggle to find relevant and meaningful information quickly. While traditional summarization tools can condense articles effectively, they typically offer little insight into how decisions are made. This can reduce user trust and limit the understanding of the generated content. This study introduces a news summarization model that combines the power of modern natural language processing (NLP) with Explainable AI (XAI) techniques to improve both summary quality and transparency. Built on the BART framework, the model includes components for pre-processing and summary generation. It uses the attention mechanisms and feature importance methods to show which parts of the text influence the summary, making the process more understandable. Efficiency trade-offs are also considered to ensure the model is practical for real-world editorial settings. Results show that the model generates coherent and accurate summaries, with strong semantic alignment to original texts, as measured by ROUGE scores. SHAP-based explanations further enhance transparency by highlighting which words contributed most to the final summary. Although the explainability process can be computationally demanding, it is well-suited for offline use in newsrooms. This work demonstrates that it is possible to build summarization tools that do not just work well but also explain how they work. The approach offers a promising path forward for making AI more accessible and trustworthy in journalism.

title: Dynamic Investment Decision Analysis for Carbon Capture Economic Models
authors: Zanning Wang, Haoyue Zhang, Weiyue Feng, Sitong Ren
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: Against the backdrop of an accelerating transformation in the global energy structure, China's thermal power sector faces significant pressure to reduce emissions. Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS) technology represents a key pathway for low-carbon retrofitting of thermal power plants. However, its economic feasibility and technology selection are dynamically influenced by multiple factors, including CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> concentration, carbon market prices, policy subsidies, and operational conditions. In response to the complexity and dynamic nature of investment decisions regarding this technology, this study develops an integrated system model combining economic analysis with convex optimization decision-making. Through sensitivity analysis, key factors affecting net present value (NPV) are identified, and an economic evaluation index system is established, encompassing total cost, total revenue, NPV, internal rate of return (IRR), and break-even carbon price. Based on this, an integer linear programming model is formulated, aiming to maximize NPV or minimize annual total cost, while incorporating constraints such as CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> concentration adaptability and capture volume requirements, thereby enabling joint optimization of capture technology and equipment configuration. This research provides a scientifically quantifiable tool for investment decision-making on carbon capture systems in thermal power plants, demonstrating strong engineering application value.

title: DDPG-Based Swing Suppression Control for Quadrotor Payloads
authors: Chengen Zheng, Bangsheng Fu, Xiaoyan Gu, Mingyi Gong
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The position tracking, attitude stabilization, and payload-swing suppression of a quadrotor with a suspended load, this paper proposes, within a double-loop control framework, a hybrid control strategy that integrates an Optimal Input Shaper (OIS) with the Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) algorithm. position loop preprocesses the desired trajectory using an Optimal Input Shaper (ZVD type), and, combined with a fuzzy PID that dynamically adjusts the weighting of the position error and its derivative, effectively suppresses payload swing caused by the vehicle's motion. To address the trajectory-tracking delay and attitude-instability issues introduced by the input shaper, a DDPG-based dynamic compensation mechanism was designed to effectively compensate the torque outputs of the Active Disturbance Rejection Control (ADRC) attitude controller. This compensator uses a dual-objective weighted reward function that jointly considers tracking accuracy and swing suppression, and is trained via the DDPG reinforcement learning algorithm to prevent extreme control responses in the suspended-load quadrotor system. Results show that the proposed scheme performs excellently in disturbance rejection, attitude stability, and payload-swing suppression.

title: Deep Learning Action Recognition Method Based on Transfer Learning and Data Augmentation
authors: Xuanwei Hu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Human action recognition has important application value in human-computer interaction, intelligent surveillance, behavior analysis and other fields. Traditional machine learning-based methods rely on manually designed visual features, which not only have high development costs but also limited generalization ability in complex scenarios. Although convolutional neural networks have achieved remarkable progress in visual tasks, they still suffer from the risk of overfitting in human action recognition tasks, and the recognition accuracy needs to be further improved. To address the above problems, this paper proposes an automatic human action classification method based on the convolutional neural network framework, integrating transfer learning and data augmentation strategies. Experimental results on the KTH Action Recognition Dataset show that the proposed method achieves 56.67% accuracy, which is significantly better than the 46.67% of the comparison method, verifying the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method.

title: Leveraging Hybrid Learning Model for Violent Recognition in Surveillance Videos
authors: Hajer A. Altaweil, Kenz A. Bozed
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Violence Recognition in Surveillance Videos is a critical and challenging task in Deep Learning and Computer Vision, forming a key component of intelligent security systems designed to automatically detect and analyze Violent Activities. The complexity arises from diverse Human Behaviors, Dynamic Environments, and Overlapping Motions that make it difficult for conventional Models to differentiate between Violent and Non-Violent actions. To address these challenges, this dissertation a Hybrid Learning Model Framework that combines spatial and temporal analysis for improved accuracy and robustness. This Hybrid Model combines a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) with a Bidirectional Convolutional Long ShortTerm Memory (BiConvLSTM) network. The VAE compresses visual features into low-dimensional latent representations while retaining essential spatial information, and the BiConvLSTM captures bidirectional temporal dependencies and spatial coherence across Frames. Experimental Evaluation on the UCFCrime Dataset demonstrated high Classification performance, achieving a micro-average AUROC of 93% and outperforming several baseline approaches. Overall, the Hybrid Learning Model (VAE-BiConvLSTM) Framework enhances automated Violent Recognition in surveillance Videos, offering a reliable and scalable foundation for intelligent monitoring systems and future security applications.

title: Walsh-Function Frequency Division and Event-Triggered Consensus Control for Discrete-Time Electromagnetic Formation Flight Systems with Input Saturation
authors: Wenbo Chu, Yuanhua Ni, Zhongxin Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Electromagnetic formation flight (EMFF) offers a propellant-free paradigm for close-proximity multi-satellite co-ordination, yet three fundamental challenges hinder its practical deployment: (1) the fully coupled, nonlinear nature of intersatellite electromagnetic interactions renders direct decentralized control intractable; (2) physical actuator constraints impose hard input saturation on the magnetic dipole amplitudes, threatening system stability; and (3) continuous inter-satellite communication is prohibitively expensive in resource-constrained space environments. To address these issues, we propose WET-EMFF (Walshfunction Event-Triggered EMFF), a unified control framework with three key innovations: (1) a Walsh-square-wave frequency-division scheme that decouples multi-satellite electromagnetic interactions and provides a coefficient-level peak-constraint guarantee superior to sinusoidal shaping; (2) a discrete-time averaged double-integrator model derived from the decoupled dynamics, which forms a tractable plant for controller synthesis; and (3) a Lyapunov-based distributed event-triggered consensus controller that explicitly handles input saturation while reducing communication transmissions by up to 86.6 % relative to time-triggered schemes. Simulation results on a four-satellite formation verify global consensus convergence and the effectiveness of all three components.

title: Implementation of Optimized Quasi-Z Source Converter for Solar Charging Stations
authors: M. Raja Nayak, M. Sita Maha Lakshmi, U. Tejaswini, V. Manoj, A. Akash, T. Siva Nagendra Kumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: This study provides the MAT Lab Simulation implementation and analysis of Quasi-Z source converter with emphasizing on optimized impedance circuit parameters to arrive enhanced potential capacity for solar charging stations application. This optimized approach aids in enhanced converter operation without relaying any additional complex control equipment's and strategies. Quasi <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{Z}$</tex> source converter claims a specialized converter due its inherent buck boost capability in single stage, reducing component complexity and improving efficiency compared to conventional converter systems. Also, a proportional-integral (PI) controller-based converter was designed and compared to assess the potential of the implemented quasi-Z source converter. To assess the efficiency of the implemented design, the. Simulation results reveal that appropriate design implementation of the impedance network parameters include <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$L$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C$</tex> aids in converter performance improvement while minimizing the necessity on additional conventional controller approaches. The proposed method of approach streamlines the network architecture, mitigating controlling complexity, and offers a reliable and economical resolution for high-performance converter operation. Finally, this work emphasis the augmented performance of optimized controller free impedance network compared to controller integrated quasi converter while maintaining potential THD remains with in the IEEE standards.

title: Impact of thermal gap filler vs. thermal gap pad materials on the solder joint lifetime of eWLB packages in topside cooling setups
authors: Martin Niessner, Peter Lutz, Walter Hartner, Harald Preu, Juliane Junesch, Holger Mai
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: The impact of thermal gap filler (TGF) materials, specifically when used in topside cooling configurations (TSC), on the solder joint reliability of electronic packages is analyzed both experimentally and by simulation. A setup is used which specifically eliminates the impact of PCB mounting in order to separate the effects of the TGF material and the PCB mounting. An embedded wafer-level ball grid array (BGA) package (eWLB) is used as a test vehicle. Compressive tests at different temperatures and at different force levels are used to identify which solder material model is able to produce quantitatively reasonable results under compressive loading. The compressive tests demonstrate that solder balls can withstand higher compressive forces at room temperature, e.g. during TSC assembly, without starting to deform. Characterization of the TGF materials shows that these materials exhibit relaxation, which is not reported in the supplier datasheet, and which will reduce the topside force loading on the package after initial assembly. Compared to thermal gap pads (TGP) studied in previous work, the two TGF materials investigated in this work are up to a factor 9x stiffer. Solder joint reliability tests show that TGF materials can reduce the lifetime of the solder balls of the eWLB package. The magnitude of the reduction depends on both the stiffness of the TGF material and the dispensing volume. Simulation is able to reproduce correctly the impact of the increased stiffness of the TGF materials.

title: IoT-Enabled Fault Detection in Power Electronic Converter Systems Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: T. Thirunavukkarasu, S. Jayakumar, M. Ganesh Kumar, M. Dharshanick, P. Hariharan, S. Gowtham
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Due to quick development in fields such as renewable energy, electric vehicle charging, and industrial automation, the demand for reliable fault diagnosis and condition monitoring of intelligent power conversion systems has received significant attention. This paper proposes an Internet of Things (IoT)-based machine learning framework for predictive fault detection in power electronic converter systems through the use of voltage, current, and temperature sensor signals. The ultimate goal is to enhance converter reliability by the early identification of nonstandard operating states, supported via a cloud-linked communication architecture for continuous remote monitoring. To date there is a fundamental research gap that needs to be filled to integrate multi-sensor acquisition, data preprocessing, feature extraction and fault classification as part of a coherent smart monitoring structure for converter applications. To solve this problem, the system integrates a series of methods such as signal filtering and normalisation, statistical feature extraction, anomaly detection, and fault classification such as overvoltage, overcurrent and thermal stress. The model is implemented and assessed through a simulation environment to study system performance during normal operating conditions as well as faulty ones. In terms of numerical results, the proposed architecture increases fault detection rate without introducing a delay in system response to abnormal events while giving a better converter's health status. This paradigm is ideal for smart energy ecosystems, electric mobility platforms, industrial drives, and remote maintenance applications, where reliable operation, minimal downtime, and intelligent decision support are paramount.

title: Physics-of-Failure Based Reliability Investigations of Copper Sintered Die Attaches for Wide Band Gap Power Electronics
authors: J. Heilmann, P. Wittwer, J. Schuh, C. Grosse-Kockert, A. Stelzer, D. Busse, M. Abo Ras, B. Wunderle
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: New generations of power modules incorporating new semiconductor materials like silicon carbide (SiC) or Gallium nitride (GaN) feature higher operating temperatures. These operation conditions have challenging requirements for the die attach material to withstand the damage resulting from the large temperature cycles. In recent years, sintered silver (SAG) has established itself as the preferred material for chip mounting in power electronics, offering resistance to high operating temperatures and for replacing solder. However, the current trend is towards sintered copper (SCU). This material is more cost-effective, has an even higher melting point, and does not require precious metal plating. Consequently, increasingly aggressive organic substances are required to produce a suitable sintering paste, and the cleaning of the copper metallization prior to sintering is becoming increasingly important. This necessitates a significantly better understanding of the materials and processes, as well as more extensive reliability studies. In this paper, we present a newly developed SCU-paste-based die attach solution produced using a state-of-the-art and industrially relevant process. We validate its interfacial and structural quality using our isothermal bending tester (ITBT), which is renowned in the community for its significantly faster reliability and quality testing results. Furthermore, thermal and electrical conductivity of the SCU are measured and evaluated.

title: Three different mowing machines on Kentucky Bluegrass: evaluation of the operative and qualitative performances
authors: Lorenzo Gagliardi, Marco Fontanelli, Giuliano Sciusco, Tommaso Federighi, Simone Magni, Marco Volterrani, Christian Frasconi, Michele Raffaelli, Sofia Matilde Luglio
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Maintaining a consistent mowing schedule at optimal heights promotes turfgrass growth, reducing the risk of disease and enhancing quality and performance parameters, whether in residential lawns, public parks, or sports fields. In this regard, a comparative analysis of operative performances of three different mowing machines was conducted on a Kentucky bluegrass (Poa pratensis L.) mature sward in S. Piero a Grado, Pisa, Italy. The treatments corresponded to three different types of mowing systems: two different autonomous mowers and a ride-on mower. Data were collected and analyzed on the operative performances and turfgrass quality. The rideon mower demonstrated to require substantially less time (0.60 h per week) to mow a 1,600 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> area, but both AM1 and AM2 mower required substantially less primary energy (respectively 2.09 and 1.22 kWh•week<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>) to complete the same area. In addition, the results suggest that while the ride-on mower achieved a slightly lower turfgrass height (2.53 cm), the quality of the turfgrass was generally higher in plots managed by the autonomous mowers (6). These results underscore the potential of autonomous mowing systems as a sustainable alternative, balancing energy efficiency and turf quality with longer operational times.

title: 5G-Enabled Intelligent Transportation Systems: V2X Architectures, Emergency Vehicle Applications, Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: Vineela Madireddy, Pranavi Konam, Deepak Reddy Pesarakayala, Anirudh Munukuntla, Deekshith Reddy Itikyala
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Urban traffic congestion is one of the major factors that influence the emergency response services mainly the ambulances, fire vehicles, and other emergency rescue services, which require swift movement in overcrowded traffic areas. Conventional traffic management systems usually do not offer effective routing of emergency cars because of the fixed signal control and its inability to give real time coordination of the traffic. The paper introduces an intelligent transportation system based on 5G and Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) Communication with a smart traffic management system to enhance the movement of emergency vehicles. This system is proposed with the help of real-time communication between vehicles, adaptive traffic control of the traffic lights, and route optimization, which is dynamic and provides the most appropriate route in diverse traffic conditions. Dijkstra algorithm is used to calculate optimal routes using a shortest-path routing mechanism and constantly updating traffic information using connected infrastructure. Evaluation of the proposed framework by means of a simulation based on the Simulation of Urban Mobility (SUMO) illustrates that the framework lowers the travel time, intersection waiting time and latency of emergency response significantly than the conventional methods of routing. The findings point to the value of the next-generation communication networks and intelligent transportation technologies that are likely to advance emergency mobility, enhance the coordination of the traffic and aid in the evolution of effective smart city transportation systems.

title: Tool-Use Failure Analysis and Recovery Framework for LLM Agents
authors: Jiayi Jiang, Chuan Li, Junjie Ma, Kun Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: As large language models (LLMs) become increasingly common in handling complex tasks that require external tools and services, the various issues that arise when calling tools have become a major factor affecting system stability and usability. This article addresses the situation where LLMs fail to learn or botch the use of tools, and proposes a practical solution for identifying and resolving such issues. The key actions taken include: First, we established a framework to categorize all cases of tool learning failures and developed a tester capable of intentionally inducing faults; Then, we proposed a runtime recovery framework called Monitor-Classifier-Recovery (MCR), which includes simple rules, a fault classifier capable of learning, and a strategy library that considers costs; Then, we introduced the MAGRPO method, which involves multiple agents optimizing strategies through a process similar to a multi-player rock-paperscissors game. During training, agents collaborate and can compensate for each other's shortcomings. We tested this approach through a combination of writing and programming tasks, such as format errors, timeouts, returning garbled characters, and sequential errors in planning. Using MCR alone significantly accelerated problem resolution and reduced human intervention. Combining MCR and MAGRPO boosted the success rate for complex tasks from 42 % to 86.7 %, with a problem resolution rate of 92.6 %, while also optimizing API costs and speed. The article also discusses whether recovery strategies can be combined like building blocks, how to balance costs and benefits, how to model API behavior in the real world, and ethical reviewthese are engineering and research challenges. This approach provides a clear path for LLM systems to become more reliable, easier to understand, and efficient when using tools in practice.

title: Extended Scene Description using Relationship Information for Manipulating 3D Reconstructed Data
authors: Chae-yeong Song, Dong-hun Lee, Chaewon Moon, Aro Kim, Sang-hyo Park, Suhyeon Lee, Jinwoo Jeong, Sungjei Kim
abstract: In this paper, we identify a critical limitation in the MPEG-I Scene Description (SD) format: the absence of a native mechanism for representing semantic relationships between objects. To address this, we propose a relationship extension framework that enables both static and dynamic inter-object semantics within 3D scenes. The proposed solution includes two integration strategies: (1) embedding relationship information within existing extensions such as scene interactivity and scene dynamic, and (2) introducing external relationship extensions that preserve the integrity of the core SD specification. We provide a concrete syntax and demonstrate the utility of our approach through applications in scene reconstruction and ISOBMFF-based integration. The results show that both options enable context-aware manipulation and efficient restoration of inter-object relations, supporting advanced interactive and media synchronization scenarios. We propose further evaluation and adoption of these extensions to enhance interoperability and backward compatibility in future MPEG standards.
