publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)
abstract: Effective Information Technology (IT) Governance, Risk Management, and Compliance (IT GRC) is crucial for organizational success, yet achieving a holistic view remains challenging. Construction companies, particularly in dynamic environments like Indonesia, face unique IT risks related to distributed operations, project complexity, and increasing regulatory demands. Existing frameworks like COBIT 2019, focusing on governance and management objectives, and the NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF), emphasizing cybersecurity risk management, offer significant strengths but often operate in silos. This paper proposes a synergistic IT GRC architecture that integrates COBIT 2019 and the NIST CSF to provide a more comprehensive and unified approach. The architecture development involved analyzing both frameworks and identifying key integration points. Its applicability and potential benefits were explored through an illustrative case study conducted at a construction company in Indonesia. The proposed architecture facilitates a clearer mapping between governance processes and security controls, potentially enhancing risk visibility and streamlining compliance efforts. The case study provides initial insights into the practical relevance of the integrated approach within the construction sector context

title: LCL Filter Design for Single-Phase PV Grid-Tied Inverter based on PR Controller
authors: Linda Hassaine, Nihad Asfirane, Haithem Boukerdoun, Mohamed Rida Bengourina, Bilal Taghezouit, Lotfi Badi
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: This paper presents the design and control strategy for a single-phase grid-connected inverter, aimed at integrating photovoltaic (PV) energy into the utility grid. The inverter system includes a proportional-integral (PI) controller for DC- link voltage regulation, a proportional-resonant (PR) controller for current control, and a phase-locked loop (PLL) for synchronizations with the grid. An LCL filter is employed to attenuate high-frequency switching harmonics. The system is simulated using MATLAB/Simulink, and control strategy implementation is carried out using the LAUNCHXL-F28379D development board. Simulation and experimental results confirm the performance of the control strategy in terms of voltage regulation, harmonic reduction, and synchronization accuracy.

title: Performance Monitoring of a Dual Axis Sensor Based Solar Tracking System using IoT
authors: Sakina Atoui, Said Boudjana, Yousra Ouail, Loubna Kherrour
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: This study investigates enhancing performance monitoring of a dual axis sensor based solar tracking system STS using internet of things IoT. An IoT connected model is used to reduce energy consumption, maintain costs, and utilize energies more efficiently. By using the user interface, the user can initiate himself to solve any problems due to environmental conditions. Physical devices have sensors that send data to a centralized platform. The energy management system is used to control, monitor and optimize the performance of the production system. The monitored data are electrical parameters including current, voltage and supplied power. Real-time IoT monitoring is implemented and tested.

title: Precision 3D Mapping of Forests and Crops Using a Visual-LiDAR System Mounted on a Rover
authors: Laura Chilin, Sebastiano Chiodini, Federico Toson, Carlo Bettanini, Adam Młynarczyk, Marcello Coradini, Carmelo Maucieri, Francesco Pirotti, Giacomo Colombatti
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Autonomous exploration and mapping technologies are becoming increasingly important tools for environmental monitoring, especially in forested and semi-structured natural environments. Within the European E-FORESTER project, a modular and adaptable visual–LiDAR system has been developed to support ecological data collection through robotic platforms. The SILVA rover served as experimental platform for testing perception, navigation, and 3D reconstruction capabilities in unstructured terrain. The results demonstrate the feasibility of using multimodal sensing for terrain understanding, environmental monitoring.

title: AGROPATROL-A Multi-Functional Terrain Roaming Bot for Field Surveillance
authors: Sivakami S, Inbarasu A, Magesh V, Mithun Srinivasan J
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Agro Patrol is an intelligent multi-functional terrain-roaming robot developed to address critical agricultural challenges such as labour shortages, the increasing cost of operation, inefficient use of pesticides, and the absence of real-time monitoring of the crops. The system combines soil moisture sensors, pesticide detector modules, GPS positioning, and high-resolution imaging to enable continuous and dynamic tracking of the field. Unlike conventional static sensing systems and drone-based monitoring, Agro Patrol performs close-range inspection and physical intervention through terrain-adaptive mobility and intelligent obstacle avoidance. An AI-driven sensor fusion framework processes real-time environmental, soil, and visual data to accurately identify crop stress, pest infestation, and irrigation requirements under varying field conditions. The precision spraying mechanism ensures targeted chemical application, significantly reducing pesticide wastage, soil contamination, and environmental impact. The communication pipeline made possible by IoT can be remotely monitored and supported in real-time to enhance irrigation planning and crop protection even when the farmland is large. Agro Patrol can provide a next-generation, efficient, and sustainable solution to precision agriculture with better productivity, crop quality, and long-run profits for farms with enhanced energy control, modular architecture, and scalable design.

title: Mobile Multi-Camera System for 3D Fruit Tree Scanning and Apple Size Estimation
authors: Jordi Gené-Mola, Jochen Hemming, Eduard Gregorio
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Three-dimensional modeling of fruit trees is necessary for implementing precision agriculture strategies in orchard management. Among the available technologies, photogrammetry-based techniques have proven effective for reconstructing accurate dense point clouds using high-resolution handheld cameras, but their manual image acquisition limits scalability in commercial settings. To overcome this limitation, we present a low-cost mobile multi-camera system to automate image acquisition for orchard 3D reconstruction. The multi-camera (MultiCam) system was built using eight synchronized RGB cameras and evaluated against a previously validated handheld photogrammetric methodology. Both systems were used to scan apple trees in an experimental orchard, and their resulting point clouds were compared in terms of geometric quality and performance in fruit size estimation. Reconstruction was performed using Structure-from-Motion and Multi-View Stereo pipelines. Apple diameters were estimated using four methods, with the least squares sphere fitting approach providing the most accurate and robust results. Despite the lower resolution of the MultiCam images, the system achieved comparable accuracy in fruit diameter estimation to the handheld approach, with a mean absolute error of 5.1 mm (vs. 4.5 mm) and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.91 (vs. 0.93). The proposed solution significantly reduces acquisition time and simplifies the scanning process, offering a practical and scalable alternative for in-field fruit monitoring using photogrammetry.

title: A cylindrical electrical and ultrasonic multi-imaging system based on piezoelectric composites
authors: Akira Kimoto, Yui Adachi
abstract: In this paper, an ultrasonic and electrical multiimaging system which consists of sixteen piezoelectric composites is developed. This system makes it possible to reconstruct both ultrasonic and electrical images of the living body since the piezoelectric composite has low acoustic impedance as one of the characteristics. Ultrasonic waves are measured by piezoelectric composites which are directly contacted to the living body, and the electrical conductance is measured by using the surface electrodes of piezoelectric composites. From their measured values, ultrasonic and electrical images are respectively reconstructed. In addition, the information of the living body is clearly detected since the electrical imaging is improved by using the ultrasonic image. In the experiment, the ultrasonic and electrical images at the living body model which plaster was inserted in $0.1 \% \mathrm{NaCl}$ solution tank were demonstrated by the developed multi-imaging system. The system was evaluated.

title: AdaptiPID: Model-Aware Adaptive Gain Tuning with Anti-Windup Compensation and Multi-Joint Coordination for Robust Robotic Manipulator Control
authors: Yuehang Zhou
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Achieving precise and robust joint-space control of multi-degree-of-freedom robotic manipulators remains challenging due to three fundamental limitations of conventional PID approaches: (1) fixed gain matrices that cannot accommodate configuration-dependent inertia and Coriolis variations across the full workspace, (2) integrator windup under actuator saturation that provokes severe post-recovery overshoot and destabilises high-speed trajectories, and (3) decentralised joint-level loops that lack awareness of Jacobian-mediated inter-joint coupling, causing Cartesian tracking residuals to accumulate undetected. To overcome these limitations, the author propose AdaptiPID, a unified adaptive PID framework that integrates three targeted innovations: (1) a model-aware gradient-descent gain adaptation law grounded in the Euler-Lagrange manipulator dynamics and a composite ITAE-energy cost functional, enabling online continuous adjustment of proportional, integral, and derivative gains; (2) a back-calculation anti-windup mechanism with a co-designed time constant that discharges the integral state proportionally to saturation depth while preserving steady-state disturbance rejection; and (3) a Jacobian pseudoinverse coupling compensation term that maps Cartesian end-effector residuals into joint-space corrective torques, closing the gap between decentralised control and whole-arm Cartesian accuracy. Comprehensive numerical simulations on a 6-DoF serial manipulator benchmark demonstrate that AdaptiPID reduces RMSE by 61.0 % over Classic PID, limits overshoot below 10 % across all tested speeds, recovers from impulsive disturbances within 0.44 s, and suppresses long-term cumulative error by 78 %, while retaining a real-time computation cost of 2.3 ms.

title: Ultra-Thin Flexible 2.5GHz Antenna Using Kapton for Biomedical Application
authors: V. Lavanya, S. Deepa, Kavina S, Rahini G, Ritiha A, Saranya P
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The ultra-thin 2.5 GHz flexible antenna using Kapton as a substrate and a microstrip feedline is proposed for biomedical applications. It is designed to comfortably transmit human vital data from the body to a monitoring device. The antenna uses Kapton polyimide as the substrate, which is known for its high flexibility, high thermal stability, and low cost. The proposed antenna provides a bandwidth at 2.5 GHz even when bent at a radius of 14.9 mm and at 2.4 GHz in the flat condition. Over the operating frequency range, the maximum gain of 1.95 dB is obtained for the bent antenna, while the unbent antenna exhibits a gain of 1.07 dB. From the experimental results, it is concluded that the antenna successfully transmits data even when it is bent.

title: LA-EDPO: Length-Aware Effect-Enhanced Direct Preference Optimization
authors: Wenhao Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This paper investigates the phenomena of reward hacking and likelihood drift encountered during the alignment of large language models with human preferences. Existing Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) methods suffer from the linear accumulation of implicit rewards relative to sequence length, which biases models toward generating structurally redundant content to achieve inflated scores, thereby inducing reward hacking. Furthermore, influenced by the asymmetry of divergence constraints, models frequently exhibit likelihood drift during the alignment process, resulting in insufficient confidence in high-quality responses. To address these challenges, we propose Length-Aware Effect-Enhanced Direct Preference Optimization (LA-EDPO). Specifically, we introduce a Length-Aware Module which, by introducing an adaptive length regularization mechanism, achieves mathematical decoupling of reward density, shifting the optimization objective from maximizing cumulative rewards to maximizing the marginal information gain per token, thus effectively suppressing redundant generation. Furthermore, we propose an Effect-Enhanced Module that constructs an asymmetric implicit reward paradigm, introducing prior support from the reference model to provide positive gradient momentum. This mitigates the likelihood drift trend while maintaining distribution anchors, thereby driving a more effective alignment optimization. Experimental results on mainstream benchmarks such as Arena-Hard-Auto and AlpacaEval 2.0 demonstrate that LA-EDPO achieves significant improvements in both raw win rate and length-controlled win rate. Without compromising fundamental model capabilities, this method substantially increases the information density and alignment quality of responses, proving its superiority and robustness as a general-purpose preference alignment method.

title: Characterization of Materials for 3D Printing by FDM Method
authors: Gilberto Carrillo, Manuel Cardona, Mauricio Gómez
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: This work analyzes the mechanical behavior of three thermoplastics commonly used in additive manufacturing using fused deposition modeling (FDM): PET-G, PLA, and ABS. Tensile tests were performed on specimens with three infill percentages ($\mathbf{1 0 0 \%, ~} \mathbf{8 0 \%}$, and $\mathbf{5 0 \%}$) at a loading rate of $\mathbf{1 0 ~ m m} / \mathbf{m i n}$, recording displacement and load using a universal testing machine. The results indicate that PET-G has the highest tensile strength, followed by PLA and ABS. A positive correlation between infill percentage and mechanical strength is also confirmed, with full infill $(100 \%)$ providing the best structural performance. These findings contribute to the optimization of FDM printing parameters for applications requiring high mechanical integrity. The procedure used includes the preparation of specimens with different infills, applying load at a speed of 10 mm per minute, recording the displacement and load data provided by the universal testing machine. The graphs of the data obtained show that the material with the best resistance of the three under study is PET-G, followed by PLA and in third place is ABS. Additionally, it is observed that the level of resistance of the materials depends on the infill, being best when it is $100 \%$, followed by $80 \%$ and finally when it is $50 \%$ percent, being significantly different from the theoretical and computer simulation values.

title: A 184mW, 61fps Image Signal Processor for Spatial-Temporal HD Video Inpainting
authors: Tai-Jung Chen, Yao-Kai Yang, Jun Nishimura, Chia-Hsiang Yang
abstract: Video inpainting reconstructs masked regions with preserved spatial structure across frames, and has been widely used for object removal and segment restoration. However, maintaining temporal consistency results in excessively high computational complexity. This work presents the first image signal processor for real-time spatial–temporal video inpainting. The design reduces computation by exploiting video codec motion information to eliminate temporal redundancy, and applies non-local frame selection and LUT-based layer mask generation to simplify the the computations for the copy-and-paste neural network. Algorithm–architecture co-optimization enables efficient hardware mapping, including adaptive processing elements that skip zero data and reduce latency by 92%. Additionally, the LUT-based layer mask generation and block-wise packing schemes further reduce latency and enable efficient memory access, and shared hardware that reduces block matcher area by 46%. With optimizations applied to both the algorithm and the architecture, the overall latency is reduced by 99%. Fabricated in 40-nm CMOS, the chip achieves 61 fps for HD videos at 184 mW. It delivers 325× higher throughput with 387,951 × [16,121 ×] higher energy [area] efficiency than a consumer-level GPU.

title: Developing the Eddy Current Testing System for the Corrosion Evaluation of Steel Rebar and Steel Cable
authors: Li He, Dongfeng He, Makoto Watanabe
abstract: We developed a compact system to evaluate the corrosion of steel rebar or steel cable using the eddy current testing method. In this system, an 80 kHz current was sent to the excitation coil to produce the AC magnetic field; a detection coil was used to detect the signal produced by the eddy current induced by the AC magnetic field in the steel rebar or steel cable. A differential-type detection coil was used to minimize the influence of environmental noise and the background signal generated by the excitation coil. A lock-in amplifier was used to get the same-phase signal and a $\mathbf{9 0}$-degree phase-difference signal, and an X-Y graph was plotted using these two signals. From the slope of the X-Y graph, the corrosion of steel rebar or steel cable can be evaluated. To make the system portable, the excitation coil, the detection coil, the amplifier, the lock-in amplifier, and the AD converter were all put in a small box with a length of about 17 cm, a width of about 8.5 cm, and a height of about 6 cm. The probe was connected to a single-board computer of the Raspberry Pi 4 Model B for data acquisition, data processing, and results displaying. Some indoor measurements and field experiments of corrosion detection and steel cable corrosion evaluation of cablestayed bridges were done using the system.

title: SE-YOLOv8: An Enhanced Lightweight Object Detection Framework for UAV Imagery
authors: Yanshen Wu, Libin Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Object detection in Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) imagery struggles to balance efficiency and representational capacity amid extreme scale variations, occlusion, and background clutter. To address this, we propose SE-YOLOv8, an enhanced lightweight framework based on YOLOv8n. We integrate the Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) mechanism to adaptively recalibrate deep channel features, emphasizing salient targets and suppressing noise. Furthermore, we investigate the underlying compatibility between attention networks and regression losses. Evaluations on the VisDrone-DET 2019 dataset reveal a critical feature distribution conflict: attention's variance-amplifying nature intrinsically clashes with the non-monotonic dynamic focusing penalty of Wise-IoU (WIoU), degrading performance. By resolving this via CIoU loss, SE-YOLOv8 achieves 43.57% mAP@0.5 (+0.76% over the baseline). It consistently outperforms variants like ECA and CBAM while maintaining strict computational efficiency and demonstrating unparalleled robustness against severe occlusions and micro-scale targets, providing both a robust UAV perception solution and essential theoretical insights into module-loss compatibility.

title: Comparative Analysis of Loss Functions for Vein Segmentation using U-Net and Attention U-Net
authors: Djamaa Nour Zidane, Zine Eddine Hadj Slimane
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Cyber Physical Systems (AICPS)
abstract: Vein segmentation using deep learning techniques is an important step in biometric recognition systems. Although many studies have explored the use of deep learning architectures for this task, limited attention has been given to the role of loss functions in guiding model training and influencing segmentation accuracy. In this study, we present a comparative analysis of nine single and combined loss functions, applied to U-Net and Attention U-Net architectures, on two public hand vein datasets, CASIA and FYO. First, we generate ground truth masks, using standard preprocessing techniques. Second, we trained our models with nine different loss functions to segment the dorsal and palm veins. Finally, segmentation performance was evaluated using Intersection over Union (IoU), Dice coefficient, precision, recall, and accuracy. Our results reveal that the loss function has a significant impact on model performance and segmentation quality, with combined loss functions generally outperforming single ones. Notably, the Attention U-Net trained with BCE-Tversky Loss achieved the highest overall accuracy and segmentation quality.

title: ICISES 2025 Author Information Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Ships and Electromechanical System (ICISES)

title: Advanced DSOGI PLL-Based Grid Synchronization for Onboard EV Charger Converters
authors: Prakrati Sahu, Jagdish Koli
abstract: The Grid-connected power electronic converters increasingly suffer from power quality degradation and poor dynamic performance when operating under distorted grid voltages, frequency deviations, and load disturbances. Conventional control strategies are unable to adequately reject these grid non-idealities, leading to significant input current distortion and a slow transient response. This paper presents a simulation-based performance comparison of conventional control, Second-Order Generalized Integrator (SOGI) based control, and a proposed Double Second-Order Generalized Integrator Phase-Locked Loop (DSOGI-PLL) based control for a single-phase grid-connected converter. The system is evaluated under practical normal grid conditions and dynamic load variations using MATLAB. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed DSOGI-PLL-based control achieves near-sinusoidal input current, reduces current THD by up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$96-97 \%$</tex> under distorted grid conditions, and improves transient settling time by approximately 70% compared to conventional control. The results confirm that the proposed control strategy provides an effective solution for enhancing power quality and dynamic performance in grid-connected applications.

title: MG-VLQA: Multi-Granularity Quality Assessment for Image Compression via Visual Language Models
authors: Hanfei Li, Anle Ke, Jiawen Gu, Chao Zhou, Tong Chen, Zhan Ma
abstract: Despite significant advances in image compression, existing evaluation metrics remain poorly aligned with human visual perception-particularly under extremely low bitrates, where reconstructed images often suffer from abstract distortions or semantic degradation that are difficult for conventional metrics to capture. To address this limitation, we propose MG-VLQA, a novel multi-granularity quality assessment framework that leverages VisionLanguage Models (VLMs) to evaluate image reconstruction fidelity through the lens of semantic consistency with the original caption. Our method formulates a suite of captionderived questions spanning three complementary dimensions: (1) entity presence (semantic completeness), (2) detail fidelity (local appearance accuracy), and (3) inter-entity interactions (relational coherence). By simulating human-like perceptual judgment via VLMbased question answering and semantic similarity scoring, MG-VLQA provides a more interpretable, fine-grained, and perceptually relevant assessment of compression quality. Extensive experiments across multiple datasets and codecs demonstrate that our metric achieves higher correlation with human judgment and offers superior discriminative power.

title: Retinal Image Analysis Using a CNN–InceptionV3 Hybrid Network for DR Identification
authors: Abhay Pratap Singh Bhadauria, Harsh Mishra, Shashi Bhushan
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy is a major complication of diabetes and can lead to total vision loss. Early detection of DR is critical to management, however the reliance on ophthalmologists and complexity of early-stage diagnosis requires a degree of automated screening. We present a Convolutional Neural Net- work and Hybrid InceptionV3 models for the classification of diabetic retinopathy. We use the APTOS Blindness Detection dataset, which contains retinal images, as well as 5 classes of diabetic retinopathy severity. Our work includes data collection and preprocessing, model architectures, model training, and the comparison of the two models. Our findings show promising results in regards to model accuracy and loss, showing the models have effectively learned and are generalizing. The confusion matrices also provide critical analysis of the models. The CNN model achieved an accuracy of 79% and the InceptionV3 followed closely with an accuracy of 84% on the APTOS dataset. The proposed automated system provides an application for early- detection classification of diabetic retinopathy, relieving demand for the health care professionals and quality of diabetic retinopathy.

title: A 92.5 TOPS/W Fully-Analog Multi-Layer Computing-in-Memory for State-Space Models in 28nm CMOS
authors: Laith A. Shamieh, Wei-Chun Wang, Narasimha Kidambi, Saibal Mukhopadhyay
abstract: An SRAM-based Fully Analog STate-space Compute-in-Memory (FAST-CIM) is demonstrated for State Space Models (SSMs). The all-analog SSM includes a recurrent-state-update CIM cascaded with feedforward CIMs, maintaining signals in the analog domain across macros with a single digitization point. A 28 nm CMOS test-chip demonstrates compute efficiency up to 92.5 TOPS/W and compute density of 28.8 TOPS/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: Fog Computing for Intelligent and Sustainable IoT: Architectures, Optimization, Datasets, and Open Challenges
authors: Prachi Chaturvedi, Shahnawaz Ahmad, Arvind Mewada
abstract: The limitations of centralized cloud computing in IoT applications, which require low latency, high bandwidth, and mobility, have been demonstrated through the growth of the IoT ecosystem. Fog computing eliminates these limitations by distributing computing, storage, and networking closer to data sources across the edge-cloud continuum. This survey summarizes recent work on fog computing in terms of architectural design, resource management and scheduling; AI-based optimization; security/privacy issues; and energy efficiency. The survey also shows that fog systems can help reduce latency and bandwidth for real-time analytics in intelligent cities, healthcare, vehicle-to-vehicle networks, and industrial IoT.

title: MEEGET 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)

title: Efficient Drying of Onions and Garlic Using a Smart Greenhouse Solar Dryer
authors: Nadia Metidji, Mohand Berdja, Ouassila. Badaoui, Houda Tassoult
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: Industrial drying of agricultural products is an essential post-harvest process. It aims to remove moisture from products to prevent spoilage, reduce their weight for easier transport, and extend their shelf life. However, this process is known to be energy-intensive. Due to the rising prices of fossil fuels and increasing environmental concerns, there's a strong drive to shift towards renewable energy sources for drying operations. Solar energy stands out as a particularly attractive alternative due to its abundance and sustainability.In this study, a forced-convection solar dryer was developed. This device consists of a drying chamber where agricultural products are placed. Solar collectors heat the air, which is then circulated through the drying chamber by fans, accelerating the drying process. This process accelerates drying and contributes to obtaining higher-quality products compared to traditional direct sun drying.The controlled drying conditions help reduce nutrient loss, preserve color, and minimize contamination, leading to a high-quality dried product. This system also reduces the carbon footprint compared to fossil fuel-dependent drying. These dryers can be adapted for a wide range of agricultural products, including fruits, vegetables, and herbs.

title: Enhancing PV Array Output Using Pyramid Sudoku Technique Under PSCs
authors: G. Meerimatha, D. Niyaz, N. Keshava, D. Abdul Hameed, S. Abdul Rehman, A. Kiran Kumar Reddy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Partial shadowing circumstances significantly impair the performance of photovoltaic (PV) arrays due to mismatch losses and the existence of several local power peaks. The primary objective of this work is to boost global maximum power extraction in large-scale PV arrays under PSCs by using an enhanced static reconfiguration technique. To achieve this, a novel mapping strategy based on Pyramid Sudoku is proposed, which improves shadow distribution over conventional Sudoku dispersion methods by including a hierarchical constraint. Five Four Pyramid shading scenarios are utilized to simulate and evaluate a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9 \times 9 \text{PV}$</tex> array with a fully crosstied topology using MATLAB/Simulink. In contrast to standard Sudoku configurations, string-parallel, bridge linked, and total crosstied, in comparison to string-parallel, bridge linked, total cross-tied, and traditional Sudoku arrangements, the simulation results show that the suggested configuration offers up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8}-\mathbf{3 0 \%}$</tex> improvement in global peak power and considerable decrease in mismatch losses. The results verify that for large-scale PV systems operating in partially shaded situations, the Pyramid Sudoku mapping efficiently improves shadow dispersion and offers a scalable static reconfiguration option.

title: Safety-Aware Uncertainty Estimation and Risk-Boundary Inference for Lightweight Drivable-Area Segmentation
authors: Zhurong Tan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Reliable perception is critical for downstream motion planning, yet most drivable-area segmentation systems output deterministic masks that may become overconfident and unsafe under adverse conditions (e.g., fog, blur, glare, and sensor noise). This paper presents a lightweight, safety-aware perception framework that augments segmentation with uncertainty maps and a risk-aware boundary output. Built upon an efficient encoder-decoder backbone, we instantiate epistemic uncertainty via Monte Carlo dropout and deep ensembles, and further improve probabilistic reliability through post-hoc temperature scaling. We derive pixel-wise predictive entropy and mutual information to localize uncertain regions, then construct conservative risk boundaries by uncertainty thresholding followed by morphological erosion, producing safety-oriented drivable masks suitable for constraint-aware planning. Experiments on a controlled synthetic benchmark with multiple corruption types and multiple random seeds show that uncertainty-aware inference substantially reduces OOD performance degradation compared with deterministic baselines, while calibration consistently improves expected calibration error without sacrificing segmentation accuracy. Qualitative results confirm that uncertainty concentrates around ambiguous boundaries and occlusions, and the proposed risk-boundary mechanism effectively suppresses high-risk areas to yield more conservative and interpretable outputs.

title: Optimized LZ77 Compression Using Match Expansion and Search Space Pruning
authors: Pinaki Shankar Chanda, Siddhant Baldota, Bin Wu
abstract: Dictionary-based compressors such as Deflate, ZLIB, GZIP, and XP10 [1] improve compression ratios using LZ77 lazy matching with a Delayed Match Window (DMW). However, conventional lazy matching performs redundant comparisons across all DMW positions, incurring high CPU cycles, memory access, and latency costs. We propose an algorithm that detects the best match early, prunes unnecessary comparisons using proven bounds, and preserves optimality and bit-exact compatibility with regular LZ77 algorithm.

title: Reinforcement Learning Driven Adaptive Sensing Strategy for Battery Aware IoT Devices
authors: E D Kanmani Ruby, Ali Hussein Ali Abu Zaid, M. Tamilselvi, Rakesh V S, P. Deepthi, Ramya M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Battery efficiency is a fundamental challenge in the deployment of large-scale Internet of Things (IoT) systems, particularly in energy-constrained environments. Conventional sensing strategies, such as fixed-rate and duty-cycled approaches, often fail to adapt to dynamic power availability and contextual relevance of data, leading to rapid energy depletion or suboptimal sensing. To overcome this drawback of artificial intelligence, this paper presents RAS-BIoTNet, a new reinforcement learning-controlled adaptive sensing device to autonomously control sensor activities according to the realtime energy estimates, priorities of sensing activity, and operational conditions. The system uses Graph-Temporal Convolutional Networks and Bayesian LSTM to predict energy with great accuracy and Hierarchical Actor-Critic model to formulate optimal sensing policies in the event of multi-objective reward structures. The experimental findings indicate that RAS-BIoTNet is a better choice to use than the baseline strategies, as it increases battery lifetime by 27%, minimum task delay by 38.5%, and sensing accuracy by more than 96.3%, even when the situation is energy-critical. The model suggested will guarantee improved uptime of the device, enhance the quality of data and its responsiveness in real-time. This study opens the doors to smart sensing of the next-generation IoT ecosystem, in which the resource constraints play a vital role. The ability to adapt to the changing surroundings is the highlight of the system, where it can be widely applicable to smart cities, environmental monitoring, wearables in healthcare, and industrial IoT.

title: Adaptive Single Image Dehazing Algorithm Based on Sky Ratio
authors: Mingjing Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address atmospheric light estimation bias and transmission imbalance in traditional dark channel prior under large sky-ratio scenes, this paper proposes an adaptive single image dehazing algorithm based on sky ratio. By introducing sky segmentation and dynamic weighting mechanism, atmospheric light and transmission are adaptively optimized. Experimental results on RESIDE dataset demonstrate significant improvement in PSNR and SSIM, especially in high sky-ratio scenarios.

title: Techno-Economic Analysis of Solar-Driven Reverse Osmosis Desalination: PV vs. CSP Comparison
authors: Muetaz Mohammed
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Water plays a vital role in meeting fundamental human needs for life and health, as well as supporting social and economic development. However, about 97.5% of the Earth’s water is found in the oceans and is unsuitable for drinking, agriculture, and most industrial applications. Desalination has therefore become one of the most important technologies for converting saline water into freshwater. This study presents a comparative techno-economic assessment of photovoltaic (PV) and several concentrated solar power (CSP) technologies, including solar tower, parabolic trough, and linear Fresnel systems, for seawater desalination. Each solar configuration is modeled and simulated using the System Advisor Model (SAM) to supply the electrical energy required by a reverse osmosis (RO) desalination plant. The performance of the proposed systems is evaluated based on key economic indicators, with particular emphasis on the levelized cost of water (LCOW). The results show that the PV-based system provides the lowest-cost option for supplying electricity to the RO process, making it the most economically attractive solution for solar-driven desalination among the technologies considered.

title: Enhancing Stability in Islanded Microgrids: AI-Based Detection and Control of Islanding Events
authors: E Vani, S Dhivagar, S Senthil Kumar, V Thirumoorthi, S Aathi, C Suriya Prakash
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy sources has significantly transformed modern power systems, driving the widespread adoption of microgrids. Although microgrids enhance reliability and operational flexibility, they present essential challenges concerning unintentional islanding conditions. Islanding occurs when a microgrid is accidentally decoupled from the main utility grid while distributed generators continue powering local loads, posing severe threats to system stability, equipment security, and protection coordination. To overcome these challenges, this paper contributes an AI-driven framework for accurate islanding detection and adaptive control in microgrids, supported by real-time IoT-based monitoring. The proposed solution utilizes a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) architecture to analyze voltage, current, and frequency data, successfully distinguishing between true islanding events and normal transient disturbances. Upon detection, a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) agent dynamically regulates voltage and frequency using distributed energy resources, energy storage systems, and controllable loads. The system is validated through MATLAB/Simulink simulations under various fault and load conditions, and its practical feasibility is demonstrated using an ESP32 microcontroller prototype. The obtained results demonstrate the framework's superior performance by achieving higher detection accuracy, reduced false tripping rates, and enhanced post-islanding stability compared to traditional methods. Specifically, the proposed system successfully maintains the frequency well-controlled at 60 Hz, keeps the voltage near 1.0 p.u., and ensures high power quality with near-zero voltage Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) during transitions.

title: IoT-Enabled Smart Rescue Robot with Integrated MQTT Communication and Python-Based Realtime Human Body Detection for CSSR Missions
authors: Sheeba Santhosh, Indhralekha M, Hema Shree T, G Hari Krishnan, Harini Devi M, G. Aloy Anuja Mary
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Collapsed Structure Search and Rescue (CSSR) operations require rapid, accurate, and personnel-safe victim detection in environments compromised by poor visibility, structural instability, and toxic materials. Conventional methods-trained rescue personnel and canine units-face critical limitations in confined debris voids, hazardous atmospheres, and extended mission durations. This paper presents an IoT-enabled Smart Rescue Robot system that integrates an ESP32-CAM imaging module, L293D motor driver, DC geared motors, and a Wi-Fi-based MQTT communication protocol to achieve wireless real-time image acquisition and transmission from disaster environments. At the control station, a multi-stage Python image processing pipeline applies Gaussian noise filtering, histogram equalisation, Canny edge detection, and contour-based feature extraction to identify human body patterns from received frames. An optional YOLOv5-based deep learning module provides confidencescored object detection with Intersection over Union (IoU) threshold validation. A three-tier decision framework classifies detections as ignored <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<60 \%)$</tex>, suspicious <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(60-80 \%)$</tex>, or confirmed (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>80 \%$</tex>), triggering automated rescue team alerts with GPS location logging. Experimental evaluation across six simulated CSSR scenarios demonstrates an overall detection accuracy of 87.4 %, precision of 91.2 %, recall of 85.6 %, F1-score of 88.3 %, and mean detection response time of 2.3 seconds. The system significantly reduces personnel risk while improving victim detection throughput, and is designed for cost-effective, scalable deployment, including future thermal imaging and swarm robotics extensions.

title: Multi-Sensor Fusion-Based Composite Map Construction
authors: Gang Bai, Shuo Kai, Wenli Li, Yongfei Xiao, Yongguo Zhao, Hao Wu, Zhengguang Ma
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address issues such as insufficient mapping accuracy, limited semantic representation, and weak navigation adaptability of mobile robots in enclosed complex environments like tunnels, this paper proposes a composite map construction scheme integrating multi-sensor data with semantic grids. As critical infrastructure in the transportation, mining, and water conservan cy sectors, tunnels present challenges in inspection and maintenance operations due to high environmental risk factors and substantial labor costs, urgently requiring mobile robots to replace manual labor for autonomous tasks. For the safe and efficient operation of mobile robots in tunnels, accurate identification of targets such as personnel, equipment, and obstacles is indispensable to support obstacle avoidance, path planning, and task decision-making. First, a multi-sensor fusion framework is established. Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) is employed to fuse data from the Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU), laser odometer, and wheel odometer. Factor graph optimization is integrated to eliminate cumulative errors, achieving high-precision pose estimation. Second, the YOLOv13 object detection algorithm is introduced to extract environmental semantic information. Object categories, locations, and confidence levels are mapped onto the grid map constructed by the LiDAR, generating a composite map that integrates both geometric structure and semantic features to enhance environmental description capabilities.

title: LUIST-DETR: A Lightweight Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Infrared Small Target Detection Algorithm Based on RT-DETR
authors: Yongcheng Wang, Chaofeng Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This paper presents LUIST-DETR, a lightweight variant of the RT-DETR model tailored for infrared small target detection on unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). To accommodate the computational limitations of edge deployment, we integrate PRConv modules into the backbone for efficient feature extraction, simultaneously employ an optimized multi-scale neck network SSFF, and adopt the Focal-Wise-MPDIoU loss function to improve bounding box regression under challenging infrared conditions. Evaluated on the HIT-UAV dataset, LUIST-DETR achieves 82.5% mAP50 and 53.6% mAP50:95, with only 13.7 million parameters and 44.0 GFLOPs, surpassing baseline models such as RT-DETR-R18 and YOLO variants in both accuracy and computational efficiency while maintaining real-time performance. Ablation studies and comparative analyses further validate the complementary effects of the proposed modules, which effectively reduce missed detections and false positives—making the model well-suited for applications in surveillance and traffic monitoring.

title: Path Planning for Autonomous Vehicles Based on an Improved RRT* Algorithm
authors: Lidong Qi, Weidong Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address global path planning for autonomous vehicles in complex environments, an improved RRT* algorithm based on heuristic sampling is proposed. A bidirectional search is first employed to quickly obtain a feasible initial path, which is pruned using a greedy strategy to remove redundant points and determine its length for constructing an initial elliptical sampling region. The improved RRT* performs sampling within this region, incorporating goal-biased sampling and artificial potential field guidance to enhance convergence. Parent reselection and rewiring are conducted within a dynamic circular neighborhood to further optimize the path. Finally, redundant points are removed. Simulation results in MATLAB under various scenarios, evaluated using a normalized weighted composite metric, demonstrate that the proposed method generates efficient global paths effectively and rapidly.

title: Adaptive Participant Grouping to Enhance Personalized and Privacy-Aware Federated Learning in the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT)
authors: Nagarjun Thanneeru, Manasa Thotla, Navadeep Santosh Pampana, Manoj Reddy Adla, Jashwanth Reddy Vireddy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) allows training privacy-applications on a large number of distributed nodes, but it suffers as there is no IID approach, unreliable federated training, and ineffective personalization in healthcare IoMT devices. A framework on adaptive participant grouping is proposed and combines the FedProx regularization, the use of trust in the weighted averaging among participants and the process of differential privacy and MAML based personalization into a multimodal federated structure. Federated members are dynamically formed into three classes of high-, medium-, and low-trust according to the consistency of updates and the stability of validation in which low-trust nodes can consist of noisy nodes. The results of the framework were tested on five rounds of communication with multimodal medical data, with the results showing that the framework attained an accuracy score range from 89 % to 96 % when the model is federated trained. The adaptive grouping method increased client trust reliability because it delivered 21 percent better trust score stability than standard federated aggregation methods. Findings indicate better strengthen, adaptation reliability control, and privacy protection in distributed healthcare (systems).

title: Machine Learning–Based Geospatial Analysis for Disaster-Resilient Renewable Energy Systems
authors: D. Ganesh, M. Sunil Kumar, R.Sujith Reddy, P. Sangeetha, R.Satish Kumar, P. Dileep
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Nowadays, the rising cases of natural disasters such as hurricanes, earthquakes, floods, etc. have led to severe destruction in relation to human life, infrastructure, and the economy. In traditional practices of analyzing disasters, the techniques have mainly relied upon past events. In this consideration, the study aims to analyze the past data of disasters to determine the behaviour of disasters in association with their geographical distribution over time. Machine learning approaches are used to improve the analysis in the study, whereby the Long Short-Term Memory model is utilized to analyze the dynamics of disasters over time, and the DBSCAN algorithm is used to analyze the geographical areas prone to disasters. By integrating the analysis of data with the visualization of geospatial analysis, the solution promotes how historical disaster data can be used to provide insightful information. The solution highlights the potential of combining machine learning analysis with geospatial analysis to enhance preparedness and identification related to disaster risk management.

title: Machine Learning-Based Toxicity Prediction of Industrial Chemicals Using Tox21 Database: Toward Applications in Epichlorohydrin, Acrylonitrile, and Styrene-Based Polymers
authors: Weiming Duan, Qi Zhang, Yinzi Shao, Fangwei Xue
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This study develops a machine learning pipeline for predicting compound toxicity using the Tox21 database, targeting applications in industrial polymer chemistry. The pipeline integrates Fisher score-based feature selection, SMOTE oversampling, cross-validated grid search, and post-hoc threshold tuning to address severe class imbalance. Four classifiers-improved Random Forest, SVM-RBF, SVM-Linear, and AdaBoost-are compared across all 12 Tox 21 endpoints. The improved Random Forest achieved the highest AUC of 0.903 on the primary NR.AhR target, and threshold optimization improved sensitivity by up to 230 percent. Multi-target evaluation yielded mean AUC exceeding 0.74 for all classifiers. The pipeline provides a foundation for toxicity screening of epichlorohydrin, acrylonitrile, styrene, and their polymeric derivatives.

title: Fallguard-Det: an Efficient Fall Detection Algorithm Based on Deep Learning
authors: Feng Lin
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Fall detection is a critical yet challenging task for the safety of the elderly. To address the limitations of manual monitoring and wearable sensors, this paper presents FallGuardDet, an efficient fall detection framework based on YOLOv12. Our approach introduces three key innovations to handle drastic posture variations and complex background interference: (1) a Multi-Scale Human Posture Perception (MSHPP) module to fuse cross-level features for diverse scale perception; (2) a Dynamic Posture-Aware Attention Mechanism (DPAM) to adaptively focus on fall-related regions and mitigate occlusions; and (3) a Key Posture Feature Enhancement (KPFE) module to refine human contour representation and reduce misidentifications. Experimental results show that FallGuard-Det outperforms mainstream object detection methods in both accuracy and inference speed, demonstrating its superior effectiveness for realtime practical applications.

title: A Fault Diagnosis System for Aerospace Bearings Based on LSTM-RNN and Envelope Spectrum Analysis
authors: Zhiyuan Gu, Ziwei Zheng, Yunlong Wu, Shouzhao Sheng
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Aviation rolling bearings are critical components in aerospace machinery, and their operational integrity directly influences system performance and safety. Due to harsh operating conditions, these bearings are susceptible to wear and failure, necessitating timely and accurate fault diagnosis. This paper proposes a fault diagnosis and classification system based on Long Short-Term Memory Recurrent Neural Networks (LSTM-RNN) combined with envelope spectrum analysis. A dataset comprising 10 fault types with a sampling frequency of 12 kHz is used. Envelope spectra are constructed to extract corresponding amplitude and statistical features. These features are used to generate envelope-based sample sequences, which are divided into training, validation, and test sets. Both single-label and multi-label classification strategies are implemented using LSTM-RNN architectures. Multi-class performance is assessed using the Kappa coefficient, and classification results are visualized via t-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE). The classification accuracy and effectiveness under different sample sizes are further analyzed through accuracy-sample size curves. The proposed method demonstrates high classification accuracy and robustness, offering a promising solution for intelligent fault diagnosis in aerospace bearing applications.

title: An Optimal Control Model for Vehicle Path Planning and Motion Control Based on TD3 Algorithm with Multi-Dimensional Penalty Mechanism
authors: Ruotao Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address the optimization problem of vehicle path planning and motion control under complex terrain conditions, this paper proposes an optimal control model integrating a multidimensional penalty mechanism with the TD3 (Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient) algorithm. Based on the vehicle's state parameters in the Global Coordinate System (GCS) and Body Coordinate System (BCS), the model constructs a multiobjective reward function system including distance penalty, energy consumption penalty, collision penalty, wheel angle penalty, action smoothness penalty, and motor power constraint. By clarifying the mapping relationship between vehicle state quantities (coordinates, attitude angles, velocities, etc.) and action quantities (acceleration, steering angle), combined with the impact of terrain on vehicle speed, a vehicle motion equation is established. Meanwhile, to meet the requirements of path planning efficiency, safety, and control stability, a hierarchical penalty mechanism is designed: single-step and global distance penalties guide the path toward the target; energy consumption penalties related to rolling friction, aerodynamic drag, and gravity work achieve energysaving optimization; collision penalty ensures driving safety; wheel angle and action smoothness penalties reduce control mutations; and motor power constraint ensures the rationality of power output. The optimal strategy is solved based on the TD3 algorithm, and the planning results and control effects are intuitively presented through visualization methods. Verification shows that the model can efficiently generate vehicle motion strategies that balance optimal path, minimum energy consumption, and stable control under complex terrain and multiconstraint conditions, providing a reliable solution for vehicle autonomous navigation and intelligent control.

title: Data Fusion Methodology for a Hyperspectral-LiDAR System
authors: Marco Bignardi, Nikos Tsoulias, Burawich Pamornnak, Dimitrios S. Paraforos
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: The demand for sustainable farming and climate change are driving the use of advanced monitoring techniques in the industry. Precision Agriculture (PA) meets this need by using sensor-based systems to optimize resource efficiency and crop yields. Fruit crops such as grapevines require detailed monitoring of plant morphology and physiology, necessitating high-resolution data. LiDAR scanning provides accurate 3D reconstruction for carbon and biomass estimation, while hyperspectral imaging offers spectral data related to plant physiology and biochemistry. However, no sensor is infallible when used in isolation. This article therefore suggests using a fusion system combining hyperspectral imaging and LiDAR to generate spectral 3D point clouds. Geometrical calibration using a chessboard pattern was used to synchronize the sensors so that spectral data can be projected onto 3D geometry. The system was tested in laboratory setting, demonstrating the possibility to fuse the data from the two devices to produce a "spectral point cloud".

title: Effect of variable blade pitch on the aeroacoustic performance of a Darrieus rotor
authors: Guermache Ahmed Chafik, Talamalek Ayoub, Khalfallah Smail
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: Small-scale vertical-axis wind turbines are designed for installation in urban areas or on building rooftops. However, people in the vicinity of these turbines may experience noise pollution caused by the rotor during operation. The noise emissions are primarily associated with the interaction between the rotor blades and the turbulent airflow, referred to as aerodynamic noise, which includes airfoil self-noise and blade tip noise. Additional noise sources originate from mechanical components, such as bearings, vibrations, supporting arms, and the rotating shaft. This mechanical noise can be reduced through effective mechanical engineering design. In contrast, aerodynamic noise can be mitigated using flow control methods or shape optimization processes. The present paper aims to evaluate the aeroacoustic performance of a dynamic blade-pitched Darrieus vertical-axis wind turbine using numerical simulations. Computational fluid dynamics (CFD) is employed to calculate the rotor's aerodynamic characteristics, while the acoustic levels are determined using the Ffowcs Williams and Hawking equations. These two disciplines are fully coupled within the commercial software ANSYS Fluent, meaning the acoustic field is computed based on the fluid properties during the simulations. The two-dimensional CFD model is validated using available published experimental data on the power coefficient, while the computational aeroacoustic (CAA) model is validated through comparison with a three-dimensional case. The appropriate source correlation length (SCL) is determined, and it is found that this parameter correlates well with the blade span. Study findings indicate that blade pitch amplitude affects power coefficients and acoustic emissions. For power generation, ± 25° is recommended as pitch amplitude, which does not influence the acoustic emission. Results also show that as tip speed ratios increase, more noise is generated. Dynamic blade-pitched Darrieus rotor emits a lower amount of wake noise than fixed-blade Darrieus rotors, which is justified by the highest vortex emission in the case of fixed blade pitch. Generally, the present paper contributes to understanding the impact of variable blade pitch on power extraction and noise reduction.

title: Megawatt-Scale Filter Design for Off-Grid Fuel Cell Systems
authors: Khlid Ben Hamad, Mohamed Salem, Mohamed A Alsharif
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: An LCL filter for a megawatt-scale off-grid fuel cell (FC) power system supplying a local mixed resistive-inductive load was designed and evaluated for performance in this study. The proposed systems integrates an LCL filter that serves as an interface between a 3-level neutral-point-clamped (NPC) inverter and the load. Due to inverter switching and the presence of inductive and nonlinear loads, the inverter output inherently contains significant harmonic components, which can adversely affect power quality and sensitive high-power equipment. Systems designed for off-grid high-power applications must comply with the specified harmonic limits in standards such as IEC 61000-3-6 and IEEE 519. A 2 MW FC stack that operates at 1400 V DC supplies the off-grid inverter, and the stack is regulated to deliver 1.8 MW and 380 kVAr active and reactive power respectively at a 600 V line-to-line voltage. The design of the LCL filter allows attenuation of the generated current and voltage harmonics by both the inverter switching actions and the connected load, ensuring compliance with the aforementioned standards. A MATLAB/Simulink environment was considered for the modelling and simulation of the complete system, including the fuel cell stack, three-level inverter, LCL filter, and off-grid load. The evaluation portrayed that the proposed LCL filter can mitigate the THD for the output voltage and output current to 0.46% and 0.74%, respectively; the overall system efficiency of the system was also noted at 94%. These results suggest the suitability of the proposed filter design for megawatt-scale off-grid fuel cell applications requiring high power quality.

title: The Analysis of Research Status of Intelligent Unmanned Cluster Based on CiteSpace
authors: Ziyun Shen, Jialong Zheng, Shaofeng Lin
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This study elucidates the research landscape of intelligent unmanned clusters by leveraging CiteSpace to conduct a visual analysis of the literature published between 2018 and 2026. Through this approach, it systematically delineates the field's intellectual structure and evolutionary trajectory, offering a comprehensive overview of its development. Five core thematic clusters are identified: Unmanned System Platforms, Cooperative Control Mechanisms, Swarm Intelligence Algorithms, Tactical Operational Applications and Resource Management. The results reveal that the first three clusters constitute the enduring technical foundation of the field. Crucially, timeline and burst detection analyses indicate that Tactical Operational Applications, driven by defense imperatives, have emerged as the predominant research frontier and primary driver of growth in recent years. The findings also highlight the increasing integration of data-driven methods, such as reinforcement learning, and the nascent yet significant rise of edge computing to address real-time data processing demands. This study provides a clear, empirical map of the field's structure and dynamics, offering valuable guidance for researchers to identify frontier topics and avoid redundant efforts, while supporting strategic decision-making for technology policy and interdisciplinary collaboration.

title: The Environmental Dimensions of Artificial Intelligence: A Multifaceted Challenge
authors: Simena Dinas, Jaime Andrés Cardona Montero, Brandon Eduardo Tobar Sánchez, Leidy Camila Cadena Vanegas, Alejandra María Rodríguez Guarín
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: This article aims to elucidate the various pollutionrelated dimensions arising from the rapid adoption of artificial intelligence (AI). The devouring amount of energy required for its operation, the devastating water consumption, the growing volume of pollution generated by solid and chemical waste in water sources, air and land and the noise generated by cooling systems and power generation, are just some of the impacts evaluated. Beyond the debates concerning its appropriate use or its ultimate benefit to human development, the present analysis intends to demonstrate that the current outcomes in terms of sustainability are alarming. The environmental footprint of AI is examined through a tripartite framework: the underlying infrastructure, the computational processing, and the resultant outputs.

title: Advanced Fault Detection and Classification in Distribution Systems Using PMUs and Optimized SVM & KNN Algorithms
authors: D. Gutiérrez-Umaña, A. Mejía-Dubón, L. Agüero-Peralta, O. Núñez-Mata
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: This paper presents an advanced methodology focused on fault detection and classification in distribution systems using real-time phasor measurements from virtual $\mu$ PMUs and optimized machine learning algorithms. A modified IEEE 24-bus feeder is simulated in OPAL-RT’s HYPERSIM, incorporating directional overcurrent protection (50/51 and 67). SVM and KNN models are enhanced through feature selection and hyperparameter tuning offline via scikit-learn, with dimensionality reduction using PCA. Results demonstrate significant improvements in classification accuracy and speed for single-, double-, and three-phase faults, validating the use of high-resolution synchrophasor data to support advanced protection in modern distribution networks.

title: An Explainable Pipeline for Automated Detection and Grading of Diabetic Retinopathy
authors: Guillermo Calderón, Carlos Alfaro, Cesar Espinoza
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a leading cause of preventable blindness worldwide. This paper presents an explainable deep learning pipeline for the automated detection and grading of DR from retinal fundus images. Using the APTOS2019 dataset, we fine-tuned pre-trained convolutional neural networks, with VGG16 achieving the best performance (F1-score of $\mathbf{9 8. 2 \%}$ for binary classification and $\mathbf{8 0. 2 \%}$ for multiclass). To enhance interpretability, we combined Grad-CAM variants with anatomical segmentation of retinal structures. Furthermore, we generated automated clinical descriptions by integrating segmentation masks, activation maps, and a large language model (GPT-4). The proposed pipeline provides a transparent, end-to-end solution for DR screening that aligns with clinical workflows and could support early detection in practice.

title: Comparison Between TLS and viDoc RTK for High Resolution Geometric Characterization of Hazelnut Plants
authors: Raffaella Brigante, Laura Marconi, Sara Zollini, Simona Lucia Facchin, Chiara Traini, Alessandra Vinci, Daniela Farinelli
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: The geometric characterization of fruit trees plays a key role in modern orchard management, supporting agronomic practices such as vigor assessment, irrigation optimization, and canopy management.This study presents a comparative analysis of two surveying techniques applied to the geometric characterization of hazelnuts (Corylus avellana L.) plants, with the aim of evaluating their effectiveness in capturing plant-related morphological information for applications in orchard monitoring and precision agriculture. The first method employs Terrestrial Laser Scanning (TLS), which provides high-density and high accuracy point clouds. The second method uses an iPhone combined with the viDoc RTK (Real Time Kinematic) Rover, a mobile photogrammetric solution capable of generating georeferenced point clouds in real time through RTK GNSS integration. The two point clouds, derived respectively from TLS and iPhone with viDoc acquisitions, were processed and compared to extract and evaluate key morphological parameters, including tree height, canopy area, and canopy volume.Quantitative comparisons were carried out both at the point cloud level and on the extracted geometric descriptors. The results show a good overall agreement: height differences were within a few centimeters, while canopy area and volume discrepancies were less than 10%. Variations are attributable to differences in point density, occlusions, and the level of structural detail captured by each method.These findings highlight the potential of smartphone-based RTK photogrammetry as a lightweight, portable, and flexible solution for vegetation surveys, offering practical advantages in terms of mobility and ease of use in field conditions.

title: Design and Application of a Vision-Guided Pick-and-Place Robot System
authors: Shengkai Sun, Zhenli Xu, Dong Xin, Ziteng Ma, Hangrong Dong
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: This paper presents a robotic picking system integrating machine vision with adaptive control. Utilizing a dual-camera high-precision positioning algorithm, it enables defect detection, precise sorting, and packaging of thin-film circuits (on hard substrates such as quartz, Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> ceramics, and sapphire). Equipped with a dual-vision system, it achieves a detection accuracy of ±0.02mm. The system employs a modular design, enabling rapid production changeovers in under 30 minutes. Field testing demonstrates a defect rate of less than 0.8% and equipment availability of 96%, fully meeting the automated inspection and packaging requirements for thin-film circuits.

title: Optimizing Cost Management in Business with Digital Solutions: *Note: Sub-titles are not captured in Xplore and should not be used
authors: Hassan Ali Al-Ababneh, Al Refai Mohammed N, Fawzieh Masad, Safwan Maghaydah, Ahmed Ali Otoom, Zeyad M. Jamhawi, Jafar Ababneh, Farah Hanna Zawaideh
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: In the context of the digital transformation of the economy, cost management is acquiring a new dimension, emphasizing the integration of automat-ed and analytical solutions into corporate decision-making. The purpose of this study is to develop a structured approach to evaluating the effectiveness of cost optimization through the application of digital technologies within business environments. The relevance of the topic stems from the growing need to enhance transparency, speed, and accuracy in managerial processes amid volatile markets and increasingly complex organizational structures. The need for the research arises from the lack of comprehensive and adaptable models for assessing the actual impact of digital tools on cost structures across various industries. The methodology involves the development of a system of key performance indicators (KPIs) that reflect the degree of reduction in direct and indirect expenses following the implementation of digital solutions. The study employs methods such as factor analysis, calculation of relative deviations, and comparative evaluation using “before-and-after implementation” models across different cost categories. The research perspectives include further adaptation of assessment models to specific industry contexts and the refinement of analytical indicators to support integrated digital cost transformation.

title: SCS-AC: Enhancing LLaVA with Salience Comprehension Scorer and Aspect-Aware Classifier for Multimodal aspect-based Sentiment Classification
authors: Zhihao Wang, Peng Wang, Yang Xiang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Multimodal Aspect-based Sentiment Classification (MASC) has broad applications in scenarios such as social media analysis and recommendation systems. However, existing methods cannot effectively deal with image-text relevance, and most methods rely on relatively small pretrained encoders for both image and text. The limited quality of multimodal alignment data further constrains their alignment capability. In this paper, we propose a new architecture SCS-AC for MASC, based on LLaVA's powerful modality alignment. Firstly, a Salience Comprehension Scorer (SCS) module is designed to suppress irrelevant image regions and highlight informative visual content. Moreover, an Aspect-aware Classifier (AC) module is proposed to enhance fine-grained semantic understanding and shift LLaVA's training objective to classification, thereby better adapting SCSAC to MASC. Extensive experiments on two public datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance and confirm the effectiveness of the proposed architecture.

title: Fiber Bragg Grating Temperature Sensor Package Design for Transformer Calibration Contacts
authors: Hang Xia, Han Song, Xiangzuo Chen, Naiqi Cheng
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: To address movable contact temperature detection in current transformer verification devices, this study proposes an fiber Bragg grating (FBG) temperature sensor and fiber lead packaging design. Encapsulating a pair of FBGs in capillary copper tubes, the sensor measures two contacts’ temperatures; the fiber lead uses a helix-straight-helix structure to reduce bending loss during contact displacement. ANSYS simulated packaged fiber lead deformation, with a spiral fiber bending loss model optimizing the helix structure for different heights. Theoretical fiber loss is 0.066 dB at 10 mm contact displacement (3.1% deviation from experiments). A bending loss platform calibrated refractive-index-step single-mode fiber intensity loss under varying bending radii; a temperature platform verified the sensor’s thermal performance. Within 0–100°C, the two encapsulated FBGs have temperature sensitivities of 12.5 pm/°C (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=99.97%) and 12.26 pm/°C (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=99.96%) (excellent linearity), with response times of 0.84 s and 0.82 s (rapid responsiveness). This packaging provides a novel solution for movable contact temperature measurement in complex electromagnetic environments.

title: From Files to URLs: A Unified ML-Based Vulnerability Scanner
authors: Bayan Abu Shawar, Muath Alshaikh, Sajnin Sultana, Jasim Majid, Suhib Melhem, Majdi Sawalha
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: This paper introduces a machine learning-based vulnerability scanner that identifies malware and malicious URLs through on-demand scanning, utilizing trained models instead of static signatures. This system is also based on two significant modules: analysis of Portable Executable (PE) files based on the properties of file headers and analysis of URLs based on structural analysis. The scanner is scaled and can be extended in the future, e.g., with real-time monitoring, because it is integrated through a web interface with user login, scan history, and visualization of the result. Evaluation with publicly available data shows reasonable quality detection rates compared with traditional methods, and offers a high-quality and scalable solution to high-stakes cybersecurity challenges.

title: AICPS 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Cyber Physical Systems (AICPS)

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)

title: Robust Traffic Sign Recognition in Complex Environments Based on Transfer Learning and RESNET-18
authors: Gaosheng Liu, Yongliang Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: As intelligent transportation systems and autonomous driving technologies advance, accurate traffic sign recognition is becoming increasingly crucial, directly impacting road safety. Traditional methods of traffic sign detection often rely on handcrafted features, which are sensitive to scale variations, motion blur, and complex backgrounds. This paper proposes a robust traffic sign recognition system based on the ResNet-18 deep learning framework. A target classification model has been developed through the training of 52,126 images from the GTSRB dataset, capable of efficiently and accurately recognizing 43 categories of traffic signs. To address the issue of overfitting and complex environmental noise, an end-to-end data preprocessing and augmentation pipeline is introduced. Experimental results indicate that the model achieves outstanding performance with a test accuracy of 99.37 %, offering a highly reliable and lightweight solution for intelligent vehicle perception systems.

title: Capacity Allocation Strategy for Independent Energy Storage in Medium- and Long-Term and Spot Electricity Markets
authors: Kaixuan Wang, Dongsheng Niu, Wei Liu, Kenan Cheng, Ziliang Gao, Kailai Qi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: With the advancement of electricity market reform in China, independent energy storage power stations have been allowed to participate simultaneously in medium- and long-term electricity markets and the spot market. Under limited storage capacity and increasing market uncertainty, how to rationally allocate capacity between the two markets has become a critical issue. This paper proposes a capacity allocation strategy for independent energy storage based on a two-stage stochastic programming framework, aiming to coordinate participation in medium- and long-term and spot markets, maximize expected profit, and effectively control downside risk. In the proposed model, medium- and long-term transactions are determined in the first stage, while spot market decisions are optimized in the second stage under multiple price scenarios. Simulation results indicate that the two-way trading mechanism enables energy storage to hedge risk through electricity purchasing under low-risk conditions and to expand deterministic profit through electricity selling under higher risk tolerance. As a result, a smoother and more balanced risk-return trade-off is achieved, demonstrating the effectiveness of two-way trading in optimizing the market participation strategy of independent energy storage power stations.

title: Cross-Company Evaluation of ML Algorithms for Employee Performance Forecasting
authors: Shadi Nashwan, Issam Jebreen, Ahmad Al-Qerem, M. Alauthman
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Employee performance is a multidimensional construct that affects organisational productivity, competitive advantage and employee development. In recent years, humanresource (HR) analytics has shifted from descriptive dashboards toward predictive and prescriptive systems that leverage machine-learning (ML) techniques. Despite this transformation, many organisations in the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), and particularly in Saudi Arabia, continue to rely on manual, subjective appraisal methods that are time consuming and prone to bias. This study systematically compares four ML algorithms-decision tree (DT), random forest (RF), support vector machine (SVM) and artificial neural network (ANN)-for forecasting employee performance using a multi-company dataset comprising demographic features, job-history variables, training and attendance records and previous appraisal scores. The research adopts a quantitative, comparative and applied paradigm and emphasises ethical data governance and interpretability throughout the modelling pipeline.
