title: Comparative Performance Analysis of PID and FL Control for a Nonlinear Magnetic Levitation System
authors: Nagmedeen Eltorky, Ahmed F. Zrigan, Ahmed J. Abougarair, Khaled S. Elsouri
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: MLS represent a significant advancement in electromechanical engineering enabling contactless motion with high precision. However, their inherent nonlinearity and open loop instability pose substantial control challenges. This paper presents a comparative study between classical PID control and a nonlinear Feedback Linearization (FL) approach for a single degree of freedom electromagnetic levitation system. A detailed nonlinear dynamic model is derived and linearized for PID design, while FL analytically cancels system nonlinearities. High-fidelity MATLAB/Simulink simulations incorporating actuator saturation and disturbances demonstrate that FL outperforms both manual and auto-tuned PID controllers in stability and energy efficiency and transient performance. Specifically, FL achieves near-zero overshoot across a wide operating range $(6-20 \mathrm{~mm})$ and reduces peak power consumption by up to 80%, and eliminates actuator saturation during aggressive maneuvers. The results validate FL as a robust and efficient control strategy for high performance MLS applications in industrial and transportation and biomedical fields.

title: Survey of Control, Learning and Robotics in Aerospace Applications
authors: Qi Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The integration of advanced control theory, machine learning, and robotic technologies has emerged as a fundamental catalyst for the development of next-generation aerospace systems, enabling unprecedented levels of autonomy, adaptability, and operational efficiency. While substantial progress has been made across individual domains, a unified perspective that synthesizes these interdisciplinary advancements is essential to address the growing complexity of modern flight and space missions. This paper presents a comprehensive survey of the state of the art at the intersection of control, learning, and robotics across aeronautical and astronautical applications. We systematically review the application of data-driven paradigms—including deep neural networks, reinforcement learning, and stochastic supervised learning—to critical aerospace challenges such as aircraft design optimization, intelligent flight control, and autonomous spacecraft trajectory planning. Furthermore, we extensively examine the convergence of trajectory-based optimal control and advanced robotics in the context of space operations, highlighting breakthroughs in autonomous on-orbit servicing (OOS), active debris removal, and the manipulation of non-cooperative targets. By critically evaluating current methodological limitations—such as formal safety verification, hardware-in-the-loop simulation fidelity, and real-time computational constraints in dynamic environments—this survey identifies key open problems and outlines future research trajectories. Ultimately, this work provides a foundational roadmap for researchers and practitioners aiming to realize fully autonomous, resilient, and intelligent aerospace platforms.

title: Intelligent Demand Aware Smart Charging Scheduling Framework for Large Scale Electric Vehicle Integration
authors: S. Vidhya, Mohammad Kanan, N. Srihitha Gunapriya, Roopa M., S.Agnes Shifani, Lavish Kansal
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The rapid adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) is placing unprecedented demands on power grids, necessitating intelligent and scalable charging management systems. This paper presents IDASC-Net, an Intelligent Demand-Aware Smart Charging Scheduling Framework designed to optimize EV charging in large-scale environments. The paradigm incorporates unsupervised data preprocessing, time behavior modelling, graph-enhanced spatio-temporal forecasting, federated learning, reinforcement-based time scheduling, and quantum-enhanced optimization. Large-scale simulations with grid and EV real data show that IDASC-Net can substantially enhance the key performance parameters. It realizes a 53.2 % decrease in the average wait time, as much as 49.7 % of the decrease in grid load deviation and 28.2 % growth in the use of renewable energy. The framework can be said to have the strength of aligning technical performance with user experience as it results in an improvement of user satisfaction scores by 24 27 points. Furthermore, edge-deployed version is real-time responsive and its scheduling runtime is approximately 2.5 seconds, which is a practical feasibility. The findings affirm that IDASC-Net can be used to integrate EV in a reliable, energy saving, and user-friendly mode. The area of future work will be the introduction of integration with vehicle-to-grid (V2G) models and city-scale mobility prediction to develop greater adaptability and resilience. This paper defines a new and successful framework of next-generation energy orchestration of EVs on the smart grids and urban infrastructures.

title: MANO-UniDerm: A Lightweight Multimodal Skin Lesion Classification Framework Based on Multi-Scale Attention and Knowledge Distillation
authors: Ying Ma, Xinhua Liu, Xiaolin Ma, Hailan Kuang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: In recent years, deep learning has achieved remarkable progress in skin image classification, especially multimodal methods that combine skin images with patient clinical metadata have demonstrated great potential. However, existing highperformance models generally suffer from large parameter sizes, high computational overhead, and excessive memory utilization. Furthermore, many models only focus on either local details of skin lesions or global contextual information, making it difficult to balance both. Meanwhile, redundant information is easily introduced during cross-modal feature extraction, increasing the inference burden of the model. To address these challenges, we propose a lightweight multimodal classification network named MANO-UniDerm. By introducing a multi-scale attention mechanism and constructing a teacher-student learning framework based on knowledge distillation, our method significantly reduces inference time and memory consumption while improving the classification accuracy of the model. Experimental results show that our proposed method achieves higher classification accuracy with reduced computational overhead in both the ISIC-2019 and PAD-UFES-20 datasets.

title: Hybrid Fuzzy AHP-TOPSIS Model for Supplier Selection in Cleanroom Construction Projects under Uncertainty
authors: Dinh-Tien Luong, Chia-Nan Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)
abstract: Supplier selection for complex cleanroom construction projects is a critical strategic decision, yet traditional methods often fail to manage the inherent uncertainty and conflicting criteria involved. This study addresses this gap by developing a hybrid MCDM framework that integrates the FAHP to weight criteria and the FTOPSIS to rank potential suppliers. The model is validated through a semiconductor fabrication plant case study, where findings reveal that economic and risk-related criteria are the most influential factors. The framework successfully identifies the optimal supplier S1 with a final Closeness Coefficient of 0.7880. While the hybrid FAHP-FTOPSIS framework is a well-established MCDM technique, this research contributes to the academic literature by customizing and validating its application within the novel context of cleanroom construction. It provides a robust, practical decision-support tool for industry practitioners facing this specialized sourcing challenge.

title: Enhanced Regenerative Braking Control for Dualmotor Electric Vehicles with Supercapacitor
authors: Santha kumar C, Kiruthigadevi V, Stephenraj M A, Gajalakshmi K, Sabari K, Sevvel L
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: This research will enhance the regenerative breaking energy recovery of electric vehicles through a comparison of the control strategies implemented by deep reinforcement learning and the optimization of energy storage through a supercapacitorbased battery hybrid system. Group 1 uses the Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) algorithm to make use of regenerative braking control in a traditional battery-based energy storage system. Group 2 relies on the Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) algorithm; it has a supercapacitor-lithiumion battery hybrid energy storage system. The findings reveal that in the case of the TD3-based regenerative braking system using a hybrid energy storage system consisting of a supercapacitor and battery the regenerative energy capture is better, the system is more efficient and energy loss during braking is lower. The positioning control strategy is more optimized, which offers better braking reaction and the use of recovered energy. The experiment finds that the TD3-based regenerative braking system of supercapacitors and battery hybrid storage provides superior energy management and braking efficiency as compared to the DDPG-based one. The approach enhances the general performance of the systems and enables a more efficient recovery of energy in electric vehicles.

title: Distributed Model-Free Adaptive Control for Leader-Follower Consensus in Disturbed UAV Swarms
authors: Yuxin Cao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This paper examines the leader-follower consensus control issue in a UAV swarm, proposing a distributed model-free adaptive control method based on dynamic linearization. An end-to-end data-driven mapping architecture is developed. Pseudo partial derivatives are then used to estimate the system dynamics online and correct feedback in real time. This avoids complex modelling work. It also improves robustness under abnormal operating conditions. Theoretical analysis shows that this method guarantees closed-loop system stability under bounded input and output conditions while ensuring bounded tracking error. These results apply to tracking both constant and bounded time-varying trajectories. Effectiveness and superiority of the method under abnormal conditions is verified by simulation results.

title:
A 108.9dB-DR, 2-2 MASH ADC Featuring a Nested Gainboosting FIA with G
                    <sub>m</sub>
                    Enhancement in Settling Phase Through Stepwise Charge Sharing
authors: Yuming Li, Lichenxi Zhang, Youxiang Chen, Kelei Zhang, Rui Pan, Junyao Ji, Jie Zhang, Xiaofei Wang, Hong Zhang
abstract: This paper presents a 2-2 multistage noise-shaping (MASH) ADC featuring a nested gain-boosting floating inverter amplifier (NGB-FIA) to fulfill the high-gain (>100dB) and low-power requirement. To achieve better utilization of the energy stored on the reservoir capacitor, a stepwise charge sharing scheme is further proposed in the NGB-FIA, which enhances the transconductance (G<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf>) by 37% during the settling phase of the integrator. Fabricated in a 180-nm CMOS process, the ADC achieves 107.4-dB SNDR and 108.9-dB dynamic range (DR) in a 16-kHz bandwidth while consuming 325.1 μW. This translates into a Schreier figure of merit (FoM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</inf>) of 185.8 dB and a FoM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">SNDR</inf> of 184.3 dB.

title: An AI Framework for EEG Signal Denoising Using Adaptive Hybrid Wavelet and Autoencoder.
authors: C R Mohan Kumar, Guruprasad S, Simha D K L N, Megha N, Madhu R, Jayapadmini Kanchan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Electroencephalogram (EEG) signals are often corrupted by physiological and environmental noise, reducing the accuracy of automated neurological analysis. Traditional denoising methods such as fixed wavelet thresholding, adaptive filtering, and standalone deep learning models either lack adaptability, interpretability, or computational efficiency when handling non-stationary EEG artifacts. This paper proposes an adaptive hybrid wavelet autoencoder framework for effective EEG denoising. Wavelet decomposition with particle swarm optimization (PSO) is used to determine optimal threshold values, followed by a deep autoencoder to remove residual nonlinear artifacts. An adaptive decision mechanism selects the best output for each EEG segment to ensure robust performance. Experimental evaluations on standard EEG datasets indicate that the proposed method performs better than both traditional techniques and recent deep learning models. It achieves lower MSE and RMSE values, along with higher SNR, highlighting its ability to reconstruct signals more accurately while effectively reducing noise.

title: Optimizing Shallow Solar Pond Performance with Nanofluids: A Comparative Analysis of Thermal and Exergy Efficiency
authors: Terfai Abdelkrim, Chiba Younes, Zirari Mounir
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: This study investigates the thermal and exergy performance enhancement of a shallow solar pond (SSP) by incorporating nanofluids under continuous heat extraction mode. A detailed numerical model was developed using MATLAB to simulate heat transfer and energy conversion processes based on coupled energy balance equations. Various nanofluids—Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>, CuO, TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, and Cu dispersed in water—were compared in terms of outlet temperature, useful energy gain, and exergetic efficiency at different nanoparticle concentrations and mass flow rates. The results indicate that the inclusion of nanoparticles significantly improves heat transfer due to enhanced thermal conductivity and convective effects. For Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>+H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O, the outlet temperature increased from 40.98 °C to 42.42 °C with 5% concentration, while the optimal flow rate of 0.018 kg/s yielded the maximum useful energy of 463.66 W. Among the tested nanofluids, Cu+H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O exhibited the highest thermal performance, whereas SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>+H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O achieved superior exergy efficiency owing to reduced entropy generation. Seasonal simulations under Medea (Algeria) climatic conditions demonstrated stable year-round performance, with peak efficiency in summer. The findings confirm that metallic and oxide nanofluids can substantially enhance both energy quantity and quality, offering practical insights for the design and optimization of high-efficiency solar thermal systems.

title: Performance Evaluation of Solar Thermal and PV Systems for Residential Buildings in Mediterranean and Semi-Arid Climates
authors: Ouiza Ait Abderrahim, Albdelkader Aris, Touhami Baki
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: Algeria faces growing energy challenges due to rising demand, fossil fuel dependency, and limited renewable integration. Despite abundant solar potential, domestic solar use remains marginal. This study evaluates the performance of integrated solar thermal and photovoltaic (PV) systems with Trnsys simulation for domestic use in two distinct Algerian climates: Mediterranean (Oran) and semi-arid (Tiaret). The purpose is to promote the integration of renewable energies in residential sector to reduce the dependency on the fossil energies.A 4 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> thermal collector and a photovoltaic system were analyzed to meet the energy demands by a typical four-person household. Results show that the thermal system effectively covers domestic hot water needs, with a maximum useful gain of 460.92 kWh in Oran and 441.29 kWh in Tiaret, achieving near-total solar coverage in summer. Annual auxiliary energy requirements were 4.3 MWh in Oran and 4.6 MWh in Tiaret. On the electrical side, the PV system covered up to 44% of January demand in Oran and 36% in Tiaret, with maximum monthly production reaching 2 MWh and full coverage observed from June to August in both regions. The findings confirm that integrating both systems into residential buildings significantly reduce reliance on conventional energy sources, with higher efficiency in regions with greater solar potential.

title: Design of Fractal-Inspired Circularly Polarized Antenna for RFID Reader Applications
authors: Sohom Dasgupta, Tanmay Mishra, Aniket Kumar Singh, Tanmaya Kumar Das
abstract: This paper presents the design and analysis of a compact fractal circularly polarized Antenna for RFID reader application operating at 5.8 GHz ISM band. The proposed antenna integrates a fractal-shaped radiating patch on a dielectric substrate, optimized for a high gain and stable operation. The design consists of probe-fed slotted square-shaped patch with fractal edge and two pairs of capacitively coupled grounded metal strips. Simulation results demonstrate a resonant frequency at 5.76 GHz with −10 dB bandwidth from 5.71 GHz to 5.87 GHz, and a peak realized gain of 6.2 dBic. The antenna achieves an excellent circular polarization, with a minimum axial ratio of 0.89 dB, ensuring consistent performance under varying orientations making it suitable for smart wearables and biomedical sensors.

title: A Holistic Perspective on UICs: Internal R&D, Big Data, and Absorptive Capacity in Enhancing Innovation Performance in Thai Food Firms
authors: Wisuwat Wannamakok, Takron Opassuwan
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: This study proposes a comprehensive analytical framework to investigate the influence of university–industry collaborations (UICs) on innovation performance within Thailand’s food industry. Grounded in the resource-based view (RBV) and knowledge-based view (KBV) of the firm, the research examines the interactive effects of internal R&D intensity, absorptive capacity, and big data analytics capabilities on firm-level innovation outcomes. Emphasizing the synergistic integration of these internal capabilities, the study further explores the moderating role of UICs in enhancing innovation performance. Drawing on data from 857 food firms, a three-stage logistic regression analysis was employed to test the proposed hypotheses through the inclusion of control variables, direct effects, and interaction terms. The empirical results reveal that R&D intensity, absorptive capacity, and the utilization of big data analytics each exert a statistically significant and positive influence on innovative performance. Importantly, UICs are found to significantly moderate the relationship between absorptive capacity and innovation outcomes, underscoring the value of external knowledge networks in strengthening firms’ capabilities to acquire, assimilate, and exploit external knowledge for innovation. These findings contribute to the broader discourse on innovation in emerging economies and offer actionable implications for both practitioners and policymakers aiming to cultivate dynamic, knowledge-intensive, and collaborative innovation ecosystems within the agro-food sector.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)

title: Computing Pure Consecutive Maximal Periodic Patterns with $k \Delta$-Errors in Raw and Compressed Data
authors: Samuel Bismuth, Avivit Levy, Dana Shapira
abstract: Identifying periodic patterns in time series data is crucial for uncovering hidden structures and predicting future events. Recognizing meaningful periodic patterns in real data requires handling approximation criteria since periodic phenomena are usually inexact. This paper introduces a suitable criterion and focuses on detecting Consecutive Periodic Patterns (CPPs) with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k \Delta$</tex>-errors, where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex> bounds the number of errors and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta$</tex> limits the size of the error. We develop efficient algorithms to detect the Longest Pure Consecutive Maximal Periodic Pattern with bounded errors in both raw and compressed data, the latter by means of the Arithmetic Progressions Tree (APT) data structure.

title: MiFuse: Continual Fusion of Multiple Industrial Networks under Domain Shift
authors: Tian-Yu Zuo, Pan Li, Hao Sun, Kai Di
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Progressive integration of industrial graphs creates a fusion-first continual-learning problem that is not well captured by standard task streams. A learner must repeatedly absorb new graph domains while retaining certified behavior from previously integrated systems. This paper presents a 4-page conference condensation of MiFuse, a fusion-aware continual graph-learning framework for this setting. MiFuse constructs a temporary graph that reconnects current-domain nodes with bounded historical memory, applies reliability-aware weighting to cross-domain edges, and optimizes a multi-term objective including replay, distillation, prototype alignment, and relational preservation. Compared with replay and regularization baselines, MiFuse delivers a stronger stability-plasticity tradeoff on controlled industrial graph streams. The largest benefits appear under topology and compound shift, where preserving structural context is as important as preserving labels. The results highlight fused replay as a practical approximation of ideal joint training under bounded-memory constraints.

title: AI Applications for Power Quality Challenges in Distribution Systems: Technical Barriers and Emerging Trends
authors: Mohamed Khaleel, Ziyodulla Yusupov, Ali Hesri, Pirimov Odil, Rashidov Jahongir, Ibrahim Imbayah
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: The integration of renewable energy sources and power electronic devices into modern distribution systems has intensified power quality (PQ) challenges, including voltage sags, harmonic distortions, and transient disturbances. This review presents a comprehensive analysis of artificial intelligence (AI) applications for PQ monitoring and control, addressing both technical limitations and emerging trends in distribution systems (DSS). Unlike previous studies that focus on isolated AI methods or specific PQ issues, this work provides a comprehensive synthesis encompassing detection, classification, and mitigation across multiple disturbance types. Using over 70 recent publications, the study classifies key PQ disturbances, evaluates AI techniques-including machine learning, deep learning, and hybrid models-and highlights their performance in terms of accuracy, real-time processing, and adaptability. The article illustrate the advantages of AI in diagnosing PQ issues more quickly and accurately than traditional methods. One significant finding is the limited application of AI-driven control in power converters (e.g., voltage source inverters, multilevel inverters) despite their central role in grid interfacing and stability. The findings emphasize that AI enhances PQ management by enabling predictive diagnostics and adaptive control in renewable-rich, bidirectional grids. However, real-time deployment is constrained by challenges such as data scarcity, model interpretability, and integration with existing SCADA systems. The review recommends advancing hybrid and federated learning models, as well as edge AI deployment and explainable AI frameworks, to overcome these barriers. Furthermore, this work provides practical insights for researchers and engineers seeking intelligent, scalable, and future-ready PQ solutions in decentralized power systems.

title: Data Mining-Based Intrusion Detection for IoT Environments
authors: Belkacem Athamena, Mohamed Raid Athamena, Zina Houhamdi
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) integrates vast numbers of resource-constrained devices, creating new security challenges. Traditional Intrusion Detection System (IDS) methods often fail under IoT constraints (limited memory, heterogeneous protocols, and massive data volume). This paper presents a data mining–based IDS framework tailored for IoT. The framework employs supervised classifiers (Decision Tree and SVM) and an unsupervised K-means clustering approach to detect intrusions. We evaluate the framework on two public IoT datasets, UNSWNB15 and Bot-IoT, using standard metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC). Results indicate that decision trees and SVM achieve high accuracy (¿ 95%) on labeled IoT traffic, while K-means can identify anomalous clusters. We analyze detection rates, compare algorithms, and discuss implications for IoT security.

title: Research on the Construction and Application of a Digital Twin System for an Automotive Lighting Plant
authors: Tao Zhu, Huafang Wang, Jueyuan Duan, Jin Cao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: This paper takes the digital twin system project for the automotive lamp injection molding and assembly workshop as the research object. It expounds the overall architecture, construction scheme, key technologies, functions, and application practices of the system. By building a digital twin platform, the system realizes visual management of workshop production, cross-system data interaction, and virtual-real synchronization, meets the requirements of Intelligent Manufacturing Capability Maturity Level 4, provides strong support for enterprises to improve production efficiency and reduce costs, and has reference value for the intelligent transformation of the auto parts industry.

title: Community Energy Management Based on Improved Multi-Objective Snowmelt Algorithm
authors: Yulong Zhang, Yuhong Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: With the widespread application of user-side energy storage devices in real life, it has become urgent to reasonably schedule localized energy storage devices to address microgrid voltage fluctuations and cost issues. This paper proposes a community energy management optimization scheme based on an improved multi-objective snow melt algorithm. First, the initialization is optimized using a chaotic mapping strategy, and a periodic oscillation mutation strategy significantly improves the model's convergence speed without increasing computational burden. Second, the non-dominated sorting strategy is introduced into the algorithm to improve its accuracy and convergence speed. At the same time, to maintain the diversity and uniform distribution of population individuals, a crowding distance calculation is introduced to measure the density of each layer of solutions after fast non-dominated sorting and to reorder the population, obtaining well-distributed Pareto solutions. This addresses the problems of the snow melt optimization algorithm being prone to local optima, lacking global search capability, and having low convergence accuracy. It reduces computational complexity while ensuring population uniformity and diversity. Finally, an optimization scheduling model is established with voltage fluctuation and operational cost as objectives, which is solved in the hourly stage using the multi-objective snow melt algorithm. Based on actual community data, simulations were conducted in MATLAB, and the results show that the proposed method can achieve good convergence and real-time performance while considering various optimization objectives.

title: Deep Learning Approach for Pneumonia Detection in Chest X-Rays with Visual Explanation Technique
authors: Abdelkader Alrabai
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Pneumonia remained a major public health challenge worldwide, particularly in low resource regions where access to timely and accurate diagnostic services was limited. Chest X-Ray (CXR) imaging has long served as a primary tool for pneumonia assessment; however, reliable interpretation often depended on experienced radiologists, whose availability was not guaranteed in many clinical settings. This study examined the application of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for automated pneumonia detection using CXR images, with emphasis on both predictive performance and interpretability of model decisions. Two CNN architectures, ResNet50 and ShuffleNet_V2, were evaluated using a publicly available dataset containing normal and pneumonia cases. The experimental results indicated that ResNet50 achieved superior performance, with an accuracy of 91.35%, precision of 91.72%, recall of 89.74%, and an $\mathbf{F 1}$-score of 90.56%. ShuffleNet_V2 also demonstrated competitive outcomes, recording an accuracy of 85.10%, precision of 84.68%, recall of 83.12%, and an F 1 -score of 83.76%. Beyond classification metrics, explainable AI techniques were integrated to enhance model transparency. In particular, integrated gradients were employed to highlight the image regions that contributed most significantly to each prediction, allowing visual assessment of clinically relevant lung features. The findings confirmed the effectiveness of deep learning approaches for pneumonia identification while underscoring the importance of explainability in medical imaging applications. The combination of reliable diagnostic performance and interpretable visual outputs supported the potential integration of such systems into clinical workflows, especially in healthcare environments constrained by limited expertise and resources.

title: Dynamics of Artificial Intelligence in Teaching and Learning
authors: Khawlah M. AL-Tkhayneh, Abdulrasheed Olowoselu, Hadil Hussein Farag
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) in education continues to evolve and offers many exciting possibilities for improving teaching and learning outcomes in schools. This study provides rational discussions on the concepts of AI, teaching, and learning. The chapter discusses processes and usage of artificial intelligence in education in the realm of language learning, assistive learning, early childhood learning, adaptive learning, lesson planning, text preparation, gamification, and learning management systems. This study further provides an overview of innovative roles of artificial intelligence in educational institutions, identified as professional development, cybersecurity, school security, students' safety, academic research, connected campuses, and universal access to learning resources. This study also explored the potential of artificial intelligence in educational institutions, such as collaborations, automation of information, and personalization of learning. This study further outlined challenges of artificial intelligence in education, such as limited ease of accessibility, ethical issues, difficulty integrating AI tools and high-cost resources, and implementation constraints. Fundamentally, the study concludes that artificial intelligence is an essential path for educational excellence.

title: Microwave-based Quality Control for Bread Production
authors: Marco Murgia, Luigi Di Michele, Matteo Bruno Lodi, Giacomo Muntoni, Andrea Melis, Giuseppe Mazzarella, Alessandro Fanti
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: This paper explores how microwave techniques can be leveraged to develop fast, non-destructive monitoring to the bread industry. We discuss a set of approaches that combine dielectric spectroscopy and microwave sensor design, and examine their relevance to modern bakery industry, reporting case studies related to traditional and gluten-free food products. Broadband spectroscopy from 0.2 to 20GHz has been used to characterize both Carasau and gluten-free doughs, with permittivity variations linked to water, salt and yeast content through three-pole Cole-Cole models showing errors below one percent. On the sensor side, we review solutions such as a hybrid Particle Swarm Optimization–Nonlinear Regression corrected coaxial fixture for in situ permittivity measurements (0.5 to 3GHz), and a 5.8GHz wireless patch antenna for non contact moisture tracking, with sensitivity of 1.25dB per percent water. The paper illustrates how combining advanced dielectric analysis with suitable sensor design enables real-time quality control and supports Industry 4.0 transformation in the bread sector.

title: A Comprehensive Evaluation of Photovoltaic Grid Integration Schemes Based on Entropy Weight and TOPSIS Methods
authors: Bin Liu, Lina Wu, Bo Yang, Dalong Luo, Xiulong Chen
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: The integration of photovoltaic (PV) systems significantly impacts distribution network voltage, frequency, harmonics, and network losses. To comprehensively evaluate these interactions, this paper proposes specific evaluation indices and a unified assessment framework. By combining the entropy weight method to eliminate subjective weighting bias and the Technique for Order Preference by Similarity to Ideal Solution (TOPSIS) method to rank alternative schemes, the framework provides an objective evaluation. A PSCAD simulation of three candidate PV integration schemes demonstrates that connecting a single large-capacity unit to the substation's primary side yields the optimal comprehensive performance. This method offers a scientific basis for optimizing PV grid-connection modes.

title: Operational Performance Comparison of Signalized Intersection, Multi-lane Roundabout, and Turbo Roundabout Using Traffic Microsimulation
authors: Abubakr A. B. Mohammed, Aeshah Alzayani
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Traffic congestion at signalized intersections within urban environments is one of the major issues throughout many cities. Therefore, it is extremely important to select the appropriate intersection control method for improving mobility, minimizing traffic delays, and enhancing traffic flow operations. This study presents the use of simulation-based comparative analysis to conduct a performance evaluation of a congested signalized intersection versus two alternative roundabout types: a multi-lane roundabout and a turbo roundabout. The comparisons were conducted via microsimulation using PTV VISSIM 2024 software under peak-hour traffic demand conditions, which provided a consistent basis for comparison. The simulation model was calibrated and validated. The key performance indicators, including average vehicle delay, queue length, and level of service (LOS), were extracted from the simulation outputs and analyzed for alternative intersection control techniques. The results indicated that the turbo roundabout demonstrates superior operational performance with an average delay reduction of 63 to 73% and a queue length reduction of 40% compared to the existing signalized intersection, along with an overall improvement in level of service. The findings suggest that turbo roundabouts represent an effective alternative to conventional roundabouts and signalized intersections in urban areas experiencing high and unbalanced traffic demand.

title: A Data-Driven Framework for Wind Power Forecasting Based on Weather Information Sensing
authors: Xiangrui Meng, Haozhe Lu, Kaixiang Zhang, Junhua Zhao
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: Accurate wind power forecasting remains challenging under rapidly changing weather conditions, where local wind variations, spatially heterogeneous meteorological fields, and forecast uncertainty jointly degrade predictive performance. To address this issue, this paper proposes a data-driven framework for wind power forecasting based on weather information sensing. The proposed method integrates historical supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) sequences with numerical weather prediction (NWP) information from both the center grid and neighboring grids. A wind speed mutation sensitivity factor (WSMSF) is introduced to characterize risk levels by jointly modeling observed wind variation and NWP-related uncertainty. In addition, a multi-head SwiGLU routing module is developed to adaptively fuse neighboring-grid NWP information from multiple weather perspectives, instead of using simple averaging or fixed aggregation. Experiments are conducted on the Hill of Towie wind farm using two years of SCADA data and GFS forecasts. The proposed method achieves the best overall performance among all compared models, with an MAE of 0.0715 and an RMSE of 0.1263. Ablation studies further demonstrate that multi-head neighboring-grid routing is more effective than center-grid-only fusion, neighboring-grid averaging, and singlehead routing. Moreover, WSMSF-weighted learning yields consistent improvements across different risk levels. These results validate the effectiveness of the proposed weather-informationsensing framework for robust wind power forecasting.

title: An Anomaly Detection Algorithm Based on Multi-Scale Features and Positional Decay in Graph Neural Networks
authors: Ping Li, Zhanquan Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Anomaly detection on graphs plays a critical role in applications such as financial risk control, social network analysis, and cybersecurity. However, existing methods often struggle to capture multi-scale node semantics and suffer from over-smoothing when stacking multiple message-passing layers. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel Multi-Scale and Decay-Aware Graph Transformer Network (MSDAGTN). MSDA-GTN introduces a Progressive Scale Aggregation module to extract and fuse multi-scale node features, constructing hierarchical semantic representations. In addition, a Position-Aware Decay mechanism dynamically adjusts attention weights based on node-pair distances, enabling more effective long-range dependency modeling through multi-head attention. A gating-based feature fusion mechanism further improves robustness, and final anomaly prediction is conducted using a multi-layer perceptron. Experiments on the Yelp and Amazon datasets demonstrate that MSDA-GTN consistently outperforms baselines in terms of AUC, F1, and AP. Ablation studies also validate the effectiveness of each proposed component.

title: Design and Implementation of a Piezoelectric-Based Footstep Energy Harvesting System
authors: Harish S. S, S. Mohamed Riyaz, G. Jothi Krishna, S. Mouli, S. Naveen Chandar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Rapid urbanization and continuous expansion of smart infrastructure have significantly increased the demand for sustainable, decentralized, and location-independent energy solutions, particularly in high-footfall public spaces such as railway stations, airports, shopping complexes, and educational campuses. While large-scale renewable energy technologies such as solar photovoltaic and wind systems contribute substantially to clean energy production, their performance is strongly dependent on environmental conditions, installation feasibility, and available open space. These constraints limit their effectiveness in indoor environments and densely constructed urban areas. Consequently, there is a growing need for complementary micro-energy harvesting systems capable of utilizing locally available mechanical energy sources independent of climatic variability. Human locomotion represents a continuously available and largely untapped mechanical energy source in crowded urban environments. Several existing energy harvesting systems have explored the conversion of footstepinduced mechanical stress into electrical energy using piezoelectric, electromagnetic, and hybrid mechanisms. However, many of these approaches encounter limitations including low conversion efficiency, unstable and fluctuating output, complex mechanical amplification structures, high implementation cost, and significant power losses in rectification and conditioning stages. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an optimized footstep power generation system utilizing strategically embedded piezoelectric sensor arrays integrated within a mechanically reinforced load-distribution platform. The design ensures efficient stress transfer, structural durability, and pedestrian safety. The electrical interface incorporates low-loss full-wave rectification, filtering, and voltage regulation to stabilize the transient AC output. A dual-stage energy storage architecture comprising high-capacitance capacitors and rechargeable batteries enhances energy buffering and reliable power delivery. Experimental validation under repeated stepping conditions demonstrates stable voltage generation up to 4 V, conditioned current levels reaching 50 mA, and peak instantaneous power approaching 200 mW. The cumulative energy accumulation confirms efficient conversion and storage performance. The modular and scalable architecture enables practical deployment in high-footfall urban environments, making the proposed system a viable micro-power solution for distributed sustainable infrastructure applications.

title: Monitoring System of Crowds’ Movement Patterns, Augmented with Bloom Filters and Homomorphic Encryption to Preserve Data Privacy
authors: Adrian Roman, Valeriu-Daniel Stanciu, Radu-Ioan Ciobanu, Ciprian Dobre
publish: 2025 24th International Symposium on Parallel and Distributed Computing (ISPDC)
abstract: Monitoring crowds of people for the purpose of statistical counting has been of great interest for a long period of time. Observing movement patterns across groups of people has proven useful for organizing public spaces. For example, when planning events, it can help event organizers and business owners find optimal locations for shops, food stands, music stages, or other key areas. The challenge rises when having to deal with privacy-sensitive data of the detected people such as: their position at a certain timestamp, the MAC address of the device they are carrying or other personal data. Guided by the General Data Protection Regulation, we select a state-of-the-art privacy-preserving crowd-monitoring architecture that combines Bloom filters and homomorphic encryption to count people based on the Wi-Fi probe requests emitted by their devices. We upgrade it from partially homomorphic to fully homomorphic encryption and install it on the campus of University POLITEHNICA of Bucharest. We prove that the solution is fast and practically deployable, being able to estimate detections at a certain location in a time interval, as well as estimating detections from the intersection of two different time intervals, while still preserving data privacy.

title: Exploring Ethical and Legal Challenges of Artificial Intelligence in Research Practices
authors: Mohammad Abdallah, Qasem Abdallah, Laila Barqawi, Daniel Staegemann
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: This paper explores the ethical and legal dimensions associated with the application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in research practices. As AI tools become increasingly integrated across disciplines, concerns have risen regarding research integrity, accountability, bias, transparency, and legal frameworks. The paper discusses key ethical considerations such as autonomy, consent, fairness, and fabrication, alongside legal challenges involving intellectual property, data protection, and liability. Recommendations are offered for the responsible and ethical deployment of AI within research settings.

title: Adaptive System for Constructing a Mobile Robot Trajectory Using Neural Network and Stochastic Optimizer
authors: Amer Abu-Jassar, Hani Attar, Mohamed Hafez, Mohammad Hamdan, Vladyslav Yevsieiev, Vyacheslav Lyashenko
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: This work addresses the challenge of building and modeling an adaptive system for trajectory construction of a mobile robot inside a dynamic environment, considering impediments, fluctuating variables, and the necessity for certain goal attainment. A method is proposed that integrates a stochastic particle swarm optimizer (PSO) with a multilayer perceptron (MLP) artificial neural network, whereby the training facilitates the approximation of the trajectory evaluation function. Utilizing the Python environment with the NumPy, Matplotlib, and Scikit-learn libraries, motion trajectories under random obstacle settings and graphs depicting the neural network's learning dynamics were generated and visualized. The experimental findings illustrate the system's capacity to adjust ambient variables, circumvent obstacles, and approach the goal point efficiently while minimizing costs. The program effectively simulates the behavior of an autonomous agent exhibiting cognitive adaptation, hence justifying its further application in intelligent control systems for mobile robotics. The proposed methodology may be advantageous in autonomous logistics, search and rescue robotics, health system access, and adaptable manufacturing systems, considering the tenets of Industry 5.0.

title: Robust Multi-Modal Fault Diagnosis for Synchronous Motors Using DRSN and Attention Fusion
authors: Jiyu Wang, Linge Li, Pengfei He, Chao Mu, Xiaoqin Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: In this paper, a new electrical fault diagnosis framework of synchronous motor is proposed, which combines transient-aware data alignment, multi-modal anti-noise fusion and deep learning. The existing data-driven methods often lead to feature leakage due to improper preprocessing, and it is difficult to extract weak fault features under strong noise. In order to solve these problems, we first propose a transient-aware alignment technology based on gradient detection, which can accurately locate the fault start time and reconstruct samples to maximize the effective information density. Then, we developed a hybrid model combining CNN, BiLSTM and spatial-temporal attention to extract and weight the critical fault features from the spatial and temporal dimensions. In order to improve the robustness under strong noise, we add Deep Residual Shrinkage Network (DRSN) with soft-thresholding, which can adaptively eliminate noise components. In addition, we also design a multi-modal fusion strategy, which dynamically weights the electrical and mechanical signals through the confidence-gated network to suppress the lowreliability modes under noise conditions. Experiments on a public synchronous motor fault dataset containing six operating states demonstrate that the proposed method achieves over 99 % diagnostic accuracy, significantly outperforming baseline models, especially in high-noise environments. The framework effectively improves feature decoupling and noise immunity, offering a reliable solution for real-time motor health monitoring.

title: Research on Deep Learning-Based Temperature Control Methods for Heating Furnaces
authors: Jiaqi Shi, Pengfei Qu, Yangkexin An, Feng Pan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This study explores a temperature control method for industrial furnaces based on rolling optimization of temperature increment prediction and model predictive control. Industrial furnaces are widely used equipment in modern manufacturing, holding significant importance particularly in metallurgy, chemical processing, and glass manufacturing. As complex thermal systems characterized by large hysteresis, strong nonlinearity, and strong coupling, industrial furnaces exhibit temperature characteristics influenced by multiple factors, including nonlinearity, multiple time delays, and disturbance uncertainties. Traditional control methods often struggle to achieve high-precision temperature regulation in dynamic environments and lack broadly applicable temperature control solutions. This study proposes a temperature control method for industrial furnaces based on rolling optimization of temperature increment prediction and model predictive control. It employs a deep learning-based temperature prediction model for forecasting and model predictive control for rolling optimization. The temperature prediction model utilizes a two-layer neural network with residual connections. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method enables real-time temperature control for industrial furnaces. This approach significantly enhances both the precision and versatility of industrial furnace temperature regulation. This work contributes to advancements in the manufacturing sector and offers new insights into the application of deep learning for industrial furnace temperature control.

title: Software-Agnostic Thermo-Mechanical Model Exchange via FMUs for Microelectronics Reliability Simulation
authors: M. van Soestbergen
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: As microelectronic systems become increasingly complex, the need for accurate, efficient, and software-agnostic simulation models across the value chain has grown significantly. This work presents such a workflow by embedding thermo-mechanical Reduced Order Models (ROMs) of microelectronic packages in Functional Mock-up Units (FMUs) for efficient, IP-safe, model exchange. In this workflow, the full microelectronic system is partitioned into a microelectronic package (linear elastic, but temperature dependent), solder interconnects (highly nonlinear), and PCB (either reduced or full fidelity), allowing model complexity to be tailored to the application. The ROM of the package is obtained via static condensation (Guyan-Irons), retaining only interface degrees of freedom (DOFs). Rigid surfaces at the interfaces lower the number of retained DOFs even further while preserving dominant shear and rocking modes. To control the size and numerical cost of the fully populated condensed matrices, a nullspace preserving sparsification method is introduced. Here, singular value decomposition is used to extract the nullspace of the condensed stiffness matrix. Minimum norm correction removes negligible couplings from the matrix, while maintaining rigid body invariance using the nullspace, thus avoiding artificial internal forces. The FMU stores temperature indexed stiffness matrices and thermal force arrays in binary form and exposes them through a shared library. Compatibility is demonstrated in Marc/Mentat, confirming that the ROM FMU can be consumed without additional license dependencies and is portable across host platforms.

title: Non-Stationary Multivariate Time Series Prediction Model Based on MLP
authors: Luoqian Guo, Haifeng Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Non-stationary multivariate time series forecasting holds significant practical value for real-world applications. To construct a model with linear complexity and high prediction accuracy, the following challenges must be addressed: (1) Balancing non-stationarity handling to avoid over-normalization that eliminates sequence diversity and restricts the model's capacity to learn cross-temporal dependencies; (2) Optimizing long-term trend feature extraction to mitigate the over-simplification problem prevalent in MLP-based models while maintaining computational efficiency and feature representation capability; (3) Seeking breakthroughs in model design philosophy by appropriately increasing complexity upon simple architectures to resolve the contradiction between the low efficiency of complex deep models and the insufficient feature capture capability of minimalist models. Based on these considerations, we propose Sim-PolyNet. Experimental comparisons with both MLP-based and Transformer-based models demonstrate significantly improved accuracy.

title: Optimizing Kidney CT Image Classification: A Stacking Ensemble Approach with Preprocessing and Fine-Tuned Deep Models
authors: Fatima Ahmed ALjaafari, Amna Mohamed Elhawil
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Kidney diseases have seen a notable increase in recent years, with timely diagnosis being crucial for successful treatment. This study presents a comprehensive Deep Learning (DL) system for automatic classification of kidney diseases from Computed Tomography (CT) images. Four pre-trained models-EfficientNetV2B1, ResNet101, VGG16, and MobileNetV3 Large-were fine-tuned for multi-class classification of kidney CT images into normal, tumour, cyst, and stone categories. The predictions from these base models were combined using a stacking ensemble approach. The ensemble achieved a high accuracy of 99.80% with Gaussian filtering, outperforming individual models. Evaluation metrics were near perfect across all categories (AUC $=1.0$). These results demonstrate that proper preprocessing and ensemble learning significantly improve classification accuracy and reliability in diagnosing kidney diseases.

title: Robust Model Predictive Control for the Decoking Process of Ethylene Cracking Furnace Based on LSTM-ARX Model
authors: Baolin Chen, Zhong Zhao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To ensure the safety constraints of coke removal operations during ethylene cracking furnace decoking process and achieve optimal coil outlet temperature (COT) tracking performance, a robust model predictive control (RMPC) method is proposed based on a Long Short-term Memory-Auto Regressive with Exogenous Inputs (LSTM-ARX) model. With multi-stage historical data from the decoking process a time-varying parameter LSTM-ARX model is identified. Based on the identified model, a RMPC method is proposed by introducing a zeroing control barrier function (ZCBF) to a funnel model predictive control (FMPC) method. Industrial application results have verified the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: AI-Driven IoT-Blockchain Framework for Smart Construction and Safety Automation
authors: R. Ruthra, Aswathi K, Kaviyapriya A, Ahamed Sithik, Jeno C G
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: A paradigm changes in the construction industry, Smart Construction lays the groundwork for future building practices by incorporating technologies that make machines, gadgets, and materials more autonomous, aware of their surroundings, and able to interact with one another. Decisions may be made with more knowledge and efficiency thanks to this intelligence, which boosts productivity and safety in the construction industry. On the other hand, our knowledge of SC is still developing. Using ideas from smart items and ubiquitous computing, this study delves into what makes Smart Construction different from conventional methods and how it might be used to Safety Automation. The TF-IDF method was used for feature extraction after data preprocessing techniques were applied to transform, validate, and clean construction-related data. To improve the precision of analyses, a deep learning hybrid model was suggested, which would merge LSTM and GRU networks. Intelligent safety management and automated construction decisionmaking were both enhanced by the LSTM-GRU model's high accuracy rate of 95.29 percent. Adaptive, data-driven, and efficient construction systems that improve performance and safety in future infrastructure development are possible thanks to Smart Construction and Safety Automation, according to these studies.

title: A Gearbox Anomaly Detection Method for Rotating Pump Equipment Based on TadGAN Algorithm
authors: Zhibin Wei, Zelin Nie, Yuxin Guan, Kangning Zhou, Hao Liu, Zhaoguo Wang, Wei Cheng
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Rotating pump equipment may experience wear and corrosion of critical components during long-term operation, which can subsequently lead to various pump failures, resulting in significant economic losses and potential operational hazards. Traditional anomaly detection methods for pump equipment primarily rely on threshold judgments based on industry experience, which are often characterized by vagueness and latency. When confronted with massive, imbalanced data samples generated during actual operation, methods that rely solely on big data analysis without algorithmic support tend to yield suboptimal results. Therefore, this study adopts the TadGAN algorithm structure, based on generative adversarial networks (GAN), and designs an anomaly detection method grounded in reconstruction techniques. This approach provides an effective unsupervised learning framework for early fault detection in pump systems, enhancing operational reliability and reducing maintenance costs.

title: Visual Semantic-Enhanced Dense Reconstruction and Object Detection for Indoor Environments
authors: Shuo Kai, Wenli Li, Gang Bai, Yongfei Xiao, Yali Wang, Zhengguang Ma
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address challenges in indoor inventory scenarios-specifically the protocol conflicts between heterogeneous data streams (UVC for RGB and OpenNI for Depth) in low-cost cameras, spatial misalignment, and the lack of semantic information-this paper proposes a dense reconstruction scheme enhanced by visual semantics. First, a unified data acquisition interface is established, employing a hardware-software collaborative registration algorithm to achieve high-precision spatiotemporal synchronization of color and depth data, thereby resolving acquisition conflicts on Linux platforms. Second, the YOLOv8 object detection algorithm is utilized to identify inventory categories. Through a pixel-level semantic re-coloring strategy, semantic information is embedded into RGB textures. Combined with the GraphSLAM algorithm, this approach generates dense point clouds that integrate both geometric structures and inventory attributes, enabling a semantically enriched representation of the environment.

title: Sex-Specific Speech Features and their Association with Major Depressive Disorder
authors: Omar Zahy
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) is a mental health condition characterized by persistent feelings of distress. Women are more likely to develop MDD than men. Speech-based biomarkers offer a promising method for objective assessment. However, sex differences in speech features can lead to skewed performance and cause misdiagnosis. Our objective is to explore sex-specific speech biomarkers of MDD individuals and relate gender-specific speech features with MDD cases. We analysed 212 participants from the Extended Distress Analysis Interview Corpus - Wizard of Oz (E-DAIC-WOZ) dataset, containing 67 MDD participants (30 male, 37 female) and 145 health controls. Depression severity was assessed using PHQ-8 selfreport (cutoff <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 10$</tex> for MDD), which was pre-filled before the session by participants. We extracted 91 speech features across seven domains: energy, frequency, temporal, lexical richness, syntactic complexity, word types, and sentiment analysis. Statistical analysis employed Mann-Whitney U tests for gender comparisons, gender-stratified Spearman rank correlations with False Discovery Rate (FDR) correction for assessing the relations between speech features and MDD, and a linear regression model for examining gender and feature interaction effect on MDD status. After FDR correction, 25 out of 91 speech features exhibited significant differences between males and females of MDD individuals, primarily in the frequency domain. Male MDD patients had seven significant correlations with PHQ-8 scores (mainly in word types and energy domains), whereas female MDD patients showed 13 significant correlations (mainly in the frequency domain). This research suggests that gender-specific modelling may reduce diagnostic bias in speech-based MDD detection.

title: Speech Emotion Recognition Using Deep Learning Models
authors: Rania Agoune, Douaa Hanane Kadi, Nada Eryhane Heltali, Said Gadri
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: This paper concerns the SER system using deep learning to classify emotions from vocalizations. We designed and implemented a CNN trained by a hybrid dataset combining RAVDESS, SAVEE, IEMOCAP and CASIA. In essence, MFCCs were extracted to capture salient audio features, and the data were further augmented with noise injections, pitch shifts, and time-stretching. The model could classify seven emotions, namely anger, disgust, fear, happiness, sadness, surprise, and neutral, with a classification accuracy of over 96 %. The results thus prove CNN-based models to be effective for addressing the complexity and variation of human emotional expressions in speech.

title: LiteMedNet: A Lightweight Direction–Frequency Enhanced Network for Cortical Bone Segmentation in X-ray Images
authors: Dongsheng Ji, Hao Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Accurate cortical bone segmentation from X-ray images is essential for bone structure analysis and osteoporosis assessment. However, conventional U-Net-based models often involve large parameter sizes and high computational costs when processing high-resolution medical images. To address this issue, this study proposes a lightweight segmentation network, LiteMedNet, which employs MobileNet as the encoder backbone while retaining the skip-connection mechanism of U-Net. To enhance the segmentation of elongated cortical structures, two modules are introduced: a Spatial-Frequency Hybrid Feature Enhancement (SFHFE) module and a Vertical Strip Convolution (VSC) module. The proposed modules improve global structural representation and directional feature extraction. Experiments on a femoral X-ray dataset demonstrate that LiteMedNet achieves competitive segmentation performance with significantly reduced computational complexity. Furthermore, the Cortical Thickness Index (CTI) derived from the segmentation results shows a significant correlation with Bone Mineral Density (BMD) (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r \approx 0.46$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p<0.001$</tex>), indicating its potential for automated bone health assessment.

title: Transformer Core Looseness Fault Diagnosis Based on Acoustic Spectrograms
authors: Chen Li, Fengyi Liu, Yanan Wang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: With the ongoing expansion of power grids, monitoring the operational status and diagnosing faults in distribution transformers have become crucial for maintaining the safety and stability of power systems. Conventional approaches relying on electrical measurements often suffer from delayed response and limited robustness to noise. This paper introduces a novel non-intrusive fault detection method based on acoustic signals. The proposed technique transforms raw audio clips into Mel-spectrograms to generate discriminative acoustic fingerprints, which are subsequently classified using a deep convolutional neural network (CNN). Experimental evaluations demonstrate that our method effectively detects core looseness faults, achieving a validation accuracy of 98.26% and an independent test accuracy of 91.7%.

title: Research on Optimization Algorithm of Back Projection Reconstruction Based on Material Specificity
authors: Ying Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This study aims to optimize the CT back projection reconstruction algorithm for industrial components with different attenuation characteristics. Traditional general reconstruction algorithms such as filtered back projection have difficulty distinguishing the physical responses of different materials, leading to problems such as beam hardening and scattering artifacts, which affect the quality of reconstruction. To address this, this paper proposes a back projection reconstruction algorithm based on material specificity. By establishing an adaptive multi-material prior model, it precisely compensates and separates the contributions of different materials during the reconstruction process, thereby suppressing artifacts from a physical mechanism. Simulation results show that this algorithm can clearly distinguish the distribution of different materials in a fan-beam scanning system, with clear image edges and complete details, while balancing data acquisition and reconstruction efficiency. This research provides a feasible method for precise and efficient CT inspection of multi-material industrial components and has good engineering application potential.

title: Analysis of Shuffling Beyond Pure Local Differential Privacy
authors: Shun Takagi, Seng Pei Liew
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
